---
title: "Multi-Omics Integration: Maisie Varcoe"
output: html_document
date: "2026-03-10"
---

### Dependencies

```{r}
#if (!require("BiocManager", quietly = TRUE))
 #   install.packages("BiocManager")
#BiocManager::install("MOFA2")

```

```{r}
# Load dependencies
library(readxl)
library(dplyr)
library(ggplot2)
library(ggpubr)
library(patchwork)
library(phyloseq)
library(vegan)
library(tibble)
library(MOFA2)
library(ggrepel)
library(pheatmap)
library(lme4)
library(lmerTest)
library(compositions)
library(MultiAssayExperiment)
library(Seurat)
library(reshape2)
```

### Load Data

```{r}
# ------------ Microbiome Data -------------------
# Microbiome data: species-level abundance table (already filtered, clean row names)
microbiome <- read.csv(
  "~/Desktop/iawards/metaphlan_output/species_abundance_table_phyloseq/species_table.csv",
  row.names = 1
)
# Load phyloseq object
load("~/Desktop/iawards/metaphlan_output/species_abundance_table_phyloseq/physeq.RData")

# ------------ Metabolomics Data -----------------
# NMR peak area metadata, includes NMR sample IDs and peak area values for metabolites
load("~/Desktop/iawards/NMR metabolomics/NMR_peakarea_metadata.RData")
NMR_meta <- NMR_peakarea_metadata

#--------- Pathway Data ---------------------
pathways <- read.csv("~/Desktop/Research_Project/Scripts/Integration/Data/pathabundance_clean.csv")
# If it's already loaded as an object, check its structure

# ------------ Linking Files -------------------
# Sequencing library - links P22757 IDs to sample numbers
lib <- read_xlsx("~/Desktop/iawards/shotgun_jose_analysis/lib.xlsx")

# Shotgun sample metadata - eg_id, site, lesional, local EASI
sample_meta <- read_xlsx("~/Desktop/iawards/shotgun_jose_analysis/iawards_shotgun_sample_metadata.xlsx")

# Shotgun patient metadata - age, sex, EASI, treatments
shotgun_patient <- read_xlsx("~/Desktop/iawards/shotgun_jose_analysis/iawards_shotgun_patient_metadata.xlsx")

# NMR-shotgun linking file
nmr_shotgun <- read.csv("~/Desktop/iawards/shotgun_jose_analysis/Abundance_tables/NMR_shotgun_sample_names.csv")

# Sample metadata with eg_id linking factor
load("~/Desktop/iawards/metadata/sample_metadata_201106.RData")
link <- sample_metadata_201106
```

# Tier 1: Microbiome & Metabolomics

## Step 1: Data Preparation

Using cleaned data files, extract S. aureus abundance from metaphlan.

Build master linking table, connecting microbiome (SA abundance) to metabolomics via eg_id.

### Data Cleaning

Row 13 (P22757_129, GYECZ1193) was incorrectly assigned GYECZ1197.BACK.LES as its NMR ID. This means:

-   **The duplicate**: BACK.LES appears twice- once correctly (row 22, the real GYECZ1197 sample) and once incorrectly (row 13, the mislinked GYECZ1193 sample)

-   **The "missing" value**: there's no actual NMR data for GYECZ1193 BACK, so the row shouldn't exist at all

Dropping row 13 solves both in one step 

```{r}
# Remove GYECZ1193 BACK - no NMR data available, incorrectly linked to GYECZ1197
nmr_shotgun <- nmr_shotgun[nmr_shotgun$sample_number != "29", ]

# Remove _S1, _S2, etc. suffixes from pathway IDs
pathways$sample_short <- gsub("_S[0-9]+$", "", pathways$sample)
```

### 1.1. Extract SA Abundance from Microbiome Data

```{r}
#------ Check for rows that have S. aureus ---------
sa_rows <- grep("aureus", rownames(microbiome), 
                value=TRUE, ignore.case=TRUE)
print(sa_rows)

# ------ Extract Species-Level SA Row-------
# (not strain level)
sa_abundance <- microbiome["s__Staphylococcus_aureus", ]

# Transpose to dataframe
sa_df <- data.frame(
  sample_id = colnames(microbiome),
  sa_abundance = as.numeric(sa_abundance)
)

# Trim sample IDs to match lib format (remove _S1_L001d_metagenome suffix)
sa_df$sample_short <- gsub("_S[0-9]+_L001d_metagenome", "", 
                            sa_df$sample_id)

print(sa_df)
```

### 1.2. Merge SA Data with Sequencing Data

Joining sa_df to lib because sa_df has IDs (e.g. PXXXXX_101 with abundance X), but doesn't have patient information. So after merging with lib, it will know that patient 1 has abundance X (intermediate ID for linking to later files).

```{r}
# Join to lib to get sample_number
sa_linked <- sa_df %>%
  left_join(
    lib %>% dplyr::select(Sample, `Submitted Name`),
    by = c("sample_short" = "Sample")
  ) %>%
  dplyr::rename(sample_number = `Submitted Name`)

# Check - do all 20 rows have a sample_number?
print(sa_linked %>% select(sample_short, sample_number, sa_abundance))
# Check for NAs
sum(is.na(sa_linked$sample_number)) # should be 0
```

### 1.3. Join sa_linked with Sample Metadata

So SA_linked now has PXXXX IDs, sample numbers and SA abundance.

Link this to sample metadata (has sample number, eg_id, sample site, lesional status, local EASI score, S. aureus growth viapath)

Need to link these so that the object with SA abundance now has info on who the patient is and info about them, specifically important that the object now has eg_id which is the linking factor between all data modalities.

```{r}
# Convert sample_meta sample_number to character to match sa_linked
sample_meta$sample_number <- as.character(sample_meta$sample_number)

# run the join
sa_linked2 <- sa_linked %>%
  left_join(
    sample_meta %>% 
      select(sample_number, eg_id, sample_site,
             lesional, local_EASI_intensity,
             s_aureus_culture_growth_viapath),
    by = "sample_number"
  )

# Check - do all 20 rows have an eg_id?
print(sa_linked2 %>% select(sample_number, eg_id, 
                              sample_site, lesional, 
                              sa_abundance))
sum(is.na(sa_linked2$eg_id)) # should be 0
```

### 1.4. Join sa_linked2 to Patient Metadata

So the sa_linked2 now has SA abundance, eg_id, sample site and lesional status.

The shotgun_patient has patient-level clinical metadata (age, sex, EASI, weeks since last systemic, weeks since last TCS or TCI, days since any topical, TEWL mean, pH mean).

Need to link these for checking for confounders later on (e.g. age/treatment) and for downstream analyses (EASI = lesionality outcome variable).

```{r}
# Join to get patient-level clinical variables
sa_linked3 <- sa_linked2 %>%
  left_join(
    shotgun_patient %>%
      select(eg_id, age, sex, EASI,
             weeks_since_last_systemic,
             weeks_since_last_TCS_or_TCI,
             days_since_any_topicals,
             TEWL_mean, pH_mean),
    by = "eg_id"
  )

# Check
print(sa_linked3 %>% select(eg_id, sample_site, lesional,
                              sa_abundance, age, sex, EASI))
sum(is.na(sa_linked3$eg_id))
```

### 1.5. Add NMR (Metabolite) to Microbiome Data

Link each microbiome sample to its corresponding NMR sample to create single table where each row has (SA abundance, patient ID, body, site, lesional status, clinical variables, and matching metabolomics ID).

This creates the master linking table which is the foundation for all downstream analysis in Tier 1.

```{r}
# Fix class mismatches for joins
sample_meta$sample_number <- as.character(sample_meta$sample_number)
nmr_shotgun$sample_number <- as.character(nmr_shotgun$sample_number)

#------------ Build master linking table-----------------
master_table <- sa_df %>%
  # Link P22757 IDs to sample numbers via sequencing library
  left_join(lib %>% 
              dplyr::select(Sample, `Submitted Name`),
            by = c("sample_short" = "Sample")) %>%
  dplyr::rename(sample_number = `Submitted Name`) %>%
  # Add sample-level metadata (eg_id, site, lesional status)
  left_join(sample_meta %>%
              dplyr::select(sample_number, eg_id, sample_site,
                     lesional, local_EASI_intensity,
                     s_aureus_culture_growth_viapath),
            by = "sample_number") %>%
  # Add patient-level clinical variables
  left_join(shotgun_patient %>%
              dplyr::select(eg_id, age, sex, EASI,
                     weeks_since_last_systemic,
                     weeks_since_last_TCS_or_TCI,
                     days_since_any_topicals,
                     TEWL_mean, pH_mean),
            by = "eg_id") %>%
  # Add NMR metabolomics sample IDs
  left_join(nmr_shotgun %>%
              dplyr::select(sample_number, NMR_sample_id),
            by = "sample_number") %>%
  # Add pathways 
  left_join(pathways %>% select(-sample),
            by = c("sample_short" = "sample_short"))

# Validation of master table 
master_table
dim(master_table)                          
length(unique(master_table$eg_id))         
table(master_table$lesional)               
summary(master_table$sa_abundance)         
colSums(is.na(master_table))   

```

## Step 2: Confounder Analysis

Confounder analysis and multi-omic alignment. Determine if clinical or environmental variables are driving observed changes in metabolomics and pathway data, rather than S. aureus abundance.

Plotting S. aureus abundance against age, EASI score, treatment washout, TEWL and pH across 9 patients. Seeing if any variables correlate with SA/pathway abundance.

Spearman correlations. Flag anything that should be carried as a covariate downstream.

### 2.1. Data Aggregation and Cleaning

Collapse master table to patient-level summary to ensure that visual comparisons of the confounders represent unique individuals rather than multiple samples from the same person. Also clean, normalise and standardise the clinical variables, converting 'none' strings to NA and enforcing numeric format. This enables correct statistical analysis.

```{r}
# Collapses master table to one row per patient for visualisation only 
patient_level <- master_table %>%
  group_by(eg_id) %>%
  summarise(
    mean_sa = mean(sa_abundance, na.rm = TRUE),
    age = dplyr::first(age),
    sex = dplyr::first(sex),
    EASI = dplyr::first(EASI),
    weeks_since_last_systemic = dplyr::first(weeks_since_last_systemic),
    weeks_since_last_TCS_or_TCI = dplyr::first(weeks_since_last_TCS_or_TCI),
    days_since_any_topicals = dplyr::first(days_since_any_topicals),
    TEWL_mean = dplyr::first(TEWL_mean),
    pH_mean = dplyr::first(pH_mean)
  )

print(patient_level)

# -------- Standardise and Clean Clinical Variables -------------
# Clean clinical variables: convert 'none' strings to NA and ensure numeric format

patient_level <- patient_level %>%
  mutate(
    # Clean systemic treatment
    had_systemic = if_else(weeks_since_last_systemic == "never", "never", "yes"),
    # Clean numeric columns 
    across(
      c(TEWL_mean, pH_mean, days_since_any_topicals),
      ~ as.numeric(na_if(as.character(.x), "none"))
    )
  )

print(patient_level %>% select(eg_id, mean_sa, had_systemic))
```

### 2.2. SA Confounder Plots

Visualising continuous variables using ggplot2 scatter plots to see relationship between potential confounders and S. aureus abundance. Plotting linear regression lines (geom_smooth) and Spearman correlation coefficients (stat_cor) to provide visual and statistical check for correlations.

Visualising categorical confounders using boxplots, comparing S. aureus levels between patients who have and have not received systemic treatment.

```{r}
# Plot theme
my_theme <-
  theme(
    plot.title = element_text(size = 16, face = "bold"),
    axis.title = element_text(size = 16),
    axis.text = element_text(size = 16),
    axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1)
  )
# ---- Continuous confounders ----
# Age vs SA
age_SA <- ggplot(patient_level, aes(x = age, y = mean_sa)) +
  geom_point(size = 3) +
  geom_smooth(method = "lm", se = TRUE, colour = "steelblue") +
  stat_cor(method = "spearman", label.x.npc = "left", size=6) +
  labs(title = "Age vs SA Abundance",
       x = "Age", y = "Mean SA Abundance (%)") +
  my_theme

# EASI vs SA
EASI_SA <- ggplot(patient_level, aes(x = EASI, y = mean_sa)) +
  geom_point(size = 3) +
  geom_smooth(method = "lm", se = TRUE, colour = "steelblue") +
  stat_cor(method = "spearman", label.x.npc = "left", size=6) +
  labs(title = "EASI vs SA Abundance",
       x = "EASI Score", y = "Mean SA Abundance (%)") +
  my_theme

# weeks since TCS vs SA
TCA_SA <- ggplot(patient_level, aes(x = weeks_since_last_TCS_or_TCI, y = mean_sa)) +
  geom_point(size = 3) +
  geom_smooth(method = "lm", se = TRUE, colour = "steelblue") +
  stat_cor(method = "spearman", label.x.npc = "left", size=6) +
  labs(title = "Weeks Since TCS/TCI vs SA Abundance",
       x = "Weeks Since Last TCS/TCI", y = "Mean SA Abundance (%)") +
  my_theme

# days since topicals vs SA
top_SA <- ggplot(patient_level, aes(x = days_since_any_topicals, y = mean_sa)) +
  geom_point(size = 3) +
  geom_smooth(method = "lm", se = TRUE, colour = "steelblue") +
  stat_cor(method = "spearman", label.x.npc = "left", size=6) +
  labs(title = "Days Since Any Topicals vs SA Abundance",
       x = "Days Since Any Topicals", y = "Mean SA Abundance (%)") +
  my_theme

# TEWL vs SA
TEWL_SA <- ggplot(patient_level, aes(x = TEWL_mean, y = mean_sa)) +
  geom_point(size = 3) +
  geom_smooth(method = "lm", se = TRUE, colour = "steelblue") +
  stat_cor(method = "spearman", label.x.npc = "left", size=6) +
  labs(title = "TEWL vs SA Abundance",
       x = "TEWL Mean", y = "Mean SA Abundance (%)") +
  my_theme

# pH vs SA
PH_SA <- ggplot(patient_level, aes(x = pH_mean, y = mean_sa)) +
  geom_point(size = 3) +
  geom_smooth(method = "lm", se = TRUE, colour = "steelblue") +
  stat_cor(method = "spearman", label.x.npc = "left", size=6) +
  labs(title = "pH vs SA Abundance",
       x = "pH Mean", y = "Mean SA Abundance (%)") +
  my_theme

# ---- Categorical confounder ----
# Systemic treatment vs SA
sys_SA <- ggplot(patient_level, aes(x = had_systemic, y = mean_sa)) +
  geom_boxplot(outlier.shape = NA) +
  geom_jitter(width = 0.1, size = 3) +
  labs(title = "Systemic Treatment vs SA Abundance",
       x = "Had Systemic Treatment", 
       y = "Mean SA Abundance (%)") +
  my_theme

# ---- Combine all plots ----
(age_SA + EASI_SA + TCA_SA) / (top_SA + TEWL_SA + PH_SA) / (sys_SA + plot_spacer() + plot_spacer())

# Save combined plot
ggsave("~/Desktop/Research_Project/Scripts/Integration/Confounders/SA_confounder_plots.png", 
       plot = (age_SA + EASI_SA + TCA_SA) / (top_SA + TEWL_SA + PH_SA) / (sys_SA + plot_spacer() + plot_spacer()),
       width = 15, 
       height = 12, 
      
       dpi = 300)

```

### 2.3. SA Spearman Correlation

```{r}
# Run Spearman correlations for each continuous variable
# Excluding days since topicals (SD=0 so correlation is impossible)
confounders <- c("age", "EASI", "weeks_since_last_TCS_or_TCI", 
                 "TEWL_mean", "pH_mean")

# Run cor.test for each and extract rho and p-value
spearman_results <- lapply(confounders, function(var) {
  test <- cor.test(patient_level$mean_sa, 
                   patient_level[[var]], 
                   method = "spearman",
                   use = "complete.obs")
  data.frame(
    variable = var,
    rho = round(test$estimate, 3),
    p_value = round(test$p.value, 3)
  )
}) %>% bind_rows
rownames(spearman_results) <- NULL

# Save
write.csv(spearman_results, 
          file = "~/Desktop/Research_Project/Scripts/Integration/Confounders/SA_Confounders.csv",
          row.names = TRUE)


print(spearman_results)
```

### 2.4. Create Matched Objects

The SA-specific confounder checks use all the samples and run a univerate test (one SA variable vs all clinical variables). Whereas the metabolite and pathway confounder checks require matches objects, which are filtered, aligned versions of the datasets, restricted to the samples that have data in all three modalities (used later for MOFA+ too). This ensures everything lines up row by row.

```{r}
# Create matched matrices (18 samples with all three modalities)
# Notes:
# - GYECZ1193 BACK excluded - no NMR data (corrected in nmr_shotgun at load time)
# - GYECZ0776 and GYECZ1111 excluded - no microbiome data
# - Final aligned dataset: 18 samples

# ------- Prepare NMR matrix --------------
load("~/Desktop/iawards/NMR metabolomics/NMR_peakarea_metadata.RData")

# Keep only NMR_sample_id and the 122 metabolite columns
# Metabolite columns are identified by the quotes in their names
metabolite_cols <- grep("'", colnames(NMR_peakarea_metadata), value = TRUE)
NMR_peakarea_metadata <- NMR_peakarea_metadata[, c("NMR_sample_id", metabolite_cols)]

rownames(NMR_peakarea_metadata) <- NMR_peakarea_metadata$NMR_sample_id
NMR_peakarea_metadata$NMR_sample_id <- NULL
colnames(NMR_peakarea_metadata) <- gsub("'", "", colnames(NMR_peakarea_metadata))

# Convert to numeric
nmr_rownames <- rownames(NMR_peakarea_metadata)
NMR_peakarea_metadata <- as.data.frame(apply(NMR_peakarea_metadata, 2, as.numeric))
rownames(NMR_peakarea_metadata) <- nmr_rownames

# ------- Prepare species matrix ----------
species_matched <- t(microbiome)
rownames(species_matched) <- master_table$NMR_sample_id[
  match(rownames(species_matched), master_table$sample_id)]

# ------- Align to shared 18 samples ------
keep_samples <- rownames(species_matched)[
  rownames(species_matched) %in% rownames(NMR_peakarea_metadata)]

species_matched <- species_matched[keep_samples, ]
species_matched <- species_matched[, apply(species_matched, 2, var) != 0]  # remove zero variance
nmr_matched     <- NMR_peakarea_metadata[keep_samples, ]

# ------- Prepare pathway matrix ----------
pathway_cols    <- colnames(pathways)[!colnames(pathways) %in% c("sample", "sample_short")]
pathway_matched <- master_table[!is.na(master_table$NMR_sample_id), pathway_cols]
rownames(pathway_matched) <- master_table$NMR_sample_id[!is.na(master_table$NMR_sample_id)]
pathway_matched <- pathway_matched[keep_samples, ]

# ------- Prepare metadata ----------------
meta_matched <- master_table[!is.na(master_table$NMR_sample_id), ] %>%
  mutate(
    TEWL_mean              = as.numeric(ifelse(TEWL_mean == "none", NA, TEWL_mean)),
    pH_mean                = as.numeric(ifelse(pH_mean == "none", NA, pH_mean)),
    days_since_any_topicals = as.numeric(ifelse(days_since_any_topicals == "none", NA, days_since_any_topicals))
  )
rownames(meta_matched) <- meta_matched$NMR_sample_id
meta_matched <- meta_matched[keep_samples, ]

# ------- Verify alignment ----------------
dim(species_matched)                                        # 18 x 44
dim(nmr_matched)                                            # 18 x 38
dim(pathway_matched)                                        # 18 x 248
all(rownames(species_matched) == rownames(pathway_matched)) # TRUE
all(rownames(species_matched) == rownames(meta_matched))    # TRUE
head(meta_matched)
```

### 2.5. Multivariate Statistical Testing and Dimensionality Reduction

Do any of the clinical variables co-vary with microbial pathway or metabolite levels? Run multivariate testing to test how much variance is explained by each confounder.

Same variables are tested against both datasets.

-   PERMANOVA (via adonis2 with Bray-Curtis dissimilarity, from vegan package) is used for pathway composition because there are 248 pathways, so this multivariate test is needed. (Bray-Curtis dissimilarity is suited to microbial data to quantify differences in species abundances).

-   PERMANOVA (but with Euclidian distance instead of Bray-Curtis) is used for metabolite data.

-   For the scatter plots, variables are plot against PC1/PCo1 of metabolites/pathways respectively

Datasets are reduced to 2D using PCA (metabolites) and PCoA (pathways, to preserve Bray-Curtis distances). Plot these components against confounders to see if main variance in data clusters by any clinical factors.

```{r}
#  Confounder Checks (Multivariate)
# List of potential confounders for individual screening
confounders <- c("age", "EASI", "weeks_since_last_TCS_or_TCI",
                 "TEWL_mean", "pH_mean", "sa_abundance", "lesional")

# ---- Remove empty pathway sample ------------------
pathway_matched_clean <- pathway_matched[rowSums(pathway_matched) > 0, ]
meta_matched_clean    <- meta_matched[rownames(pathway_matched_clean), ]

# Ensure pH is numeric for both dataframes
meta_matched_clean$pH_mean <- as.numeric(meta_matched_clean$pH_mean)
meta_matched$pH_mean       <- as.numeric(meta_matched$pH_mean)

# ---- Pathway PERMANOVA (Screening + Primary Model) ----

# Screening Individual Variables
pathway_screening <- lapply(confounders, function(var) {
  df <- meta_matched_clean[!is.na(meta_matched_clean[[var]]), ]
  pw <- pathway_matched_clean[rownames(df), ]
  
  set.seed(123)
  test <- adonis2(vegdist(pw, method = "bray") ~ df[[var]], permutations = 999)
  data.frame(variable = var, R2 = round(test$R2[1], 3), p_value = round(test$`Pr(>F)`[1], 3))
}) %>% bind_rows()

# PRIMARY MODEL: SA adjusted for pH
# Only use samples where both SA and pH data exist
pathway_complete <- meta_matched_clean[complete.cases(meta_matched_clean[, c("sa_abundance", "pH_mean")]), ]
pw_dist_complete <- vegdist(pathway_matched_clean[rownames(pathway_complete), ], method = "bray")

set.seed(123)

# 'by = margin' assesses the unique contribution of SA after accounting for pH
pathway_primary_model <- adonis2(pw_dist_complete ~ pH_mean + sa_abundance, 
                                 data = pathway_complete, 
                                 permutations = 999, 
                                 by = "margin",
                                 strata = pathway_complete$eg_id)

cat("\n--- Pathway Primary Model (Adjusted for pH) ---\n")
print(pathway_primary_model)

# ----  Metabolite PERMANOVA (Screening + Primary Model) ----

# Screening Individual Variables
metabolite_screening <- lapply(confounders, function(var) {
  df <- meta_matched[!is.na(meta_matched[[var]]), ]
  nm <- nmr_matched[rownames(df), ]
  
  set.seed(123)
  test <- adonis2(dist(scale(nm)) ~ df[[var]], permutations = 999)
  data.frame(variable = var, R2 = round(test$R2[1], 3), p_value = round(test$`Pr(>F)`[1], 3))
}) %>% bind_rows()

# PRIMARY MODEL: SA adjusted for pH
met_complete <- meta_matched[complete.cases(meta_matched[, c("sa_abundance", "pH_mean")]), ]
nm_scaled    <- scale(nmr_matched[rownames(met_complete), ])
met_dist_complete <- dist(nm_scaled, method = "euclidean")

set.seed(123)
metabolite_primary_model <- adonis2(met_dist_complete ~ pH_mean + sa_abundance, 
                                    data = met_complete, 
                                    permutations = 999, 
                                    by = "margin",
                                    strata = met_complete$eg_id)

cat("\n--- Metabolite Primary Model (Adjusted for pH) ---\n")
print(metabolite_primary_model)

# Standardizing Metabolite objects for Section 3c
metab_matched_clean <- nmr_matched    # Use the NMR data as your metabolite base
meta_metab_clean    <- meta_matched   # Use the matched metadata
# ---- 2.5.4 Save Screening Results -----------------------
write.csv(pathway_screening, "~/Desktop/Research_Project/Scripts/Integration/Confounders/Pathway_Confounders.csv")
write.csv(metabolite_screening, "~/Desktop/Research_Project/Scripts/Integration/Confounders/Metabolite_Confounders.csv")


# ---- 2.3.5 Shared plot theme ----------------------------
my_theme <- theme(
  plot.title = element_text(size = 16, face = "bold"),
  axis.title = element_text(size = 16),
  axis.text  = element_text(size = 16),
  axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1)
)

# ---- 2.5.6 Metabolite PCA -------------------------------
# Reduce NMR data into few PCs
metabolite_pca <- prcomp(nmr_matched, scale. = TRUE)
# Calulate how much of total variance in metabolome is captured by first two PCs
var_explained  <- round(summary(metabolite_pca)$importance[2, 1:2] * 100, 1)

# Merge PCA results with clinical metadata into single table for plotting
meta_plot_df <- data.frame(
  PC1          = metabolite_pca$x[, 1],
  PC2          = metabolite_pca$x[, 2],
  age          = meta_matched$age,
  EASI         = meta_matched$EASI,
  TEWL_mean    = meta_matched$TEWL_mean,
  pH_mean      = meta_matched$pH_mean,
  sa_abundance = meta_matched$sa_abundance,
  lesional     = meta_matched$lesional
)

# Scatter plot for numerical factors
# Adds linear regression line (geom_smooth) and statistical test (stat_cor) to look for significant correlations between metabolites profiles and factors
plot_met_continuous <- function(x_var, title) {
  ggplot(meta_plot_df, aes(x = .data[[x_var]], y = PC1)) +
    geom_point(size = 3) +
    geom_smooth(method = "lm", se = TRUE, colour = "steelblue") +
    stat_cor(method = "spearman", label.x.npc = "left", size = 5) +
    labs(title = title, x = x_var,
         y = paste0("PC1 (", var_explained[1], "%)")) +
    theme_classic() + my_theme
}

# Boxplots to compare categorical groups, comparing metabolic profile (PC1) 
plot_met_categorical <- function(x_var, title) {
  ggplot(meta_plot_df, aes(x = .data[[x_var]], y = PC1,
                            colour = .data[[x_var]])) +
    geom_boxplot(outlier.shape = NA, colour = "black") +
    geom_jitter(width = 0.1, size = 3) +
    scale_colour_manual(values = c("LES" = "salmon", "NON_LES" = "#008080")) +
    labs(title = title, x = x_var,
         y = paste0("PC1 (", var_explained[1], "%)")) +
    theme_classic() + my_theme +
    theme(legend.position = "none")
}

# Plot
m1 <- plot_met_continuous("sa_abundance", "SA Abundance vs Metabolite PC1")
m2 <- plot_met_continuous("EASI",         "EASI vs Metabolite PC1")
m3 <- plot_met_continuous("pH_mean",      "pH vs Metabolite PC1")
m4 <- plot_met_continuous("TEWL_mean",    "TEWL vs Metabolite PC1")
m5 <- plot_met_continuous("age",          "Age vs Metabolite PC1")
m6 <- plot_met_categorical("lesional",    "Lesional vs Metabolite PC1")

metabolite_confounder_plot <- (m1 + m2 + m3) / (m4 + m5 + m6) +
  plot_annotation(title = "Metabolite Composition by Confounder (PCA)")

print(metabolite_confounder_plot)
# Save
ggsave("~/Desktop/Research_Project/Scripts/Integration/Confounders/metabolite_confounder_pca.png",
       plot = metabolite_confounder_plot, width = 15, height = 10, dpi = 300)

# ---- 2.5.7 Pathway PCoA ---------------------------------
# PCoA is more suited to microbiome data than PCA, better at handling the high zero counts and focuses on relative composition of pathways between samples

# Distance matrix (vegdist), calulating how 'different' each sample is from every other based on its pathway profile. Using Bray-Curtis distance.
# Dimensionality reduction (cmdscale), takes distance matrix and projects into 2D space, physical distance between points represents similarity
pathway_pcoa <- cmdscale(vegdist(pathway_matched_clean, method = "bray"),
                         k = 2, eig = TRUE)
eig_vals  <- pathway_pcoa$eig
# Variance calculation (pcoa_var), calculates how much of total variation in pathway profiles is explained by the first two axes.
pcoa_var  <- round(eig_vals[1:2] / sum(eig_vals[eig_vals > 0]) * 100, 1)

# Merges PCoA results with clinical metadata into single table for plotting
pathway_plot_df <- data.frame(
  PC1          = pathway_pcoa$points[, 1],
  PC2          = pathway_pcoa$points[, 2],
  age          = meta_matched_clean$age,
  EASI         = meta_matched_clean$EASI,
  TEWL_mean    = meta_matched_clean$TEWL_mean,
  pH_mean      = meta_matched_clean$pH_mean,
  sa_abundance = meta_matched_clean$sa_abundance,
  lesional     = meta_matched_clean$lesional
)

# Scatter plot for numerical factors
# Adds linear regression line (geom_smooth) and statistical test (stat_cor) to look for significant correlations between pathway profiles and factors
plot_pw_continuous <- function(x_var, title, label_y_pos) {
  ggplot(pathway_plot_df, aes(x = .data[[x_var]], y = PC1)) +
    geom_point(size = 3) +
    geom_smooth(method = "lm", se = TRUE, colour = "steelblue") +
    stat_cor(method = "spearman", label.x.npc = "left", label.y = label_y_pos, size = 5) +
    labs(title = title, x = x_var,
         y = paste0("PCo1 (", pcoa_var[1], "%)")) +
    theme_classic() + my_theme
}

# Boxplots to compare categorical groups, comparing pathway profile (PCo1) 
plot_pw_categorical <- function(x_var, title) {
  ggplot(pathway_plot_df, aes(x = .data[[x_var]], y = PC1,
                               colour = .data[[x_var]])) +
    geom_boxplot(outlier.shape = NA, colour = "black") +
    geom_jitter(width = 0.1, size = 3) +
    scale_colour_manual(values = c("LES" = "salmon", "NON_LES" = "#008080")) +
    labs(title = title, x = x_var,
         y = paste0("PCo1 (", pcoa_var[1], "%)")) +
    theme_classic() + my_theme +
    theme(legend.position = "none")
}

# Plot
p1 <- plot_pw_continuous("sa_abundance", "SA Abundance vs Pathway PCo1", label_y_pos = 0.275)
p2 <- plot_pw_continuous("EASI",         "EASI vs Pathway PCo1", label_y_pos = 0.275)
p3 <- plot_pw_continuous("pH_mean",      "pH vs Pathway PCo1", label_y_pos = 0.275)
p4 <- plot_pw_continuous("TEWL_mean",    "TEWL vs Pathway PCo1", label_y_pos = 0.275)
p5 <- plot_pw_continuous("age",          "Age vs Pathway PCo1", label_y_pos = 0.275)
p6 <- plot_pw_categorical("lesional",    "Lesional vs Pathway PCo1")

pathway_confounder_plot <- (p1 + p2 + p3) / (p4 + p5 + p6) +
  plot_annotation(title = "Pathway Composition by Confounder (PCoA)")

print(pathway_confounder_plot)
# Save
ggsave("~/Desktop/Research_Project/Scripts/Integration/Confounders/pathway_confounder_pcoa.png",
       plot = pathway_confounder_plot, width = 15, height = 10, dpi = 300)
ggsave("~/Desktop/Research_Project/Scripts/Integration/Confounders/pathway_ph.png",
       plot = p3, width = 6, height = 5, dpi = 300)
```

## Step 3: Confirm S. Aureus Structures the Microbiome Composition

Phyloseq object with samples and taxa that matches the species table.

The samples are paired

```{r}
SEED <- 123
# --- Output directory ---
results_dir <- "~/Desktop/Research_Project/Scripts/Integration/SA_Effect/Cleaned/"
dir.create(results_dir, recursive = TRUE, showWarnings = FALSE)
 
# --- Samples manually excluded from ALL omics layers ---
# No data
manual_drops <- c("P22757_140_S22_L001d_metagenome")
```

### 3.1. Add Metadata and SA Grouping

The SA distribution is heavily skewed towards high SA - 16 out of 20 samples have SA above 30%. This means a simple median split at 67% gives you 10 high and 10 low, which is balanced, but the "low" group still has quite high SA values (up to 67%).

I will use the median split for the formal PERMANOVA then use continuous SA abundance in the Mantel test which avoids the binary split problem.

```{r}
## METADATA SETUP & SA GROUPING
physeq_meta <- master_table %>%
  select(sample_id, eg_id, sample_site, lesional,
         sa_abundance, EASI, age, sex,
         weeks_since_last_TCS_or_TCI,
         TEWL_mean, pH_mean) %>%
  column_to_rownames("sample_id")
 
sample_data(physeq) <- sample_data(physeq_meta)
 
# Median split for PERMANOVA / beta-dispersion grouping
median_sa <- median(sample_data(physeq)$sa_abundance)
cat("Median SA abundance:", median_sa, "\n")
 
sample_data(physeq)$sa_group <- ifelse(
  sample_data(physeq)$sa_abundance >= median_sa,
  "SA_high", "SA_low"
)
print(table(sample_data(physeq)$sa_group))
 
```

### 3.2. Sample Filtering

```{r}
## SAMPLE FILTERING 
 
otu_raw  <- as.data.frame(t(otu_table(physeq)))   # samples as rows
meta_raw <- data.frame(sample_data(physeq))
meta_raw$pH_mean <- as.numeric(as.character(meta_raw$pH_mean))
 
zero_samples  <- rownames(otu_raw)[rowSums(otu_raw) == 0]
na_ph_samples <- rownames(meta_raw)[is.na(meta_raw$pH_mean)]
 
all_drops <- unique(c(zero_samples, na_ph_samples, manual_drops))
cat("Dropping samples from all omics layers:\n")
print(all_drops)
 
keep_samples <- setdiff(rownames(otu_raw), all_drops)
 
microbiome_data <- otu_raw[keep_samples, ]
microbiome_meta <- meta_raw[keep_samples, ]
 
stopifnot(identical(rownames(microbiome_data), rownames(microbiome_meta)))
cat("Final microbiome N =", nrow(microbiome_meta), "\n")
 
microbiome_dist <- vegdist(microbiome_data, method = "bray")
 
```

### 3.3. Define Functions

```{r}
## FUNCTIONS 
 
# Align pathway/metabolite tables to the (already-filtered) microbiome metadata via "eg_id.SITE.STATUS" bridge IDs, then drop any samples with all-zero rows for that layer 
# --- 3.1 Align an omics layer to the (already-filtered) microbiome sample
#         set via "eg_id.SITE.STATUS" bridge IDs.
#
#         omics_table     : abundance/feature table for this layer
#         omics_meta      : metadata data frame row-aligned 1:1 to
#                           omics_table (must contain eg_id, sample_site,
#                           lesional, pH_mean, sa_abundance)
#         microbiome_meta : filtered microbiome metadata -- defines which
#                           samples survived the global exclusion criteria
#         median_sa       : SA median used for the microbiome sa_group
#                           split, reused here so sa_group is consistent
#                           across all omics layers
#
#         Bridge IDs are built independently from each side's own eg_id /
#         sample_site / lesional columns and then intersected, so this
#         works regardless of what naming convention the omics table's
#         rownames happen to use. ---
align_omics_table <- function(omics_table, omics_meta, microbiome_meta, median_sa) {
 
  stopifnot(identical(rownames(omics_table), rownames(omics_meta)))
 
  build_bridge_id <- function(meta) {
    meta %>%
      mutate(
        pH_mean    = as.numeric(as.character(pH_mean)),
        les_clean  = gsub("NON_LES", "NONLES", lesional),
        site_clean = toupper(sample_site),
        bridge_id  = paste(eg_id, site_clean, les_clean, sep = ".")
      )
  }
 
  omics_meta_b      <- build_bridge_id(omics_meta)
  microbiome_meta_b <- build_bridge_id(microbiome_meta)
 
  shared_ids <- intersect(omics_meta_b$bridge_id, microbiome_meta_b$bridge_id)
 
  if (length(shared_ids) == 0) {
    stop(
      "No overlapping bridge IDs found between this omics layer and the ",
      "microbiome metadata. Check that 'eg_id', 'sample_site' and ",
      "'lesional' are coded consistently across layers."
    )
  }
 
  keep_rows    <- omics_meta_b$bridge_id %in% shared_ids
  aligned_data <- omics_table[keep_rows, , drop = FALSE]
  aligned_meta <- omics_meta_b[keep_rows, ]
 
  # Recompute sa_group from the same median used for the microbiome layer,
  # so the SA high/low split is identical across all three omics layers
  aligned_meta$sa_group <- ifelse(
    aligned_meta$sa_abundance >= median_sa, "SA_high", "SA_low"
  )
 
  # Zero-impute missing feature values, then drop samples with no
  # detected features in this layer
  aligned_data[is.na(aligned_data)] <- 0
  nonzero <- rowSums(aligned_data) > 0
 
  list(data = aligned_data[nonzero, , drop = FALSE],
       meta = aligned_meta[nonzero, ])
}
 
 
# PERMANOVA with a safeguard that the expected terms exist
run_permanova <- function(dist_mat, meta, seed = SEED) {
 
  set.seed(seed)
  result <- adonis2(
    dist_mat ~ pH_mean + sa_group,
    data         = meta,
    permutations = 999,
    by           = "margin",
    strata       = meta$eg_id
  )
 
  required_terms <- c("pH_mean", "sa_group")
  missing_terms  <- setdiff(required_terms, rownames(result))
 
  if (length(missing_terms) > 0) {
    stop(
      "PERMANOVA output is missing expected term(s): ",
      paste(missing_terms, collapse = ", "),
      ". Check factor coding / adonis2() term names before ",
      "indexing results (e.g. result['sa_group', 'R2'])."
    )
  }
 
  result
}
 
# Partial Mantel: SA abundance vs composition, controlling for pH 
run_partial_mantel <- function(dist_mat, meta, seed = SEED) {
  set.seed(seed)
  mantel.partial(
    dist_mat,
    dist(meta$sa_abundance),
    dist(meta$pH_mean),
    method       = "spearman",
    permutations = 999
  )
}
 
# --- Beta-dispersion test ---
run_betadisper <- function(dist_mat, meta, seed = SEED) {
  set.seed(seed)
  bd      <- betadisper(dist_mat, group = meta$sa_group, type = "centroid")
  bd_test <- permutest(bd, permutations = 999, pairwise = TRUE)
  list(bd = bd, test = bd_test)
}
 
# ---  Richness LMM (pathway/metabolite richness; microbiome handled separately since it also includes Shannon) ---
run_richness_lmm <- function(abund_data, meta) {
 
  richness_df <- data.frame(
    richness     = rowSums(abund_data > 0),
    sa_abundance = meta$sa_abundance,
    pH_mean      = meta$pH_mean,
    eg_id        = meta$eg_id,
    lesional     = meta$lesional
  )
 
  model <- lmer(richness ~ sa_abundance + pH_mean + (1 | eg_id), data = richness_df)
 
  list(richness_df = richness_df, model = model, summary = summary(model))
}
 
# --- Full pipeline for one omics layer: PERMANOVA, beta-dispersion, partial Mantel, richness LMM, and a saved text report ---
run_omics_block <- function(dist_mat, abund_data, meta, label, results_dir, seed = SEED) {
 
  stopifnot(identical(rownames(abund_data), rownames(meta)))
 
  permanova <- run_permanova(dist_mat, meta, seed)
  mantel    <- run_partial_mantel(dist_mat, meta, seed)
  bd        <- run_betadisper(dist_mat, meta, seed)
  richness  <- run_richness_lmm(abund_data, meta)
 
  report_path <- file.path(results_dir, paste0(label, "_results.txt"))
  sink(report_path)
  cat(toupper(label), "RESULTS\n")
  cat("N =", nrow(meta), "\n")
  cat("Date:", as.character(Sys.Date()), "\n")
 
  cat("--- 1. PERMANOVA (Bray-Curtis ~ pH_mean + sa_group) ---\n")
  print(permanova)
 
  cat("\n--- 2. Beta-dispersion (sa_group) ---\n")
  print(bd$test)
  cat("\nMean distance to centroid:\n")
  print(tapply(bd$bd$distances, bd$bd$group, mean))
 
  cat("\n--- 3. Partial Mantel (SA abundance vs composition | pH) ---\n")
  print(mantel)
 
  cat("\n--- 4. Richness LMM (richness ~ sa_abundance + pH_mean + (1|eg_id)) ---\n")
  print(richness$summary)
 
  sink()
  cat("Saved report to:", report_path, "\n")
 
  list(
    permanova   = permanova,
    mantel      = mantel,
    betadisper  = bd,
    richness    = richness,
    meta        = meta,
    dist        = dist_mat
  )
}
 
# --- PCoA biplot coloured by SA group (centroids + dispersion) ---
plot_betadisper_biplot <- function(bd_obj, meta, title, bd_p, permanova_r2,
                                   colours = c(SA_high = "#404080",
                                               SA_low  = "#69b3a2")) {
 
  scores_df <- as.data.frame(scores(bd_obj, display = "sites"))
  scores_df$group     <- meta$sa_group[match(rownames(scores_df), rownames(meta))]
  scores_df$sample_id <- rownames(scores_df)
 
  centroids_df <- as.data.frame(bd_obj$centroids)
  centroids_df$group <- rownames(centroids_df)
 
  eig     <- bd_obj$eig
  eig_pos <- eig[eig > 0]
  pct     <- round(eig[1:2] / sum(eig_pos) * 100, 1)
 
  disp_vals  <- tapply(bd_obj$distances, bd_obj$group, mean)
  disp_label <- paste0(names(disp_vals), ": ", round(disp_vals, 3), collapse = "   ")
 
  ggplot() +
    geom_segment(
      data = scores_df,
      aes(x = PCoA1, y = PCoA2,
          xend = centroids_df$PCoA1[match(group, centroids_df$group)],
          yend = centroids_df$PCoA2[match(group, centroids_df$group)],
          colour = group),
      alpha = 0.35, linewidth = 0.4
    ) +
    geom_point(
      data = scores_df,
      aes(x = PCoA1, y = PCoA2, fill = group),
      size = 3, shape = 21, colour = "white", stroke = 0.6
    ) +
    geom_point(
      data = centroids_df,
      aes(x = PCoA1, y = PCoA2, fill = group),
      size = 5, shape = 23, colour = "black", stroke = 0.8
    ) +
    stat_ellipse(
      data = scores_df,
      aes(x = PCoA1, y = PCoA2, colour = group),
      type = "t", level = 0.95, linewidth = 0.7, linetype = "dashed"
    ) +
    scale_colour_manual(values = colours, name = "SA group") +
    scale_fill_manual(values = colours, name = "SA group") +
    labs(
      title = title,
      subtitle = paste0(
        "PERMANOVA R\u00b2 = ", round(permanova_r2, 3),
        "   |   Betadispersion p = ", round(bd_p, 3), "\n",
        "Mean distance to centroid \u2014 ", disp_label
      ),
      x = paste0("PCoA1 (", pct[1], "%)"),
      y = paste0("PCoA2 (", pct[2], "%)")
    ) +
    theme_classic(base_size = 15) + ### CHANGED: Set a baseline size for all plot text (default is 11)
    theme(
      plot.title    = element_text(size = 18, face = "bold"),   ### CHANGED: Bumped up from 14
      plot.subtitle = element_text(size = 18, colour = "black"), ### CHANGED: Bumped up from 10
      legend.position = "right"
    )
}
 
# --- PCoA biplot with pH fitted as an envfit vector ---
plot_ph_envfit <- function(bd_obj, meta, title, permanova_r2, seed = SEED) {
 
  scores_df <- as.data.frame(scores(bd_obj, display = "sites"))
  scores_df$pH       <- as.numeric(meta$pH_mean[match(rownames(scores_df), rownames(meta))])
  scores_df$sa_group <- meta$sa_group[match(rownames(scores_df), rownames(meta))]
 
  eig     <- bd_obj$eig
  eig_pos <- eig[eig > 0]
  pct     <- round(eig[1:2] / sum(eig_pos) * 100, 1)
 
  complete_idx <- !is.na(scores_df$pH)
  env_data     <- data.frame(pH = scores_df$pH[complete_idx])
 
  set.seed(seed)
  ef <- envfit(
    scores_df[complete_idx, c("PCoA1", "PCoA2")],
    env_data,
    permutations = 999,
    na.rm = TRUE
  )
 
  arrow_scale  <- 0.4
  ef_scores    <- as.data.frame(scores(ef, display = "vectors"))
  ef_scores$r2 <- ef$vectors$r
  ef_scores$p  <- ef$vectors$pvals
  ef_scores$label <- paste0("pH  R\u00b2=", round(ef_scores$r2, 2),
                            "  p=", round(ef_scores$p, 3))
 
  arrow_df <- data.frame(
    x = 0, y = 0,
    xend = ef_scores$PCoA1 * arrow_scale,
    yend = ef_scores$PCoA2 * arrow_scale
  )
 
  ggplot(scores_df, aes(x = PCoA1, y = PCoA2)) +
    geom_point(aes(fill = pH, shape = sa_group),
               size = 3.5, colour = "white", stroke = 0.6) +
    scale_fill_gradient2(
      low = "#3288BD", mid = "#FFFFBF", high = "#D53E4F",
      midpoint = median(scores_df$pH, na.rm = TRUE),
      name = "Skin pH", na.value = "grey70"
    ) +
    scale_shape_manual(values = c(SA_high = 21, SA_low = 24), name = "SA group") +
    geom_segment(
      data = arrow_df,
      aes(x = x, y = y, xend = xend, yend = yend),
      arrow = arrow(length = unit(0.25, "cm"), type = "closed"),
      colour = "black", linewidth = 0.8
    ) +
    geom_text(
      data = arrow_df,
      aes(x = xend * 1.15, y = yend * 1.15, label = ef_scores$label), ### CHANGED: Nudged labels slightly out (1.12 to 1.15) so they don't overlap the arrow tip
      size = 4.5, hjust = 0.5 ### CHANGED: Bumped up from 3.2 (Uses mm)
    ) +
    geom_hline(yintercept = 0, colour = "grey80", linewidth = 0.3) +
    geom_vline(xintercept = 0, colour = "grey80", linewidth = 0.3) +
    labs(
      title = title,
      subtitle = paste0(
        "PERMANOVA R\u00b2 for pH = ", round(permanova_r2, 3),
        "   |   envfit R\u00b2 = ", round(ef_scores$r2, 2),
        "   p = ", round(ef_scores$p, 3)
      ),
      x = paste0("PCoA1 (", pct[1], "%)"),
      y = paste0("PCoA2 (", pct[2], "%)")
    ) +
    theme_classic(base_size = 15) + ### CHANGED: Set a baseline size for all plot text
    theme(
      plot.title    = element_text(size = 18, face = "bold"),   ### CHANGED: Bumped up from 14
      plot.subtitle = element_text(size = 12, colour = "grey40"), ### CHANGED: Bumped up from 10
      legend.position = "right"
    ) +
    guides(
      fill  = guide_colorbar(order = 1, barwidth = 1.0, barheight = 5), ### CHANGED: Made colorbar slightly wider (0.8 to 1.0) to match larger text
      shape = guide_legend(order = 2, override.aes = list(fill = "grey50", size = 4))   ### CHANGED: Increased legend point size from 3 to 4
    )
}
```

### 3.4. Microbiome

PERMANOVA confirms that the microbiome composition differs by SA high/low. Adjusting for effect of pH to ensure the change is from SA and not just due to skin acidity.

Partial mantel test uses continuous SA rather than binary split to complement the PERMANOVA result. If both agree = robust. Partial mantel test adjusts for the influence of pH.

```{r}
##  MICROBIOME LAYER

microbiome_results <- run_omics_block(
  dist_mat    = microbiome_dist,
  abund_data  = microbiome_data,
  meta        = microbiome_meta,
  label       = "microbiome",
  results_dir = results_dir
)
 
# --- Alpha diversity (Shannon + Observed), separate from pathway/metabolite "richness" since it uses phyloseq::estimate_richness with two metrics ---
alpha_div <- estimate_richness(physeq, measures = c("Shannon", "Observed"))
alpha_div <- alpha_div[rownames(microbiome_meta), ]
alpha_div$sa_abundance <- microbiome_meta$sa_abundance
alpha_div$pH_mean      <- microbiome_meta$pH_mean
alpha_div$eg_id        <- microbiome_meta$eg_id
alpha_div$sa_group     <- microbiome_meta$sa_group
 
shannon_lmm  <- lmer(Shannon  ~ sa_abundance + pH_mean + (1 | eg_id), data = alpha_div)
observed_lmm <- lmer(Observed ~ sa_abundance + pH_mean + (1 | eg_id), data = alpha_div)
 
shannon_p  <- summary(shannon_lmm)$coefficients["sa_abundance", "Pr(>|t|)"]
observed_p <- summary(observed_lmm)$coefficients["sa_abundance", "Pr(>|t|)"]
 
alpha_report_path <- file.path(results_dir, "microbiome_alpha_diversity_results.txt")
sink(alpha_report_path)
cat("MICROBIOME ALPHA DIVERSITY RESULTS\n")
cat("Model: metric ~ sa_abundance + pH_mean + (1|eg_id)\n")
cat("Date:", as.character(Sys.Date()), "\n")
cat("--- Shannon diversity ---\n")
print(summary(shannon_lmm))
cat("\n--- Observed richness ---\n")
print(summary(observed_lmm))
sink()
 
library(ggplot2)
library(ggpubr) # Assuming you are using stat_cor

# 1. Define a global base font size (e.g., 16 or 18 for larger text)
large_theme <- theme_classic(base_size = 16)

p_shannon <- ggplot(alpha_div, aes(x = sa_abundance, y = Shannon)) +
  geom_point(size = 3, alpha = 0.7) +
  geom_smooth(method = "lm", se = TRUE, colour = "steelblue") +
  # Note: 'size' in stat_cor controls the text size inside the plot (in mm)
  stat_cor(method = "spearman", label.x.npc = "left", size = 6) + 
  labs(title = "Shannon diversity vs SA",
       subtitle = paste("LMM p =", format(shannon_p, digits = 2)),
       x = "SA abundance (%)", y = "Shannon index") +
  large_theme

p_observed <- ggplot(alpha_div, aes(x = sa_abundance, y = Observed)) +
  geom_point(size = 3, alpha = 0.7) +
  geom_smooth(method = "lm", se = TRUE, colour = "steelblue") +
  stat_cor(method = "spearman", label.x.npc = "left", size = 6) + 
  labs(title = "Species richness vs SA",
       subtitle = paste("LMM p =", format(observed_p, digits = 2)),
       x = "SA abundance (%)", y = "Observed species") +
  large_theme

alpha_plot <- p_shannon + p_observed

ggsave(file.path(results_dir, "microbiome_alpha_diversity_plots.png"),
       plot = alpha_plot, width = 12, height = 5, dpi = 300, bg = "white")
```

### 3.5. Microbial Pathways

```{r}
##  PATHWAY LAYER
pathway_aligned <- align_omics_table(
  omics_table     = pathway_matched_clean,
  omics_meta      = meta_matched_clean,
  microbiome_meta = microbiome_meta,
  median_sa       = median_sa
)
pathway_data <- pathway_aligned$data
pathway_meta <- pathway_aligned$meta
pathway_dist <- vegdist(pathway_data, method = "bray")
 
cat("Final pathway N =", nrow(pathway_meta), "\n")
 
pathway_results <- run_omics_block(
  dist_mat    = pathway_dist,
  abund_data  = pathway_data,
  meta        = pathway_meta,
  label       = "pathway",
  results_dir = results_dir
)
 
pathway_p <- pathway_results$richness$summary$coefficients["sa_abundance", "Pr(>|t|)"]
 
p_pathway_rich_sa <- ggplot(pathway_results$richness$richness_df,
                             aes(x = sa_abundance, y = richness)) +
  geom_point(size = 3, alpha = 0.7) +
  geom_smooth(method = "lm", se = TRUE, colour = "steelblue") +
  stat_cor(method = "spearman", label.x = 5,
           label.y = 275) +
  labs(title = "Pathway richness vs SA",
       subtitle = paste("LMM p =", format(pathway_p, digits = 3)),
       x = "SA abundance (%)", y = "Number of pathways") +
  large_theme
 
show(p_pathway_rich_sa)

p_pathway_rich_ph <- ggplot(pathway_results$richness$richness_df,
                             aes(x = pH_mean, y = richness, colour = lesional)) +
  geom_point(size = 3) +
  geom_smooth(method = "lm", se = TRUE, colour = "black", linetype = "dashed") +
  scale_colour_manual(values = c("LES" = "#E41A1C", "NON_LES" = "#377EB8")) +
  labs(title = "Pathway richness vs skin pH", x = "pH", y = "Pathway richness") +
  large_theme
 
pathway_richness_plot <- p_pathway_rich_sa 
ggsave(file.path(results_dir, "pathway_richness.png"),
       plot = pathway_richness_plot, width = 6, height = 5, dpi = 300, bg = "white")
 
```

### 3.6. Metabolites

```{r}
metab_matched_clean <- nmr_matched    # Use the NMR data as metabolite base
meta_metab_clean    <- meta_matched   # Use the matched metadata
## METABOLITE LAYER
metabolite_aligned <- align_omics_table(
  omics_table     = metab_matched_clean,
  omics_meta      = meta_metab_clean,
  microbiome_meta = microbiome_meta,
  median_sa       = median_sa
)
metabolite_data <- metabolite_aligned$data
metabolite_meta <- metabolite_aligned$meta

metabolite_dist <- vegdist(metabolite_data, method = "bray")
 
cat("Final metabolite N =", nrow(metabolite_meta), "\n")
 
metabolite_results <- run_omics_block(
  dist_mat    = metabolite_dist,
  abund_data  = metabolite_data,
  meta        = metabolite_meta,
  label       = "metabolite",
  results_dir = results_dir
)
 
metabolite_p <- metabolite_results$richness$summary$coefficients["sa_abundance", "Pr(>|t|)"]
 
p_metab_rich_sa <- ggplot(metabolite_results$richness$richness_df,
                           aes(x = sa_abundance, y = richness)) +
  geom_point(size = 3, alpha = 0.7, colour = "darkgreen") +
  geom_smooth(method = "lm", se = TRUE, colour = "darkgreen") +
  stat_cor(method = "spearman", label.y.npc = "top") +
  labs(title = "Metabolite richness vs SA",
       subtitle = paste("LMM p =", format(metabolite_p, digits = 3)),
       x = "SA abundance (%)", y = "Number of metabolites") +
  theme_classic()
 
p_metab_rich_ph <- ggplot(metabolite_results$richness$richness_df,
                           aes(x = pH_mean, y = richness, colour = lesional)) +
  geom_point(size = 3) +
  geom_smooth(method = "lm", se = TRUE, colour = "black", linetype = "dashed") +
  scale_colour_manual(values = c("LES" = "#E41A1C", "NON_LES" = "#377EB8")) +
  labs(title = "Metabolite richness vs skin pH", x = "pH", y = "Metabolite richness") +
  theme_classic()
 
metabolite_richness_plot <- p_metab_rich_sa | p_metab_rich_ph
ggsave(file.path(results_dir, "metabolite_richness_combined.png"),
       plot = metabolite_richness_plot, width = 12, height = 5, dpi = 300, bg = "white")
 
 
```

### 3.7. PCoA Biplots

```{r}
## PCoA BIPLOTS (SA group + pH envfit) FOR ALL THREE LAYERS
omics_layers <- list(
  microbiome = microbiome_results,
  pathway    = pathway_results
  #metabolite = metabolite_results
)
 
biplots_sa <- list()
biplots_ph <- list()
 
for (layer in names(omics_layers)) {
 
  res <- omics_layers[[layer]]
 
  sa_r2 <- res$permanova["sa_group", "R2"]
  ph_r2 <- res$permanova["pH_mean", "R2"]
  bd_p  <- res$betadisper$test$tab["Groups", "Pr(>F)"]
 
  biplots_sa[[layer]] <- plot_betadisper_biplot(
    bd_obj       = res$betadisper$bd,
    meta         = res$meta,
    title        = paste0(tools::toTitleCase(layer), " composition by SA group (Bray-Curtis PCoA)"),
    bd_p         = bd_p,
    permanova_r2 = sa_r2
  )
 
  biplots_ph[[layer]] <- plot_ph_envfit(
    bd_obj       = res$betadisper$bd,
    meta         = res$meta,
    title        = paste0(tools::toTitleCase(layer), " composition \u2014 pH gradient (Bray-Curtis PCoA)"),
    permanova_r2 = ph_r2
  )
 
  ggsave(file.path(results_dir, paste0(layer, "_biplot_sa.png")),
         plot = biplots_sa[[layer]], width = 8, height = 6, dpi = 300, bg = "white")
 
  ggsave(file.path(results_dir, paste0(layer, "_biplot_pH.png")),
         plot = biplots_ph[[layer]], width = 8, height = 6, dpi = 300, bg = "white")
 
  combined <- biplots_sa[[layer]] | biplots_ph[[layer]]
  ggsave(file.path(results_dir, paste0(layer, "_SA_pH_combined.png")),
         plot = combined, width = 16, height = 6, dpi = 300, bg = "white")
 
  betadisp_report <- file.path(results_dir, paste0("betadispersion_", layer, ".txt"))
  sink(betadisp_report)
  cat(toupper(layer), "BETADISPERSION\n")
  cat("Distance: Bray-Curtis   |   Group: sa_group\n")
  cat("Permutations: 999\n\n")
  print(res$betadisper$test)
  cat("\nMean distance to centroid per group:\n")
  print(tapply(res$betadisper$bd$distances, res$betadisper$bd$group, mean))
  sink()
}
 
combined_biplots <- biplots_sa$microbiome +
  plot_annotation(
    theme = theme(plot.title = element_text(size =22, face = "bold"))
  )
 
ggsave(file.path(results_dir, "betadispersion_combined.png"),
       plot = combined_biplots, width = 9, height = 7, dpi = 300, bg = "white")
 

```

```{r}
## VARIANCE PARTITIONING SUMMARY

variance_df <- bind_rows(lapply(names(omics_layers), function(layer) {
  perm <- omics_layers[[layer]]$permanova
  data.frame(
    Level     = tools::toTitleCase(layer),
    pH_mean   = perm["pH_mean", "R2"],
    sa_group  = perm["sa_group", "R2"],
    Residuals = perm["Residual", "R2"]
  )
}))
 
variance_long <- melt(variance_df, id.vars = "Level",
                       variable.name = "Source", value.name = "R2")
 
variance_plot <- ggplot(variance_long, aes(x = Level, y = R2, fill = Source)) +
  geom_bar(stat = "identity", colour = "white") +
  theme_minimal() +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c("pH_mean" = "#69b3a2",
                                "sa_group" = "#404080",
                                "Residuals" = "#d3d3d3")) +
  labs(title = "Variance partitioning across multi-omic levels",
       subtitle = "Comparing microbial, functional and metabolomic signal",
       y = "Proportion of variance (R2)", x = "")
 
ggsave(file.path(results_dir, "CombinedPERM_Variances.png"),
       plot = variance_plot, width = 6, height = 5, dpi = 300, bg = "white")

writeLines(capture.output(sessionInfo()),
           con = file.path(results_dir, "session_info.txt"))
 
cat("Analysis complete. All results, plots, and session info\n")
cat("saved to:", results_dir, "\n")

writeLines(capture.output(sessionInfo()),
           con = file.path(results_dir, "session_info.txt"))
 
cat("Analysis complete. All results, plots, and session info\n")
cat("saved to:", results_dir, "\n")
```

# to delete if above works:

# This uses continuous SA rather than binary split to complement the PERMANOVA result. If both agree = robust.

Partial mantel test adjusts for the influence of pH.

```{r}
# Mantel test - continuous SA vs microbiome distance
# 1. Ensure SA and pH are strictly numeric for the distance calculation
sa_values <- as.numeric(meta_final$sa_abundance)
ph_values <- as.numeric(meta_final$pH_mean)

# 2. Re-calculate the three distance matrices
# Community distance (Bray-Curtis)
bc_dist <- vegdist(otu_final, method = "bray")

# S. aureus distance (Euclidean distance of abundance)
sa_dist <- dist(sa_values)

# pH distance (Euclidean distance of pH)
ph_dist <- dist(ph_values)

# 3. Check for NAs one last time (Should return 0)
cat("NAs in BC:", sum(is.na(bc_dist)), 
    "| NAs in SA:", sum(is.na(sa_dist)), 
    "| NAs in pH:", sum(is.na(ph_dist)), "\n")

# 4. Run the Partial Mantel
set.seed(123)
mantel_result <- mantel.partial(bc_dist, sa_dist, ph_dist, 
                                 method = "spearman", 
                                 permutations = 999)
# ------- Save--------------
# Create a dedicated results file
report_path <- "~/Desktop/Research_Project/Scripts/Integration/SA_Effect/Partial_Mantel_Microbiome.txt"

# Open connection
sink(report_path)

cat("========================================================\n")
cat("SECTION 3A: PARTIAL MANTEL TEST RESULTS\n")
cat("Question: Is Microbiome structure linked to SA, controlling for pH?\n")
cat("Date:", Sys.Date(), "\n")
cat("========================================================\n\n")

print(mantel_result)

# Close connection
sink()

cat("Report saved to:", report_path, "\n")

print(mantel_result)
```

### 3a.4. Alpha Diversity (Microbiome)

Alpha diversity = measuring the diversity within a single sample = how many different species are present and how evenly distributed are they. One number per sample summarises its complexity.

Measuring:

1.  **Observed (richness)** - counts how many different species are present in a sample (doesn't measure abundance)
2.  **Shannon diversity** - accounts for both number of species present and how evenly distributed their abundances are.

Will correlate each sample's alpha diversity score with its SA abundance using Spearman's correlation. (Sig correlation if SA high samples have lower diversity than SA low samples).

Adjusts for pH. Uses Linear Mixed Model (lme4 package), to handle covariates.

Shannon and Richness:

```{r}
# =========================================================
# 3a.4. Alpha Diversity (LMM - Adjusted for pH & Patient)
# =========================================================

# 1. Calculate Alpha Diversity
# Note: MetaPhlAn relative abundance data lacks singletons; ignore warnings.
alpha_div <- estimate_richness(physeq, measures = c("Shannon", "Observed"))
# Remove bad sample from Alpha Diversity
alpha_div <- alpha_div[rownames(meta_final), ]

# 2. Synchronize Metadata
# We pull these directly from the 'meta' object created in 3a.2 to ensure row alignment
alpha_div$sa_abundance <- meta_final$sa_abundance
alpha_div$pH_mean      <- as.numeric(meta_final$pH_mean)
alpha_div$eg_id        <- meta_final$eg_id
alpha_div$sa_group     <- meta_final$sa_group

# 3. Linear Mixed-Effects Models (LMM)
# (1|eg_id) handles the 6 pairs and 1 single sample automatically.
# sa_abundance + pH_mean tests the unique effect of SA while holding pH constant.

cat("\n--- LMM: Shannon Diversity vs SA (Adjusted for pH) ---\n")
shannon_lmm <- lmer(Shannon ~ sa_abundance + pH_mean + (1|eg_id), data = alpha_div)
print(summary(shannon_lmm))

cat("\n--- LMM: Observed Richness vs SA (Adjusted for pH) ---\n")
richness_lmm <- lmer(Observed ~ sa_abundance + pH_mean + (1|eg_id), data = alpha_div)
print(summary(richness_lmm))

# 4. Extracting Clean Results for Reporting
# We pull the 'Estimate' and 'Pr(>|t|)' for the sa_abundance row
shannon_stats  <- summary(shannon_lmm)$coefficients["sa_abundance", ]
richness_stats <- summary(richness_lmm)$coefficients["sa_abundance", ]

cat("\n--- Summary Results (Adjusted for pH and Pairing) ---\n")
cat("Shannon vs SA  - Estimate:", round(shannon_stats[1], 3), 
    "p-value:", format(shannon_stats[5], scientific = TRUE, digits = 3), "\n")
cat("Richness vs SA - Estimate:", round(richness_stats[1], 3), 
    "p-value:", format(richness_stats[5], scientific = TRUE, digits = 3), "\n")

# Save
lmm_report_path <- "~/Desktop/Research_Project/Scripts/Integration/SA_Effect/Alpha_Diversity_LMM_Microbiome.txt"
# Open connection
sink(lmm_report_path)

cat("========================================================\n")
cat("SECTION 3A: ALPHA DIVERSITY LMM RESULTS\n")
cat("Question: Does SA affect Diversity/Richness after adjusting for pH?\n")
cat("Model: Metric ~ sa_abundance + pH_mean + (1|eg_id)\n")
cat("Date:", Sys.Date(), "\n")
cat("========================================================\n\n")

cat("--- 1. SHANNON DIVERSITY MODEL ---\n")
print(summary(shannon_lmm))
cat("\n--------------------------------------------------------\n")

cat("\n--- 2. OBSERVED RICHNESS MODEL ---\n")
print(summary(richness_lmm))
cat("\n--------------------------------------------------------\n")

cat("\n--- 3. QUICK SUMMARY TABLE (Fixed Effects) ---\n")
cat("Shannon vs SA  - Estimate:", round(summary(shannon_lmm)$coefficients["sa_abundance", 1], 4), 
    "| p-value:", format(summary(shannon_lmm)$coefficients["sa_abundance", 5], scientific = TRUE), "\n")
cat("Richness vs SA - Estimate:", round(summary(richness_lmm)$coefficients["sa_abundance", 1], 4), 
    "| p-value:", format(summary(richness_lmm)$coefficients["sa_abundance", 5], scientific = TRUE), "\n")

# Close connection
sink()


# 5. Visualization
# We keep Spearman on the plot for a "raw trend" view, 
# but your text should report the LMM p-values from above.

p_shannon <- ggplot(alpha_div, aes(x = sa_abundance, y = Shannon)) +
  geom_point(size = 3, alpha = 0.7) +
  geom_smooth(method = "lm", se = TRUE, colour = "steelblue") +
  stat_cor(method = "spearman", label.x.npc = "left", size = 5) +
  labs(title = "Shannon Diversity vs SA",
       subtitle = paste("LMM p-adj =", format(shannon_stats[5], digits=2)),
       x = "SA Abundance (%)", y = "Shannon Index") +
  theme_classic() + my_theme

p_richness <- ggplot(alpha_div, aes(x = sa_abundance, y = Observed)) +
  geom_point(size = 3, alpha = 0.7) +
  geom_smooth(method = "lm", se = TRUE, colour = "steelblue") +
  stat_cor(method = "spearman", label.x.npc = "left", size = 5) +
  labs(title = "Species Richness vs SA",
       subtitle = paste("LMM p-adj =", format(richness_stats[5], digits=2)),
       x = "SA Abundance (%)", y = "Observed Species") +
  theme_classic() + my_theme

final_plot <- (p_shannon + p_richness) 
# Save to Desktop
ggsave("~/Desktop/Research_Project/Scripts/Integration/SA_Effect/alpha_diversity_plots.png",
       plot = final_plot,
       width = 12, height = 5, dpi = 300)

# Combine plots using patchwork (ensure library(patchwork) is loaded)
p_shannon + p_richness

```

### 3b. SA Effect on Pathways

```{r}
# =========================================================
# 3b. Pathway Data: SA Structures & Integration (N=14-17)
# =========================================================

# ---- 3b.1 Bridge IDs & Data Alignment -------------------

# 1. Use the "Final 17" metadata as the base
meta_pw_final <- meta_final 

# 2. Fix naming conventions to match Pathway Table (e.g., NON_LES -> NONLES)
meta_pw_final$les_clean <- gsub("NON_LES", "NONLES", meta_pw_final$lesional)
meta_pw_final$site_clean <- toupper(meta_pw_final$sample_site)

# 3. Create the bridge ID (Format: eg_id.SITE.STATUS)
meta_pw_final$bridge_id <- paste(meta_pw_final$eg_id, 
                                 meta_pw_final$site_clean, 
                                 meta_pw_final$les_clean, 
                                 sep = ".")

# 4. Pull and align Pathway data
# This uses the bridge IDs to pull data from the raw pathway table
pathway_final <- pathway_matched_clean[meta_pw_final$bridge_id, ]
rownames(pathway_final) <- rownames(meta_pw_final)

# 5. Handle NAs (Convert missing detections to 0)
pathway_clean <- pathway_final
pathway_clean[is.na(pathway_clean)] <- 0

# 6. Filter out "Ghost Samples" (Samples that had no match/zero pathways)
# This prevents the "missing value where TRUE/FALSE needed" error
keep_idx <- rowSums(pathway_clean) > 0
final_pathway_data <- pathway_clean[keep_idx, ]
final_metadata     <- meta_pw_final[keep_idx, ]

# Final Verification
cat("Total Samples with data:", nrow(final_metadata), "\n")
cat("NAs remaining in pathway data:", sum(is.na(final_pathway_data)), "\n")

# ---- 3b.2 Primary Pathway PERMANOVA ---------------------
set.seed(123)
permanova_pathway <- adonis2(
  vegdist(final_pathway_data, method = "bray") ~ pH_mean + sa_group,
  data = final_metadata,
  permutations = 999,
  by = "margin",                
  strata = final_metadata$eg_id 
)
cat("\n--- Pathway PERMANOVA Results ---\n")
print(permanova_pathway)

# ---- 3b.3 Integration: Partial Mantel Test --------------
pw_dist_mat <- vegdist(final_pathway_data, method = "bray")
sa_dist_pw  <- dist(final_metadata$sa_abundance)
ph_dist_pw  <- dist(final_metadata$pH_mean)

set.seed(123)
mantel_pw <- mantel.partial(
  pw_dist_mat, sa_dist_pw, ph_dist_pw, 
  method = "spearman", permutations = 999
)
cat("\n--- Partial Mantel Results ---\n")
print(mantel_pw)

# ---- 3b.4 Pathway Richness (LMM) ------------------------
pathway_richness_df <- data.frame(
  richness     = rowSums(final_pathway_data > 0),
  sa_abundance = final_metadata$sa_abundance,
  pH_mean      = final_metadata$pH_mean,
  eg_id        = final_metadata$eg_id,
  lesional     = final_metadata$lesional
)

library(lme4)
library(lmerTest)
pw_rich_lmm <- lmer(richness ~ sa_abundance + pH_mean + (1|eg_id), 
                    data = pathway_richness_df)
pw_rich_summary <- summary(pw_rich_lmm)
print(pw_rich_summary)

# Extract p-value for plotting
pw_p_val <- pw_rich_summary$coefficients["sa_abundance", "Pr(>|t|)"]

# ---- 3b.5 Save Results & Visualization ------------------

# 1. Save Text Report
sink("~/Desktop/Research_Project/Scripts/Integration/SA_Effect/Pathway_Results.txt")
cat("SECTION 3B: FUNCTIONAL PATHWAY RESULTS\n")
cat("Final N =", nrow(final_metadata), "\n\n")
cat("--- 1. PATHWAY PERMANOVA ---\n")
print(permanova_pathway)
cat("\n--- 2. PATHWAY PARTIAL MANTEL (SA vs Pathways | pH) ---\n")
print(mantel_pw)
cat("\n--- 3. PATHWAY RICHNESS LMM ---\n")
print(pw_rich_summary)
sink()

# 2. Generate Plots
library(ggplot2)
library(ggpubr)
library(patchwork)

p_pw_rich_sa <- ggplot(pathway_richness_df, aes(x = sa_abundance, y = richness)) +
  geom_point(size = 3, alpha = 0.7) +
  geom_smooth(method = "lm", se = TRUE, colour = "steelblue") +
  stat_cor(method = "spearman", label.y.npc = "top") +
  labs(title = "Pathway Richness vs SA",
       subtitle = paste("LMM Adjusted p =", format(pw_p_val, digits = 3)),
       x = "SA Abundance (%)", y = "Number of Pathways") +
  theme_classic()

p_pw_rich_ph <- ggplot(pathway_richness_df, aes(x = pH_mean, y = richness, colour = lesional)) +
  geom_point(size = 3) +
  geom_smooth(method = "lm", se = TRUE, colour = "black", linetype = "dashed") +
  scale_colour_manual(values = c("LES" = "#E41A1C", "NON_LES" = "#377EB8")) +
  labs(title = "Pathway Richness vs Skin pH",
       x = "pH", y = "Pathway Richness") +
  theme_classic()

combined_pw_plot <- p_pw_rich_sa | p_pw_rich_ph
print(combined_pw_plot)

ggsave("~/Desktop/Research_Project/Scripts/Integration/SA_Effect/pathway_richness_combined.png",
       plot = combined_pw_plot, width = 12, height = 5, dpi = 300)
```

### 3c. SA Effect on Metabolites

```{r}
# =========================================================
# 3c. Metabolite Data: SA Structures & Integration
# =========================================================

# ---- 3c.1 Bridge IDs & Data Alignment -------------------

# 1. Align Metabolite Data (assuming object is named 'metab_matched_clean')
meta_metab_final <- meta_final 
meta_metab_final$les_clean <- gsub("NON_LES", "NONLES", meta_metab_final$lesional)
meta_metab_final$site_clean <- toupper(meta_metab_final$sample_site)

# 2. Create the bridge ID (e.g., GYECZ1193.BACK.LES)
meta_metab_final$bridge_id <- paste(meta_metab_final$eg_id, 
                                    meta_metab_final$site_clean, 
                                    meta_metab_final$les_clean, 
                                    sep = ".")

# 3. Filter and Sync
metab_final_data <- metab_matched_clean[meta_metab_final$bridge_id, ]
rownames(metab_final_data) <- rownames(meta_metab_final)
metab_final_data[is.na(metab_final_data)] <- 0

# 4. Filter empty rows (standard for metabolomics to avoid Bray-Curtis errors)
keep_metab <- rowSums(metab_final_data) > 0
metab_clean <- metab_final_data[keep_metab, ]
meta_metab_clean <- meta_metab_final[keep_metab, ]

cat("Metabolite Analysis N:", nrow(meta_metab_clean), "\n")

# ---- 3c.2 Primary Metabolite PERMANOVA ------------------
set.seed(123)
permanova_metab <- adonis2(
  vegdist(metab_clean, method = "bray") ~ pH_mean + sa_group,
  data = meta_metab_clean,
  permutations = 999,
  by = "margin",                
  strata = meta_metab_clean$eg_id 
)

# ---- 3c.3 Integration: Partial Mantel Test --------------
# *** ADDED THIS SECTION TO FIX THE ERROR ***
metab_dist_mat <- vegdist(metab_clean, method = "bray")
sa_dist_metab  <- dist(meta_metab_clean$sa_abundance)
ph_dist_metab  <- dist(meta_metab_clean$pH_mean)

set.seed(123)
mantel_metab <- mantel.partial(
  metab_dist_mat, sa_dist_metab, ph_dist_metab, 
  method = "spearman", permutations = 999
)

# ---- 3c.4 Metabolite Richness (LMM) ------------------------
metab_richness_df <- data.frame(
  richness     = rowSums(metab_clean > 0),
  sa_abundance = meta_metab_clean$sa_abundance,
  pH_mean      = meta_metab_clean$pH_mean,
  eg_id        = meta_metab_clean$eg_id,
  lesional     = meta_metab_clean$lesional
)

library(lme4)
library(lmerTest)
metab_rich_lmm <- lmer(richness ~ sa_abundance + pH_mean + (1|eg_id), 
                       data = metab_richness_df)
metab_rich_summary <- summary(metab_rich_lmm)
metab_p_val <- metab_rich_summary$coefficients["sa_abundance", "Pr(>|t|)"]

# ---- 3c.5 Save Results & Visualization ------------------

# 1. Save Text Report
sink("~/Desktop/Research_Project/Scripts/Integration/SA_Effect/Metabolite_Results.txt")
cat("SECTION 3C: METABOLITE DATA RESULTS\n")
cat("Final N =", nrow(meta_metab_clean), "\n\n")
cat("--- 1. METABOLITE PERMANOVA ---\n")
print(permanova_metab)
cat("\n--- 2. METABOLITE PARTIAL MANTEL (SA vs Metabolites | pH) ---\n")
print(mantel_metab)
cat("\n--- 3. METABOLITE RICHNESS LMM ---\n")
print(metab_rich_summary)
sink()

# 2. Generate Plots
library(ggplot2)
library(ggpubr)
library(patchwork)

p_metab_rich_sa <- ggplot(metab_richness_df, aes(x = sa_abundance, y = richness)) +
  geom_point(size = 3, alpha = 0.7, color = "darkgreen") +
  geom_smooth(method = "lm", se = TRUE, colour = "darkgreen") +
  stat_cor(method = "spearman", label.y.npc = "top") +
  labs(title = "Metabolite Richness vs SA",
       subtitle = paste("LMM Adjusted p =", format(metab_p_val, digits = 3)),
       x = "SA Abundance (%)", y = "Number of Metabolites") +
  theme_classic()

p_metab_rich_ph <- ggplot(metab_richness_df, aes(x = pH_mean, y = richness, colour = lesional)) +
  geom_point(size = 3) +
  geom_smooth(method = "lm", se = TRUE, colour = "black", linetype = "dashed") +
  scale_colour_manual(values = c("LES" = "#E41A1C", "NON_LES" = "#377EB8")) +
  labs(title = "Metabolite Richness vs Skin pH",
       x = "pH", y = "Metabolite Richness") +
  theme_classic()

combined_metab_plot <- p_metab_rich_sa | p_metab_rich_ph
print(combined_metab_plot)

ggsave("~/Desktop/Research_Project/Scripts/Integration/SA_Effect/metabolite_richness_combined.png",
       plot = combined_metab_plot, width = 12, height = 5, dpi = 300)
```

```{r}
# Store R2 for plotting
variance_results$Taxonomy <- data.frame(
  Level = "Taxonomy",
  pH_mean = permanova_microbiome["pH_mean", "R2"],
  sa_group = permanova_microbiome["sa_group", "R2"],
  Residuals = permanova_microbiome["Residual", "R2"]
)
variance_results$Pathways <- data.frame(
  Level = "Pathways",
  pH_mean = permanova_pathway["pH_mean", "R2"],
  sa_group = permanova_pathway["sa_group", "R2"],
  Residuals = permanova_pathway["Residual", "R2"]
)
variance_results$Metabolites <- data.frame(
  Level = "Metabolites",
  pH_mean = permanova_metab["pH_mean", "R2"],
  sa_group = permanova_metab["sa_group", "R2"],
  Residuals = permanova_metab["Residual", "R2"]
)

# Combine all results into one table
final_variance_df <- do.call(rbind, variance_results)

# Transform for ggplot (wide to long)
library(reshape2)
plot_df <- melt(final_variance_df, id.vars = "Level", 
                variable.name = "Source", value.name = "R2")

# Final Plot
library(ggplot2)
PERM_variance <- ggplot(plot_df, aes(x = Level, y = R2, fill = Source)) +
  geom_bar(stat = "identity", color = "white") +
  theme_minimal() +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c("pH_mean" = "#69b3a2", 
                               "sa_group" = "#404080", 
                               "Residuals" = "#d3d3d3")) +
  labs(title = "Variance Partitioning Across Multi-Omic Levels",
       subtitle = "Comparing Microbial, Functional and Metabolomic Signal",
       y = "Proportion of Variance (R2)",
       x = "")
ggsave("~/Desktop/Research_Project/Scripts/Integration/SA_Effect/CombinedPERM_Variances.png",
       plot = PERM_variance,
       width = 6, height = 5, dpi = 300, bg="white")

```

### 3d. Beta Dispersion Tests

Beta Dispersion tests to check the robustness of the PERMANOVA results - are the centroids significantly different?

```{r}
## ----------------------------------------------------------------------------
# =========================================================
# BETA DISPERSION TESTS + ORDINATION BIPLOTS
# Runs after each PERMANOVA to test whether group differences
# reflect centroid separation (real composition difference)
# vs dispersion difference (one group is more variable).
# Plotted as PCoA biplot coloured by SA high vs low.
#
# Sections:
#   A. Microbiome  - uses bray_dist_final + meta_final
#   B. Pathways    - uses pw_dist_mat   + final_metadata
#   C. Metabolites - uses metab_dist_mat + meta_metab_clean
# =========================================================


## ----------------------------------------------------------------------------
# ---- Helper: make biplot from betadisper object ----
# Reused for all three views to keep code DRY.
# Returns a ggplot object.

plot_betadisper_biplot <- function(bd_obj, meta_df, group_col,
                                   title, bd_p, permanova_r2,
                                   colours = c(SA_high = "#404080",
                                               SA_low  = "#69b3a2")) {
  
  # Extract PCoA coordinates from betadisper
  scores_df <- as.data.frame(scores(bd_obj, display = "sites"))
  scores_df$group     <- meta_df[[group_col]][
    match(rownames(scores_df), rownames(meta_df))]
  scores_df$sample_id <- rownames(scores_df)
  
  # Centroid coordinates
  centroids_df <- as.data.frame(bd_obj$centroids)
  centroids_df$group <- rownames(centroids_df)
  
  # Variance explained by each PCoA axis
  eig      <- bd_obj$eig
  eig_pos  <- eig[eig > 0]
  pct      <- round(eig[1:2] / sum(eig_pos) * 100, 1)
  
  # Dispersion (average distance to centroid) per group
  disp_vals <- tapply(bd_obj$distances, bd_obj$group, mean)
  disp_label <- paste0(
    names(disp_vals), ": ",
    round(disp_vals, 3), collapse = "   "
  )
  
  ggplot() +
    # Spider lines: sample to its centroid
    geom_segment(
      data = scores_df,
      aes(x    = PCoA1, y = PCoA2,
          xend = centroids_df$PCoA1[match(group, centroids_df$group)],
          yend = centroids_df$PCoA2[match(group, centroids_df$group)],
          colour = group),
      alpha = 0.35, linewidth = 0.4
    ) +
    # Sample points
    geom_point(
      data = scores_df,
      aes(x = PCoA1, y = PCoA2, colour = group, fill = group),
      size = 3, shape = 21, colour = "white", stroke = 0.6
    ) +
    # Centroid points (larger, filled)
    geom_point(
      data = centroids_df,
      aes(x = PCoA1, y = PCoA2, fill = group),
      size = 5, shape = 23, colour = "black", stroke = 0.8
    ) +
    # 95% ellipse around each group
    stat_ellipse(
      data = scores_df,
      aes(x = PCoA1, y = PCoA2, colour = group),
      type = "t", level = 0.95, linewidth = 0.7, linetype = "dashed"
    ) +
    scale_colour_manual(values = colours, name = "SA group") +
    scale_fill_manual(values   = colours, name = "SA group") +
    labs(
      title    = title,
      subtitle = paste0(
        "PERMANOVA R\u00b2 = ", round(permanova_r2, 3),
        "   |   Betadispersion p = ", round(bd_p, 3), "\n",
        "Mean distance to centroid \u2014 ", disp_label
      ),
      x = paste0("PCoA1 (", pct[1], "%)"),
      y = paste0("PCoA2 (", pct[2], "%)")
    ) +
    theme_classic() +
    theme(
      plot.title    = element_text(size = 14, face = "bold"),
      plot.subtitle = element_text(size = 10, colour = "grey40"),
      legend.position = "right"
    )
}


## ----------------------------------------------------------------------------
# =========================================================
# SECTION A: MICROBIOME BETADISPERSION
# Distance matrix: bray_dist_final
# Metadata:        meta_final
# Group:           sa_group  (SA_high / SA_low)
# =========================================================

# --- A1. Betadispersion test ---
# Tests H0: groups have equal dispersion (spread around centroid).
# If p < 0.05: groups differ in variability, not just location.
# Interpret PERMANOVA result cautiously if this is significant.

set.seed(123)
bd_micro <- betadisper(bray_dist_final,
                       group = meta_final$sa_group,
                       type  = "centroid")   # centroid preferred over median for PCoA

bd_micro_test <- permutest(bd_micro,
                           permutations = 999,
                           pairwise     = TRUE)

cat("\n--- Microbiome Betadispersion ---\n")
print(bd_micro_test)

# Mean distance to centroid per group
cat("\nMean distance to centroid:\n")
print(tapply(bd_micro$distances, bd_micro$group, mean))

# Save text results
sink("~/Desktop/Research_Project/Scripts/Integration/SA_Effect/betadispersion_microbiome.txt")
cat("MICROBIOME BETADISPERSION\n")
cat("Distance: Bray-Curtis   |   Group: sa_group\n")
cat("Permutations: 999\n\n")
print(bd_micro_test)
cat("\nMean distance to centroid per group:\n")
print(tapply(bd_micro$distances, bd_micro$group, mean))
sink()

# --- A2. Microbiome biplot ---
# Pull PERMANOVA R2 for sa_group from your existing results
micro_r2 <- permanova_microbiome["sa_group", "R2"]
bd_micro_p <- bd_micro_test$tab["Groups", "Pr(>F)"]

p_micro_biplot <- plot_betadisper_biplot(
  bd_obj       = bd_micro,
  meta_df      = meta_final,
  group_col    = "sa_group",
  title        = "Microbiome composition by SA group (Bray-Curtis PCoA)",
  bd_p         = bd_micro_p,
  permanova_r2 = micro_r2
)

print(p_micro_biplot)

ggsave(
  "~/Desktop/Research_Project/Scripts/Integration/SA_Effect/microbiome_biplot_sa.png",
  plot   = p_micro_biplot,
  width  = 8, height = 6, dpi = 300, bg = "white"
)


## ----------------------------------------------------------------------------
# =========================================================
# SECTION B: PATHWAY BETADISPERSION
# Distance matrix: pw_dist_mat
# Metadata:        final_metadata
# Group:           sa_group
# =========================================================

set.seed(123)
bd_pathway <- betadisper(pw_dist_mat,
                         group = final_metadata$sa_group,
                         type  = "centroid")

bd_pathway_test <- permutest(bd_pathway,
                             permutations = 999,
                             pairwise     = TRUE)

cat("\n--- Pathway Betadispersion ---\n")
print(bd_pathway_test)

cat("\nMean distance to centroid:\n")
print(tapply(bd_pathway$distances, bd_pathway$group, mean))

sink("~/Desktop/Research_Project/Scripts/Integration/SA_Effect/betadispersion_pathways.txt")
cat("PATHWAY BETADISPERSION\n")
cat("Distance: Bray-Curtis   |   Group: sa_group\n")
cat("Permutations: 999\n\n")
print(bd_pathway_test)
cat("\nMean distance to centroid per group:\n")
print(tapply(bd_pathway$distances, bd_pathway$group, mean))
sink()

pathway_r2  <- permanova_pathway["sa_group", "R2"]
bd_pathway_p <- bd_pathway_test$tab["Groups", "Pr(>F)"]

p_pathway_biplot <- plot_betadisper_biplot(
  bd_obj       = bd_pathway,
  meta_df      = final_metadata,
  group_col    = "sa_group",
  title        = "Pathway composition by SA group (Bray-Curtis PCoA)",
  bd_p         = bd_pathway_p,
  permanova_r2 = pathway_r2
)

print(p_pathway_biplot)

ggsave(
  "~/Desktop/Research_Project/Scripts/Integration/SA_Effect/pathway_biplot_sa.png",
  plot   = p_pathway_biplot,
  width  = 8, height = 6, dpi = 300, bg = "white"
)


## ----------------------------------------------------------------------------
# =========================================================
# SECTION C: METABOLITE BETADISPERSION
# Distance matrix: metab_dist_mat
# Metadata:        meta_metab_clean
# Group:           sa_group
# =========================================================

set.seed(123)
bd_metab <- betadisper(metab_dist_mat,
                       group = meta_metab_clean$sa_group,
                       type  = "centroid")

bd_metab_test <- permutest(bd_metab,
                           permutations = 999,
                           pairwise     = TRUE)

cat("\n--- Metabolite Betadispersion ---\n")
print(bd_metab_test)

cat("\nMean distance to centroid:\n")
```
