```{r}
print(tapply(bd_metab$distances, bd_metab$group, mean))

sink("~/Desktop/Research_Project/Scripts/Integration/SA_Effect/betadispersion_metabolites.txt")
cat("METABOLITE BETADISPERSION\n")
cat("Distance: Bray-Curtis   |   Group: sa_group\n")
cat("Permutations: 999\n\n")
print(bd_metab_test)
cat("\nMean distance to centroid per group:\n")
print(tapply(bd_metab$distances, bd_metab$group, mean))
sink()

metab_r2   <- permanova_metab["sa_group", "R2"]
bd_metab_p <- bd_metab_test$tab["Groups", "Pr(>F)"]

p_metab_biplot <- plot_betadisper_biplot(
  bd_obj       = bd_metab,
  meta_df      = meta_metab_clean,
  group_col    = "sa_group",
  title        = "Metabolite composition by SA group (Bray-Curtis PCoA)",
  bd_p         = bd_metab_p,
  permanova_r2 = metab_r2
)

print(p_metab_biplot)

ggsave(
  "~/Desktop/Research_Project/Scripts/Integration/SA_Effect/metabolite_biplot_sa.png",
  plot   = p_metab_biplot,
  width  = 8, height = 6, dpi = 300, bg = "white"
)


## ----------------------------------------------------------------------------
# ---- Combined panel: all three biplots side by side ----

combined_biplots <- p_micro_biplot / p_pathway_biplot / p_metab_biplot +
  plot_annotation(
    title   = "Beta dispersion across omics layers - SA high vs low",
    theme   = theme(plot.title = element_text(size = 15, face = "bold"))
  )

ggsave(
  "~/Desktop/Research_Project/Scripts/Integration/SA_Effect/betadispersion_combined.png",
  plot   = combined_biplots,
  width  = 9, height = 18, dpi = 300, bg = "white"
)

cat("\n========================================================\n")
cat("Betadispersion complete. Results saved to SA_Effect folder.\n")
cat("Interpret alongside PERMANOVA:\n")
cat("  BD p > 0.05 + PERMANOVA p < 0.05 = centroid difference (good)\n")
cat("  BD p < 0.05 + PERMANOVA p < 0.05 = dispersion confounded\n")
cat("========================================================\n")


## ----------------------------------------------------------------------------
# =========================================================
# pH ENVFIT BIPLOTS — all three omics views
#
# pH is continuous so betadisper() is not appropriate.
# Instead we use vegan::envfit() to fit pH as a vector onto
# the PCoA ordination. The arrow shows the direction of
# steepest pH increase; its length scales with R2 (strength
# of association). Points are coloured by pH value.
#
# Sections:
#   A. Microbiome  - PCoA from bray_dist_final + meta_final
#   B. Pathways    - PCoA from pw_dist_mat     + final_metadata
#   C. Metabolites - PCoA from metab_dist_mat  + meta_metab_clean
#
# Run after betadispersion_biplots.R (reuses bd_* objects for
# PCoA coordinates — no need to recompute ordinations).
# =========================================================


## ----------------------------------------------------------------------------
# ---- Helper: pH envfit biplot ----
# bd_obj     : betadisper object (already computed — reused for PCoA coords)
# meta_df    : metadata dataframe aligned to distance matrix
# ph_col     : name of pH column in meta_df (default "pH_mean")
# dist_mat   : original distance matrix (needed for envfit)
# title      : plot title string
# permanova_r2 : R2 for pH from your existing PERMANOVA result

plot_ph_envfit <- function(bd_obj, meta_df, dist_mat,
                           title, permanova_r2,
                           ph_col = "pH_mean") {

  # --- PCoA coordinates from betadisper object ---
  scores_df        <- as.data.frame(scores(bd_obj, display = "sites"))
  scores_df$pH     <- as.numeric(meta_df[[ph_col]][
    match(rownames(scores_df), rownames(meta_df))])
  scores_df$sa_group <- meta_df$sa_group[
    match(rownames(scores_df), rownames(meta_df))]

  # --- Variance explained ---
  eig     <- bd_obj$eig
  eig_pos <- eig[eig > 0]
  pct     <- round(eig[1:2] / sum(eig_pos) * 100, 1)

  # --- envfit: fit pH vector onto the PCoA space ---
  # Uses only complete pH observations
  complete_idx <- !is.na(scores_df$pH)
  env_data     <- data.frame(pH = scores_df$pH[complete_idx])

  set.seed(123)
  ef <- envfit(
    scores_df[complete_idx, c("PCoA1", "PCoA2")],
    env_data,
    permutations = 999,
    na.rm        = TRUE
  )

  # Extract arrow endpoint (scaled by R2 for visual weight)
  arrow_scale  <- 0.4   # adjust if arrow too long/short for your data range
  ef_scores    <- as.data.frame(scores(ef, display = "vectors"))
  ef_scores$r2 <- ef$vectors$r
  ef_scores$p  <- ef$vectors$pvals
  ef_scores$label <- paste0("pH  R\u00b2=", round(ef_scores$r2, 2),
                             "  p=", round(ef_scores$p, 3))

  arrow_df <- data.frame(
    x    = 0,
    y    = 0,
    xend = ef_scores$PCoA1 * arrow_scale,
    yend = ef_scores$PCoA2 * arrow_scale
  )

  # --- Plot ---
  ggplot(scores_df, aes(x = PCoA1, y = PCoA2)) +
    # Points coloured by pH — gradient low (blue) to high (red)
    geom_point(aes(fill = pH, shape = sa_group),
               size = 3.5, colour = "white", stroke = 0.6) +
    scale_fill_gradient2(
      low      = "#3288BD",
      mid      = "#FFFFBF",
      high     = "#D53E4F",
      midpoint = median(scores_df$pH, na.rm = TRUE),
      name     = "Skin pH",
      na.value = "grey70"
    ) +
    scale_shape_manual(
      values = c(SA_high = 21, SA_low = 24),
      name   = "SA group"
    ) +
    # envfit arrow
    geom_segment(
      data = arrow_df,
      aes(x = x, y = y, xend = xend, yend = yend),
      arrow     = arrow(length = unit(0.25, "cm"), type = "closed"),
      colour    = "black", linewidth = 0.8
    ) +
    # Arrow label
    geom_text(
      data = arrow_df,
      aes(x = xend * 1.12, y = yend * 1.12,
          label = ef_scores$label),
      size = 3.2, hjust = 0.5
    ) +
    # Zero crosshairs (light reference lines)
    geom_hline(yintercept = 0, colour = "grey80", linewidth = 0.3) +
    geom_vline(xintercept = 0, colour = "grey80", linewidth = 0.3) +
    labs(
      title    = title,
      subtitle = paste0(
        "PERMANOVA R\u00b2 for pH = ", round(permanova_r2, 3),
        "   |   envfit R\u00b2 = ", round(ef_scores$r2, 2),
        "   p = ", round(ef_scores$p, 3)
      ),
      x = paste0("PCoA1 (", pct[1], "%)"),
      y = paste0("PCoA2 (", pct[2], "%)")
    ) +
    theme_classic() +
    theme(
      plot.title    = element_text(size = 14, face = "bold"),
      plot.subtitle = element_text(size = 10, colour = "grey40"),
      legend.position = "right"
    ) +
    guides(
      fill  = guide_colorbar(order = 1, barwidth = 0.8, barheight = 5),
      shape = guide_legend(order = 2,
                           override.aes = list(fill = "grey50", size = 3))
    )
}


## ----------------------------------------------------------------------------
# =========================================================
# SECTION A: MICROBIOME — pH biplot
# Reuses bd_micro (from betadispersion_biplots.R)
# =========================================================

micro_ph_r2 <- permanova_microbiome["pH_mean", "R2"]

p_micro_ph <- plot_ph_envfit(
  bd_obj       = bd_micro,
  meta_df      = meta_final,
  dist_mat     = bray_dist_final,
  title        = "Microbiome composition — pH gradient (Bray-Curtis PCoA)",
  permanova_r2 = micro_ph_r2
)

print(p_micro_ph)

ggsave(
  "~/Desktop/Research_Project/Scripts/Integration/SA_Effect/microbiome_biplot_pH.png",
  plot  = p_micro_ph,
  width = 8, height = 6, dpi = 300, bg = "white"
)


## ----------------------------------------------------------------------------
# =========================================================
# SECTION B: PATHWAYS — pH biplot
# Reuses bd_pathway (from betadispersion_biplots.R)
# =========================================================

pathway_ph_r2 <- permanova_pathway["pH_mean", "R2"]

p_pathway_ph <- plot_ph_envfit(
  bd_obj       = bd_pathway,
  meta_df      = final_metadata,
  dist_mat     = pw_dist_mat,
  title        = "Pathway composition — pH gradient (Bray-Curtis PCoA)",
  permanova_r2 = pathway_ph_r2
)

print(p_pathway_ph)

ggsave(
  "~/Desktop/Research_Project/Scripts/Integration/SA_Effect/pathway_biplot_pH.png",
  plot  = p_pathway_ph,
  width = 8, height = 6, dpi = 300, bg = "white"
)


## ----------------------------------------------------------------------------
# =========================================================
# SECTION C: METABOLITES — pH biplot
# Reuses bd_metab (from betadispersion_biplots.R)
# =========================================================

metab_ph_r2 <- permanova_metab["pH_mean", "R2"]

p_metab_ph <- plot_ph_envfit(
  bd_obj       = bd_metab,
  meta_df      = meta_metab_clean,
  dist_mat     = metab_dist_mat,
  title        = "Metabolite composition — pH gradient (Bray-Curtis PCoA)",
  permanova_r2 = metab_ph_r2
)

print(p_metab_ph)

ggsave(
  "~/Desktop/Research_Project/Scripts/Integration/SA_Effect/metabolite_biplot_pH.png",
  plot  = p_metab_ph,
  width = 8, height = 6, dpi = 300, bg = "white"
)


## ----------------------------------------------------------------------------
# ---- Combined: SA biplot + pH biplot side by side per view ----
# Useful for figures — shows both variables on the same ordination space

combined_micro  <- p_micro_biplot  | p_micro_ph
combined_pw     <- p_pathway_biplot | p_pathway_ph
combined_metab  <- p_metab_biplot  | p_metab_ph

ggsave(
  "~/Desktop/Research_Project/Scripts/Integration/SA_Effect/microbiome_SA_pH_combined.png",
  plot  = combined_micro,
  width = 16, height = 6, dpi = 300, bg = "white"
)

ggsave(
  "~/Desktop/Research_Project/Scripts/Integration/SA_Effect/pathway_SA_pH_combined.png",
  plot  = combined_pw,
  width = 16, height = 6, dpi = 300, bg = "white"
)

ggsave(
  "~/Desktop/Research_Project/Scripts/Integration/SA_Effect/metabolite_SA_pH_combined.png",
  plot  = combined_metab,
  width = 16, height = 6, dpi = 300, bg = "white"
)

cat("\n========================================================\n")
cat("pH envfit biplots complete.\n")
cat("Individual plots saved to SA_Effect folder.\n")
cat("Combined SA+pH panels also saved per omic layer.\n")
cat("\nNote: envfit R2 and PERMANOVA R2 will differ slightly —\n")
cat("envfit fits onto existing PCoA axes; PERMANOVA partitions\n")
cat("total multivariate variance. Both are valid to report.\n")
cat("========================================================\n")
```

## Step 4: MOFA+

MOFA+ finds latent factors between data groups, allowing you to then correlate these factors with SA abundance or lesionality to see if the data types co-vary.

```{r}
library(MOFA2)
library(data.table)

# (Optional) set up reticulate connection with Python
# reticulate::use_python("/Users/ricard/anaconda3/envs/base_new/bin/python", required = T)

###############
## Load data ##
###############

# Multiple formats are allowed for the input data:

## -- Option 1 -- ##
# nested list of matrices, where the first index refers to the view and the second index refers to the group.
# samples are stored in the rows and features are stored in the columns.
# Missing values must be filled with NAs, including samples missing an entire view

# (...)

## -- Option 2 -- ##
# data.frame with columns ["sample","feature","view","group","value"]
# In this case there is no need to have missing values in the data.frame,
# they will be automatically filled in when creating the corresponding matrices

file = "ftp://ftp.ebi.ac.uk/pub/databases/mofa/getting_started/data.txt.gz"
data = fread(file)
colnames(data)
rownames(data)


#######################
# Create MOFA object ##
#######################

MOFAobject <- create_mofa(data)

# Visualise data structure
plot_data_overview(MOFAobject)

####################
## Define options ##
####################

# Data options
# - scale_views: if views have different ranges/variances, it is good practice to scale each view to unit variance (default is FALSE)
# - scale_groups: if groups have different ranges/variances, it is good practice to scale each group to unit variance (default is FALSE)
data_opts <- get_default_data_options(MOFAobject)


# Model options
# - likelihoods: likelihood per view (options are "gaussian","poisson","bernoulli"). By default, they are guessed internally
# - num_factors: number of factors. By default K=10
model_opts <- get_default_model_options(MOFAobject)
model_opts$num_factors <- 10

# Training options
# - maxiter: number of iterations
# - convergence_mode: "fast", "medium", "slow". For exploration, the fast mode is good enough.
# - startELBO: initial iteration to compute the ELBO (the objective function used to assess convergence)
# - freqELBO: frequency of computations of the ELBO (the objective function used to assess convergence)
# - drop_factor_threshold: minimum variance explained criteria to drop factors while training. Default is -1 (no dropping of factors)
# - gpu_mode: use GPU mode? This needs cupy installed and a functional GPU, see https://cupy.chainer.org/
# - verbose: verbose mode?
# - seed: random seed
train_opts <- get_default_training_options(MOFAobject)
train_opts$convergence_mode <- "medium"
train_opts$seed <- 42

# (Optional) Set stochastic inference options
# Only recommended with very large sample size (>1e6) and when having access to GPUs
# - batch_size: float value indicating the batch size (as a fraction of the total data set: 0.10, 0.25 or 0.50)
# - learning_rate: learning rate (we recommend values from 0.25 to 0.75)
# - forgetting_rate: forgetting rate (we recommend values from 0.1 to 0.5)
# - start_stochastic: first iteration to apply stochastic inference (recommended > 5)
# stochastic_opts <- get_default_stochastic_options(MOFAobject)

#########################
## Prepare MOFA object ##
#########################

MOFAobject <- prepare_mofa(MOFAobject,
  data_options = data_opts,
  model_options = model_opts,
  training_options = train_opts
  # stochastic_options = stochastic_opts
)

#####################
## Train the model ##
#####################

outfile <- paste0(getwd(),"/model.hdf5")
MOFAobject <- run_mofa(MOFAobject, outfile, use_basilisk=TRUE)
```

### 4.1. Preprocessing & Feature Selection

```{r}
# ========================================================= # # 1. Alignment and Subsetting (Two-View) # # ========================================================= #  
if (nrow(species_matched) == 44) { species_matched <- t(species_matched) } 
# We only need common samples between the "Big Two" 
common_samples <- intersect(rownames(nmr_matched), rownames(species_matched)) 
nmr_sub     <- nmr_matched[common_samples, ] 
species_sub <- species_matched[common_samples, ]
# --- MODIFIED FILTERING --- # 
# Only remove features that are ZERO in all samples (keeps the rare species)
species_sub <- species_sub[, colSums(species_sub != 0) > 0] 
nmr_sub     <- nmr_sub[, colSums(nmr_sub != 0) > 0] 
cat("\nFeatures for Two-View Run:\n") 
cat("Species:", ncol(species_sub), "| NMR:", ncol(nmr_sub), "\n") 
#========================================================= # # 2. Transformation & Global Scaling # # ========================================================= # 
# Define PQN Normalisation Function 
pqn_normalise <- function(data) {
  # 1. Create a reference sample (median of all samples)
  reference <- apply(data, 2, median, na.rm = TRUE)
  # 2. Calculate quotients (sample / reference) 
  # We use t() because R operates on columns by default
  quotients <- t(t(data) / reference) 
  # 3. Find the median quotient for each sample
  sample_medians <- apply(quotients, 1, median, na.rm = TRUE)
  # 4. Divide original data by those medians
  # If a median is 0 (rare), we replace with 1 to avoid NaN 
  sample_medians[sample_medians == 0] <- 1 
  return(data / sample_medians) 
}

#  # # Microbiome (Unfiltered) 
species_log <- log1p(species_sub) 
sd_sp <- sd(as.matrix(species_log), na.rm = TRUE) 
if(is.na(sd_sp) || sd_sp == 0) sd_sp <- 1
species_final_scaled <- (species_log - mean(as.matrix(species_log))) / sd_sp 
# NMR (Unfiltered) 
nmr_log <- log1p(pqn_normalise(nmr_sub)) 
sd_nmr <- sd(as.matrix(nmr_log), na.rm = TRUE) 
if(is.na(sd_nmr) || sd_nmr == 0) sd_nmr <- 1 
nmr_final_scaled <- (nmr_log - mean(as.matrix(nmr_log))) / sd_nmr 

#  # # --- Step 3: Robust Capping --- 
species_final_scaled[species_final_scaled > 3]  <- 3 
species_final_scaled[species_final_scaled < -3] <- -3 
nmr_final_scaled[nmr_final_scaled > 3]          <- 3 
nmr_final_scaled[nmr_final_scaled < -3]         <- -3 

# # ========================================================= # # 4. Create the 2-View MAE # # ========================================================= #  
mae_two_view <- MultiAssayExperiment(
  experiments = ExperimentList( 
    metabolites = SummarizedExperiment(assays = list(data = t(as.matrix(nmr_final_scaled)))), 
    species     = SummarizedExperiment(assays = list(data = t(as.matrix(species_final_scaled))))    )  ) 

# # Align Metadata 
metadata_for_mae <- master_table %>% 
  filter(!is.na(NMR_sample_id)) %>% 
  distinct(NMR_sample_id, .keep_all = TRUE) %>% 
  column_to_rownames("NMR_sample_id")   
metadata_for_mae <- metadata_for_mae[intersect(rownames(metadata_for_mae), colnames(mae_two_view[[1]])), ] 
colData(mae_two_view) <- DataFrame(metadata_for_mae) 

# ========================================================= # # 5. Final Quality Control Checks # # ========================================================= #  
cat("\nFinal MAE Structure (3-View Stable):\n") 
print(mae_two_view) 
if(any(sapply(experiments(mae_two_view), function(x) any(is.na(assay(x)))))) { 
  stop("NAs detected! Check filtering or Log steps.") 
} else { 
    cat("\nSuccess: Preprocessing complete. Ready for MOFA+.\n") 
  }


```

### 4.2. Train MOFA+ Model

```{r}
# =========================================================
# 4.2. Training Block - The "Atomic" Stable Two-View
# =========================================================
mofa_two <- create_mofa(mae_two_view)

model_opts <- get_default_model_options(mofa_two)
data_opts <- get_default_data_options(mofa_two)
train_opts <- get_default_training_options(mofa_two)

# 1. Manual Scaling Override
data_opts$scale_views <- FALSE

# 2. THE NaN KILLERS
model_opts$num_factors <- 3        # K=3 is safe for N=17
model_opts$spikeslab_weights <- FALSE # CRITICAL: Disable the NaN trigger
model_opts$ard_weights <- FALSE    # Disable automatic feature pruning
model_opts$ard_factors <- FALSE    # Disable factor pruning

# 3. Execution
train_opts$convergence_mode <- "fast"
train_opts$seed <- 64

# Prepare and Run
out_h5 <- "/Users/maisievarcoe/Desktop/mofa_two_view_unfiltered.hdf5"
mofa_two <- prepare_mofa(mofa_two, data_options = data_opts, 
                         model_options = model_opts, training_options = train_opts)
mofa_two@training_options$outfile <- out_h5

cat("\n--- Running Unfiltered Two-View Model (N=17) ---\n")
run_mofa(mofa_two, use_basilisk = TRUE)

# =========================================================
# 6. LOAD, VERIFY, AND SAVE (Two-View Unfiltered)
# =========================================================
if(file.exists(out_h5)) {
  # Load the model from the temporary HDF5 file
  model_two_final <- load_model(out_h5)
  
  # Check mathematical stability (ELBO check)
  elbo_vals <- model_two_final@training_stats$elbo
  elbo_final <- as.numeric(tail(elbo_vals, 1))
  
  if(is.finite(elbo_final)) {
    saveRDS(model_two_final, out_rds)
    cat("\n--- SUCCESS! Two-View Model saved to project folder ---\n")
    
    # 1. Visual check of variance explained
    print(plot_variance_explained(model_two_final))
    
    # 2. Plot the data heatmap to see the factor alignment
    print(plot_data_heatmap(model_two_final, view = "species", factor = 1, features = 10))
    
  } else {
    stop("Model training failed: ELBO resulted in NaN.")
  }
} else {
  stop("HDF5 file was not created.")
}

# =========================================================
# 7. QUICK BIOLOGICAL CHECK (S. aureus Focus)
# =========================================================
if(exists("model_two_final")) {
  # 1. Extract weights for Species
  sw <- get_weights(model_two_final, views="species")[[1]]
  sa_name <- "s__Staphylococcus_aureus"
  
  if(sa_name %in% rownames(sw)) {
    cat("\nS. aureus Loadings across Factors:\n")
    print(round(sw[sa_name, ], 3))
    
    # Identify the factor where S. aureus has the strongest influence
    target_factor <- which.max(abs(sw[sa_name, ]))
    cat("\nS. aureus is the primary driver of Factor:", target_factor, "\n")
    
    # 2. Identify Metabolites linked to this specific Factor
    mw <- get_weights(model_two_final, views="metabolites")[[1]]
    top_metabs <- sort(abs(mw[, target_factor]), decreasing = TRUE)
    top_metabs_names <- names(head(top_metabs, 10))
    
    cat("\nTop 10 Metabolites associated with the S. aureus Factor (Factor", target_factor, "):\n")
    print(round(mw[top_metabs_names, target_factor], 3))
  }
}
```

## DIABLO

```{r}
library(mixOmics)

# 1. Prepare Matrices
X1 <- as.matrix(species_final_scaled)
X2 <- as.matrix(nmr_final_scaled)

# 2. Extract Grouping Factor
Y_vector <- metadata_for_mae[rownames(X1), "lesional"]
Y_status <- factor(Y_vector)
Y_status <- droplevels(Y_status)

# 3. Run the DIABLO model
data_list <- list(microbiome = X1, metabolites = X2)
diablo_res <- block.splsda(X = data_list, Y = Y_status, ncomp = 2, design = "full")

# 4. Visualizations
# 4a. Sample Plot
png("~/Desktop/Research_Project/Scripts/Integration/DIABLO/DIABLO_lesvsnon_clusters.png", 
    width = 8, height = 6, units = "in", res = 300)
plotIndiv(diablo_res, ind.names = FALSE, legend = TRUE, title = "DIABLO: LES vs NON_LES Clusters")
dev.off()

# 4b. Variable Plot
png("~/Desktop/Research_Project/Scripts/Integration/DIABLO/DIABLO_Correlation_Circle.png", 
    width = 2400, height = 2400, res = 300)
plotVar(diablo_res, cutoff = 0.5, pch = c(16, 17), legend = TRUE, title = "DIABLO: Species-Metabolite Correlations")
dev.off()

# 4c. Network Plot
png("~/Desktop/Research_Project/Scripts/Integration/DIABLO/DIABLO_Network_Plot.png", 
    width = 1200, height = 1200, res = 150)
network(diablo_res, blocks = c(1,2), cutoff = 0.6, color.node = c("lightcyan", "mistyrose"))
dev.off()

# 5. Extract Statistics and Loadings
micro_loadings <- diablo_res$loadings$microbiome[,1]
metab_loadings <- diablo_res$loadings$metabolites[,1]

# 6. S. aureus Validation Regression (Alanine Example)
plot_data_ala <- data.frame(
  SA_Abundance = species_sub[, sa_name],
  Alanine = nmr_sub[, "Alanine"],
  Status = metadata_for_mae[rownames(species_sub), "lesional"]
)

fit_ala <- lm(Alanine ~ SA_Abundance, data = plot_data_ala)
sum_ala <- summary(fit_ala)

ala_plot <- ggplot(plot_data_ala, aes(x = SA_Abundance, y = Alanine)) +
  geom_smooth(method = "lm", color = "black", linetype = "dashed", se = TRUE, alpha = 0.1) +
  geom_point(aes(color = Status), size = 4, alpha = 0.8) +
  scale_color_manual(values = c("LES" = "firebrick", "NON_LES" = "dodgerblue")) +
  labs(title = "Validation: S. aureus vs Alanine",
       subtitle = paste0("R² = ", round(sum_ala$r.squared, 3), 
                         " | p-value = ", round(sum_ala$coefficients[2,4], 4)),
       x = "S. aureus Abundance", y = "Alanine Concentration (NMR)") +
  theme_bw()

ggsave("~/Desktop/Research_Project/Scripts/Integration/DIABLO/Regression_SA_vs_Alanine_Colored.png", 
       plot = ala_plot, width = 7, height = 5, dpi = 300)
```

### Sensitivity & Robustness Checks

```{r}
# ==============================================================================
# SENSITIVITY ANALYSIS — MOFA+ WITHOUT OUTLIERS (N=15)
# ==============================================================================

# 1. PREPARE THE "CLEAN" DATA (N=14)
# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
patients_to_drop <- c("GYECZ1187", "GYECZ1197")
keep_logic <- !(mae_data$eg_id %in% patients_to_drop)
mae_clean <- mae_data[, keep_logic, ]

# --- CRITICAL: CLEAN DATA BEFORE INITIALISING MOFA ---
pw_data <- assay(mae_clean[["pathways"]])
bad_features <- which(apply(pw_data, 1, var) == 0)

if(length(bad_features) > 0) {
  cat("Warning: Found", length(bad_features), "pathways with zero variance. Removing...\n")
  mae_clean <- mae_clean[-bad_features, , "pathways"]
}

# 2. INITIALISE BASE OBJECT
# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
mofa_clean_base <- create_mofa(mae_clean)

data_opts_clean <- get_default_data_options(mofa_clean_base)
model_opts_clean <- get_default_model_options(mofa_clean_base)
train_opts_clean <- get_default_training_options(mofa_clean_base)

data_opts_clean$scale_views <- TRUE
model_opts_clean$num_factors <- 3
train_opts_clean$convergence_mode <- "fast"
train_opts_clean$maxiter <- 500

mofa_clean_base <- prepare_mofa(
  object = mofa_clean_base,
  data_options = data_opts_clean,
  model_options = model_opts_clean,
  training_options = train_opts_clean
)

# 3. STABILITY LOOP
# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
n_inits <- 3
best_elbo_clean <- -Inf
best_model_clean <- NULL

for (i in 1:n_inits) {
  cat("\n--- Running Sensitivity Init", i, "of", n_inits, "---\n")
  tmp_obj <- mofa_clean_base
  tmp_obj@training_options$seed <- 123 + i
  
  try({
    trained <- run_mofa(tmp_obj, use_basilisk = TRUE)
    current_elbo <- as.numeric(tail(trained@training_stats$elbo, 1))
    
    if (!is.na(current_elbo) && is.finite(current_elbo) && current_elbo > best_elbo_clean) {
      best_elbo_clean <- current_elbo
      best_model_clean <- trained
      cat(">>> New Best Sensitivity Model! ELBO:", round(best_elbo_clean, 2), "\n")
    }
  })
}
```

### Visualisations

```{r}


# 1. SETUP
# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
mofa_to_plot <- readRDS("~/Desktop/Research_Project/Scripts/Integration/mofa_model_v4_BEST.rds")

# Ensure the output directory exists
output_dir <- "~/Desktop/Research_Project/Scripts/Integration/MOFA+/"
if (!dir.exists(output_dir)) dir.create(output_dir, recursive = TRUE)

# 2. VARIANCE EXPLAINED
# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
p1 <- plot_variance_explained(mofa_to_plot, x="view", y="factor") + 
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1))

p2 <- plot_variance_explained(mofa_to_plot, plot_total = TRUE)

ggsave(paste0(output_dir, "mofa_variance_per_factor_v5.png"), 
       plot = p1, width = 7, height = 5, dpi = 300, bg="white")
ggsave(paste0(output_dir, "mofa_variance_total_v5.png"), 
       plot = p2, width = 6, height = 5, dpi = 300, bg="white")

# 3. FACTOR SCORES HEATMAP
# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
factors <- get_factors(mofa_to_plot)$group1

# Create sample annotation based on row names
sample_anno <- data.frame(
  Status = ifelse(grepl("NONLES", rownames(factors)), "NONLES", "LES"),
  row.names = rownames(factors)
)

anno_colours <- list(Status = c(LES = "#E41A1C", NONLES = "#377EB8"))

png(paste0(output_dir, "mofa_heatmap_v5.png"), 
    width = 1300, height = 800, res = 120, bg = "white")

pheatmap(factors, 
         annotation_row = sample_anno, 
         annotation_colors = anno_colours, 
         cluster_rows = TRUE, 
         cluster_cols = FALSE, 
         scale = "column", 
         color = colorRampPalette(c("#2166AC", "white", "#D6604D"))(100), 
         main = "MOFA+ Factor Scores per Sample", 
         fontsize = 12, 
         cellwidth = 50)
dev.off()

# Plot the top 15 features for Factor 1
p_weights <- plot_top_weights(mofa_to_plot, 
                             view = "pathways", 
                             factor = 1, 
                             nfeatures = 15)
ggsave(paste0(output_dir, "mofa_top_pathways_weights_F1.png"), p_weights)
```

### 4.3. Correlate Factor Scores with S. Aureus Abundance

Correlate MOFA+ factor scores with SA abundance to identify which factor tracks with SA.

**Why do this? -\>** MOFA+ factors are hidden variables capturing a pattern of co-variation across data. When factors is correlated with SA abundance and score consistently high it means samples from SA high patients tend to score high on factor X (vice versa for low SA). Which would mean the biological variation captured by factor X tracks with SA abundance. Finding out this would help you answer the biological story of what changes in the microbiome and metabolome when SA is high.

So the Spearman correlation are between each factor's score with SA and EASI (lesionality). For each factor (1-4), across all 18 samples, asking if that samples score on that factor track with how much SA that sample has.

```{r}
# Add SA abundance to factors dataframe # factors_df <- as.data.frame(factors) # factors_df$sa_abundance <- master_table$sa_abundance[ # match(rownames(factors_df), master_table$NMR_sample_id)
# ]
# factors_df$EASI <- master_table$EASI[
#   match(rownames(factors_df), master_table$NMR_sample_id) # ] # factors_df$lesional <- master_table$lesional[ # match(rownames(factors_df), master_table$NMR_sample_id)
# ]
#
# # -------- F1&2 Correlations with SA & EASI -------------
# # Spearman correlations with SA - Factors 1 and 2 only
# sa_cors <- cor(factors_df[, 1:2],
#                factors_df$sa_abundance, # method = "spearman", # use = "complete.obs") # # print(sa_cors) # # Spearman correlations with EASI - Factors 1 and 2 only # easi_cors <- cor(factors_df[, 1:2], # factors_df$EASI,
#                  method = "spearman",
#                  use = "complete.obs")
#
# print(easi_cors)
# # Run cor.test for each factor against SA and EASI
# # p values for Factors 1 and 2 only
# for(f in paste0("Factor", 1:2)) {
#   cat("\n---", f, "---\n")
#
#   sa_test <- cor.test(factors_df[[f]],
#                       factors_df$sa_abundance, # method = "spearman", exact=FALSE) # cat("SA: rho =", round(sa_test$estimate, 3),
#       "  p =", round(sa_test$p.value, 3), "\n") ## easi_test <- cor.test(factors_df[[f]], # factors_df$EASI,
#                         method = "spearman", exact=FALSE)
#   cat("EASI: rho =", round(easi_test$estimate, 3), # " p =", round(easi_test$p.value, 3), "\n")
# }
#
# #------- F1&2 Correlations with all Clincal Variables-------
# # Select numeric clinical variables
# clinical_vars <- master_table %>%
#   select(sa_abundance, EASI, age, TEWL_mean, pH_mean,
#          weeks_since_last_TCS_or_TCI) %>%
#   mutate(
#     TEWL_mean = as.numeric(gsub("none|not_done", NA, TEWL_mean)),
#     pH_mean = as.numeric(gsub("none|not_done", NA, pH_mean)),
#     EASI = as.numeric(EASI),
#     age = as.numeric(age)
#   )
# # Match to factor scores by NMR sample ID
# clinical_vars <- clinical_vars[
#   match(rownames(factors_df), master_table$NMR_sample_id), # ] # # # Correlate all clinical variables against Factors 1 and 2 # for(f in paste0("Factor", 1:2)) { # cat("\n======", f, "======\n") # for(v in colnames(clinical_vars)) { # test <- cor.test(factors_df[[f]], clinical_vars[[v]], # method = "spearman", exact = FALSE) # cat(v, ": rho =", round(test$estimate, 3),
#         "  p =", round(test$p.value, 3), "\n") # } # } # # # Add lesional status to factors_df and plot Factor 1 by lesional status # F1_lesional <- ggplot(factors_df, aes(x = lesional, y = Factor1, fill = lesional)) + # geom_boxplot() + # geom_jitter(width = 0.1) + # labs(title = "Factor 1 scores by lesional status") + # theme_classic() # # # Test it statistically # wilcox.test(Factor1 ~ lesional, data = factors_df) # # ggsave("~/Desktop/Research_Project/Scripts/Integration/MOFA+/F1_by_Lesionality3.png", # plot = F1_lesional, # width = 8, height = 5, dpi = 300) # # # test all factors correlation with lesionality # for(f in paste0("Factor", 1:4)) { # test <- wilcox.test(factors_df[[f]] ~ factors_df$lesional)
#   cat(f, ": p =", round(test$p.value, 3), "\n") # } #
```

```         

Exploring the factors

# {r}
# plot_variance_explained(mofa_obj, max_r2 =10)
# 
# 
# # --- Top Weights ----------
# # Plots top n features with the highest absolute loading value
# # for a specific factor
# # Good for identifying the main drivers of a factor
# # high absolute loading value >0.8
# 
# target_factors <- 1:4
# target_views <- c("species", "metabolites")
# 
# for (f in target_factors) {
#   for (v in target_views) {
#     
#     # 1. Create the Top Weights plot
#     p <- plot_top_weights(mofa_obj, 
#                           view = v, 
#                           factor = f, 
#                           nfeatures = 10, 
#                           scale = TRUE) + 
#       ggtitle(paste("Top 10", v, "Weights for Factor", f)) +
#       theme(axis.text.y = element_text(size = 10)) # Adjust font here
#     
#     # 2. Create a dynamic filename
#     file_name <- paste0("~/Desktop/Research_Project/Scripts/Integration/MOFA+/top_", v, "_weights_F", f, ".png")
#     
#     # 3. Save
#     ggsave(file_name, plot = p, width = 9, height = 6, dpi = 300)
#     
#     message(paste("Saved plot for Factor", f, "View", v))
#   }
# }
```

Three-view model with pathways

```{r}
# {r} # # ------- Post-hoc Analysis - 3 View Model ------- #  # # Add clinical variables to factors dataframe # factors_df <- as.data.frame(factors) # factors_df$sa_abundance <- master_table$sa_abundance[ #   match(rownames(factors_df), master_table$NMR_sample_id)] # factors_df$EASI <- master_table$EASI[ #   match(rownames(factors_df), master_table$NMR_sample_id)] # factors_df$lesional <- master_table$lesional[ #   match(rownames(factors_df), master_table$NMR_sample_id)] #  # # -------- Correlations with all factors (not just 1&2) ------------- # # Now we know Factors 1 and 2 are SA-associated, test all 4 # for(f in paste0("Factor", 1:4)) { #   cat("\n---", f, "---\n") #    #   sa_test <- cor.test(factors_df[[f]], #                       factors_df$sa_abundance, #                       method = "spearman", exact = FALSE) #   cat("SA:   rho =", round(sa_test$estimate, 3), #       "  p =", round(sa_test$p.value, 3), "\n") #    #   easi_test <- cor.test(factors_df[[f]], #                         factors_df$EASI, #                         method = "spearman", exact = FALSE) #   cat("EASI: rho =", round(easi_test$estimate, 3), #       "  p =", round(easi_test$p.value, 3), "\n") # } #  # # -------- Correlations with all clinical variables ------- # clinical_vars <- master_table %>% #   select(sa_abundance, EASI, age, TEWL_mean, pH_mean, #          weeks_since_last_TCS_or_TCI) %>% #   mutate( #     TEWL_mean = as.numeric(gsub("none|not_done", NA, TEWL_mean)), #     pH_mean = as.numeric(gsub("none|not_done", NA, pH_mean)), #     EASI = as.numeric(EASI), #     age = as.numeric(age) #   ) # clinical_vars <- clinical_vars[ #   match(rownames(factors_df), master_table$NMR_sample_id), ] #  # # Test all 4 factors against all clinical variables # for(f in paste0("Factor", 1:4)) { #   cat("\n======", f, "======\n") #   for(v in colnames(clinical_vars)) { #     test <- cor.test(factors_df[[f]], clinical_vars[[v]], #                      method = "spearman", exact = FALSE) #     cat(v, ": rho =", round(test$estimate, 3), #         "  p =", round(test$p.value, 3), "\n") #   } # } #  # # -------- Lesionality ------- # F1_lesional <- ggplot(factors_df, aes(x = lesional, y = Factor1,  #                                        fill = lesional)) + #   geom_boxplot() + #   geom_jitter(width = 0.1) + #   scale_fill_manual(values = c("LES" = "#F8766D", "NON_LES" = "#00BFC4")) + #   labs(title = "Factor 1 scores by lesional status") + #   theme_classic() #  # wilcox.test(Factor1 ~ lesional, data = factors_df) #  # ggsave("~/Desktop/Research_Project/Scripts/Integration/MOFA+/F1_by_Lesionality_3view.png", #        plot = F1_lesional, width = 8, height = 5, dpi = 300) #  # # Test all factors against lesionality # for(f in paste0("Factor", 1:4)) { #   test <- wilcox.test(factors_df[[f]] ~ factors_df$lesional) #   cat(f, ": p =", round(test$p.value, 3), "\n") # } #  # # -------- Variance explained ------- # plot_variance_explained(mofa_obj, max_r2 = 10) #  # # -------- Top weights - now includes pathways view ------- # target_factors <- 1:4 # target_views <- c("species", "metabolites", "pathways")  # added pathways #  # for(f in target_factors) { #   for(v in target_views) { #     p <- plot_top_weights(mofa_obj, #                           view = v, #                           factor = f, #                           nfeatures = 10, #                           scale = TRUE) + #       ggtitle(paste("Top 10", v, "Weights for Factor", f)) + #       theme(axis.text.y = element_text(size = 10)) #      #     file_name <- paste0("~/Desktop/Research_Project/Scripts/Integration/MOFA+/top_", #                         v, "_weights_F", f, "_3view.png") #     ggsave(file_name, plot = p, width = 9, height = 6, dpi = 300) #     message(paste("Saved plot for Factor", f, "View", v)) #   } # } #  # # Factor 1 scores by SA abundance - scatter plot # factors_df$sa_abundance <- as.numeric(factors_df$sa_abundance) #  # p_f1_sa <- ggplot(factors_df, aes(x = sa_abundance, y = Factor1,  #                                    colour = lesional)) + #   geom_point(size = 3) + #   geom_smooth(method = "lm", se = TRUE, colour = "black", linetype = "dashed") + #   scale_colour_manual(values = c("LES" = "#E41A1C", "NON_LES" = "#377EB8")) + #   labs(title = "MOFA+ Factor 1 vs SA Abundance", #        x = "SA Abundance (%)", #        y = "Factor 1 Score", #        colour = "Lesional Status") + #   annotate("text", x = max(factors_df$sa_abundance, na.rm = TRUE) * 0.1, #            y = max(factors_df$Factor1, na.rm = TRUE) * 0.9, #            label = "rho = -0.776, p < 0.001", #            hjust = 0, size = 4.5) + #   theme_classic() #  # p_f1_sa #  # ggsave("~/Desktop/Research_Project/Scripts/Integration/MOFA+/F1_by_SA_3view.png", #        plot = p_f1_sa, width = 8, height = 5, dpi = 300, bg = "white")
```

## Step 5: Linear **Regressions - Identifying SA High/Low Associated Features**

Identifying molecular signatures driven by SA colonisation, running feature-wise linear regressions across two data modalities: NMR Metabolites (n=38) and microbial pathways (n = 248)

-   **The Model:** treating each metabolite or pathway as a dependent variable to see if their levels differ significantly between the SA high and low groups:

    -   Feature∼SA status+Lesionality+pH

-   **The Predictor:** SA abundance is the variable of interest, so asking... *"*Is there a significant difference in the level of this feature between high SA and low SA samples, independent of pH and skin inflammation?

-   **Covariate Adjustment:**

    -   **pH:** was significant (\<0.05) in upstream confounder checking

    -   **Lesionality:** fixed covariate to make sure associations are driven by SA abundance not just general skin inflammation

-   **Multiple Testing Correction:** to account for high number of simultaneous hypotheses (especially in the 248 microbial pathways), p-values are adjusted using Benjamini-Hochberg (FDR)

-   **Validation:** significant results from the linear regression are cross-validated using Spearman correlation to ensure the differences between high and low groups are robust and not driven by outliers

### 5.1. Setup

```{r}
library(ggplot2)
library(dplyr)
library(stringr)
 
OUT_DIR <- "~/Desktop/Research_Project/Scripts/Integration/SA_Regressions/Cleaned/"
dir.create(OUT_DIR, showWarnings = FALSE, recursive = TRUE)

## ----Define SA high/low groups ------
# High = sa_abundance > 35, Low = sa_abundance < 10, mid-range -> NA
master_table <- master_table %>%
  mutate(SA_status = case_when(
    sa_abundance > 35 ~ "High",
    sa_abundance < 10 ~ "Low",
    TRUE              ~ NA_character_
  ))
 
# 'Low' set as reference level: regression coefficients reflect the
# High-vs-Low contrast
master_table$SA_status <- factor(master_table$SA_status, levels = c("Low", "High"))
 
# Check group sizes (including NA / mid-range samples)
table(master_table$SA_status, useNA = "always")

## ---- Helper: p-value -> significance stars ---

 
get_stars <- function(p) {
  if (p < 0.001) return("***")
  if (p < 0.01)  return("**")
  if (p < 0.05)  return("*")
  return("ns")
}
```

### 5.2. Linear Regressions

```{r}
## ---- Regression functions -----
 
# SA ~ feature, adjusting for lesional status and pH
run_omics_regression <- function(data_matrix, master_df, predictor_col = "SA_status") {
 
  results_list <- list()
  common_ids   <- intersect(rownames(data_matrix), master_df$NMR_sample_id)
  master_sub   <- master_df[master_df$NMR_sample_id %in% common_ids, ]
 
  for (feature in colnames(data_matrix)) {
 
    idx     <- match(common_ids, master_sub$NMR_sample_id)
    df_temp <- data.frame(
      outcome  = as.numeric(as.character(data_matrix[common_ids, feature])),
      SA_group = as.factor(master_sub[[predictor_col]][idx]),
      lesional = as.factor(master_sub$lesional[idx]),
      pH       = as.numeric(as.character(master_sub$pH[idx]))
    )
    df_temp <- na.omit(df_temp)
 
    if (length(unique(df_temp$SA_group)) < 2 || nrow(df_temp) < 5) next
 
    model <- tryCatch({
      lm(outcome ~ SA_group + lesional + pH, data = df_temp)
    }, error = function(e) return(NULL))
 
    if (!is.null(model)) {
      summ <- summary(model)$coefficients
      if ("SA_groupHigh" %in% rownames(summ)) {
        res         <- as.data.frame(t(summ["SA_groupHigh", ]))
        res$feature <- feature
        results_list[[feature]] <- res
      }
    }
  }
 
  if (length(results_list) == 0) stop("No significant coefficients found. Check group sizes!")
 
  final_df            <- do.call(rbind, results_list)
  colnames(final_df)  <- c("Estimate", "Std_Error", "t_value", "p_value", "feature")
  final_df$p_adjusted <- p.adjust(final_df$p_value, method = "fdr")
  return(final_df)
}
 
# Spearman correlation: feature ~ continuous sa_abundance (sensitivity check)
run_spearman_check <- function(data_matrix, master_df, predictor_col = "sa_abundance") {
 
  results_list <- list()
  common_ids   <- intersect(rownames(data_matrix), master_df$NMR_sample_id)
 
  for (feature in colnames(data_matrix)) {
 
    val_outcome   <- as.numeric(as.character(data_matrix[common_ids, feature]))
    val_predictor <- as.numeric(as.character(master_df[[predictor_col]][match(common_ids, master_df$NMR_sample_id)]))
    df_temp       <- na.omit(data.frame(x = val_predictor, y = val_outcome))
 
    if (nrow(df_temp) > 5) {
      test <- cor.test(df_temp$x, df_temp$y, method = "spearman", exact = FALSE)
      results_list[[feature]] <- data.frame(
        feature    = feature,
        rho        = as.numeric(test$estimate),
        p_spearman = as.numeric(test$p.value)
      )
    }
  }
  return(do.call(rbind, results_list))
}
 
# Pathway -> Metabolite regression: metabolite ~ pathway + lesional + pH
run_pathway_metabolite_regression <- function(nmr_matrix, pathway_matrix, master_df) {
 
  results_list <- list()
  common_ids   <- Reduce(intersect, list(
    rownames(nmr_matrix),
    rownames(pathway_matrix),
    master_df$NMR_sample_id
  ))
 
  master_sub   <- master_df[master_df$NMR_sample_id %in% common_ids, ]
  idx          <- match(common_ids, master_sub$NMR_sample_id)
  lesional_vec <- as.factor(master_sub$lesional[idx])
  pH_vec       <- as.numeric(as.character(master_sub$pH[idx]))
 
  for (pathway in colnames(pathway_matrix)) {
 
    pathway_vec <- as.numeric(as.character(pathway_matrix[common_ids, pathway]))
 
    for (metabolite in colnames(nmr_matrix)) {
 
      metabolite_vec <- as.numeric(as.character(nmr_matrix[common_ids, metabolite]))
      df_temp <- na.omit(data.frame(
        outcome  = metabolite_vec,
        pathway  = pathway_vec,
        lesional = lesional_vec,
        pH       = pH_vec
      ))
 
      if (nrow(df_temp) < 5) next
 
      model <- tryCatch({
        lm(outcome ~ pathway + lesional + pH, data = df_temp)
      }, error = function(e) return(NULL))
 
      if (!is.null(model)) {
        summ <- summary(model)$coefficients
        if ("pathway" %in% rownames(summ)) {
          res            <- as.data.frame(t(summ["pathway", ]))
          res$metabolite <- metabolite
          res$pathway    <- pathway
          results_list[[paste(pathway, metabolite, sep = "__")]] <- res
        }
      }
    }
  }
 
  final_df                 <- do.call(rbind, results_list)
  colnames(final_df)[1:4]  <- c("Estimate", "Std_Error", "t_value", "p_value")
  final_df$p_adjusted      <- p.adjust(final_df$p_value, method = "fdr")
  return(final_df)
}
```

```{r}
## Run the three regressions
 
results_metabolites <- run_omics_regression(nmr_matched,     master_table, predictor_col = "SA_status")
results_pathways    <- run_omics_regression(pathway_matched, master_table, predictor_col = "SA_status")
results_path_met    <- run_pathway_metabolite_regression(nmr_matched, pathway_matched, master_table)
 
```

### 5.3. Spearman Sensitivity Checks (continuous SA / pathway-metabolite pairs)

Running Spearman's correlations on the whole dataset and then finding overlapping significant features with the linear regressions, to see what is significant in both tests.

This doesn't use the high/low groups like the linear regressions, it works on a continuous scale.

```{r}
# SA ~ metabolites
spearman_met  <- run_spearman_check(nmr_matched, master_table, predictor_col = "sa_abundance")
overlap_met   <- merge(results_metabolites, spearman_met, by = "feature")
high_conf_met <- overlap_met[overlap_met$p_value < 0.05 & overlap_met$p_spearman < 0.05, ]
 
# SA ~ pathways
spearman_path  <- run_spearman_check(pathway_matched, master_table, predictor_col = "sa_abundance")
overlap_path   <- merge(results_pathways, spearman_path, by = "feature")
high_conf_path <- overlap_path[overlap_path$p_value < 0.05 & overlap_path$p_spearman < 0.05, ]
 
# Pathway ~ metabolite pairs: only validate pairs surviving FDR correction
fdr_hits   <- results_path_met[results_path_met$p_adjusted < 0.05, ]
common_ids <- Reduce(intersect, list(rownames(nmr_matched), rownames(pathway_matched), master_table$NMR_sample_id))
 
spearman_path_met_results <- list()
for (i in seq_len(nrow(fdr_hits))) {
  path <- fdr_hits$pathway[i]
  met  <- fdr_hits$metabolite[i]
 
  x <- as.numeric(pathway_matched[common_ids, path])
  y <- as.numeric(nmr_matched[common_ids, met])
  df_temp <- na.omit(data.frame(x = x, y = y))
 
  if (nrow(df_temp) > 5) {
    test <- cor.test(df_temp$x, df_temp$y, method = "spearman", exact = FALSE)
    spearman_path_met_results[[paste(path, met, sep = "__")]] <- data.frame(
      pathway    = path,
      metabolite = met,
      rho        = as.numeric(test$estimate),
      p_spearman = as.numeric(test$p.value)
    )
  }
}
 
spearman_path_met_df <- do.call(rbind, spearman_path_met_results)
high_conf_path_met   <- merge(fdr_hits, spearman_path_met_df, by = c("pathway", "metabolite"))
high_conf_path_met   <- high_conf_path_met[high_conf_path_met$p_spearman < 0.05, ]
high_conf_path_met   <- high_conf_path_met[order(high_conf_path_met$p_adjusted), ]
high_conf_path_met$stars <- sapply(high_conf_path_met$p_value, get_stars)
```

```{r}
## Pathway name mapping (PWY identifiers -> readable names)
 
pathway_name_map <- c(
  "ARGININE.SYN4.PWY..L.ornithine.biosynthesis.II"                        = "L-Ornithine Biosynthesis",
  "ASPASN.PWY..superpathway.of.L.aspartate.and.L.asparagine.biosynthesis" = "L-Aspartate & L-Asparagine Biosynthesis",
  "GLYCOGENSYNTH.PWY..glycogen.biosynthesis.I..from.ADP.D.Glucose."       = "Glycogen Biosynthesis",
  "HEME.BIOSYNTHESIS.II.1..heme.b.biosynthesis.V..aerobic."               = "Heme Biosynthesis (Aerobic)",
  "P161.PWY..acetylene.degradation..anaerobic."                           = "Anaerobic Acetylene Degradation",
  "PWY.1269..CMP.3.deoxy.D.manno.octulosonate.biosynthesis"               = "CMP-Kdo Biosynthesis",
  "PWY.5136..fatty.acid..beta..oxidation.II..plant.peroxisome."           = "Fatty Acid Beta-Oxidation",
  "PWY.6292..superpathway.of.L.cysteine.biosynthesis..mammalian."         = "L-Cysteine Biosynthesis",
  "PWY.8182..valproate..beta..oxidation"                                  = "Valproate Beta-Oxidation",
  "PWY0.1296..purine.ribonucleosides.degradation"                         = "Purine Ribonucleoside Degradation",
  "PWY0.1297..superpathway.of.purine.deoxyribonucleosides.degradation"    = "Purine Deoxyribonucleoside Degradation",
  "PWY0.1477..ethanolamine.utilization"                                   = "Ethanolamine Utilisation",
  "PWY66.409..superpathway.of.purine.nucleotide.salvage"                  = "Purine Nucleotide Salvage",
  "PWY.5910..superpathway.of.geranylgeranyldiphosphate.biosynthesis.I..via.mevalonate." = "Geranylgeranyl Diphosphate Biosynthesis",
  "PWY.6859..all.trans.farnesol.biosynthesis"                             = "Farnesol Biosynthesis",
  "PWY.922..mevalonate.pathway.I..eukaryotes.and.bacteria."               = "Mevalonate Pathway",
  "LACTOSECAT.PWY..lactose.and.galactose.degradation.I"                   = "Lactose & Galactose Degradation",
  "PWY.621..sucrose.degradation.III..sucrose.invertase."                  = "Sucrose Degradation (Invertase)",
  "PWY.5384..sucrose.degradation.IV..sucrose.phosphorylase."              = "Sucrose Degradation (Phosphorylase)",
  "POLYISOPRENSYN.PWY..polyisoprenoid.biosynthesis..E..coli."             = "Polyisoprenoid Biosynthesis",
  "P125.PWY..superpathway.of..R.R..butanediol.biosynthesis"               = "Butanediol Biosynthesis",
  "PWY.1861..formaldehyde.assimilation.II..assimilatory.RuMP.Cycle."      = "Formaldehyde Assimilation (RuMP Cycle)",
  "PWY.702..L.methionine.biosynthesis.II"                                 = "L-Methionine Biosynthesis",
  "PWY.7391..isoprene.biosynthesis.II..engineered."                       = "Isoprene Biosynthesis",
  "PANTO.PWY..phosphopantothenate.biosynthesis.I"                          = "Phosphopantothenate Biosynthesis",
  "PWY.5667..CDP.diacylglycerol.biosynthesis.I"                            = "CDP-Diacylglycerol Biosynthesis",
  "PWY.6122..5.aminoimidazole.ribonucleotide.biosynthesis.II"              = "5-Aminoimidazole Ribonucleotide Biosynthesis",
  "PWY.6277..superpathway.of.5.aminoimidazole.ribonucleotide.biosynthesis" = "5-Aminoimidazole Ribonucleotide Biosynthesis (Superpathway)",
  "PWY66.429..fatty.acid.biosynthesis.initiation..mitochondria."           = "Mitochondrial Fatty Acid Biosynthesis",
  "UDPNAGSYN.PWY..UDP.N.acetyl.D.glucosamine.biosynthesis.I"               = "UDP-N-Acetylglucosamine Biosynthesis",
  "HSERMETANA.PWY..L.methionine.biosynthesis.III"                          = "L-Methionine Biosynthesis III",
  "TCA.GLYOX.BYPASS..superpathway.of.glyoxylate.bypass.and.TCA"            = "Glyoxylate Bypass & TCA Cycle",
  "ARGSYNBSUB.PWY..L.arginine.biosynthesis.II..acetyl.cycle."              = "L-Arginine Biosynthesis (Acetyl Cycle)",
  "PWY.4984..urea.cycle"                                                   = "Urea Cycle",
  "ANAEROFRUCAT.PWY..homolactic.fermentation"                              = "Homolactic Fermentation"
)
 
clean_pathway_name <- function(feature) {
  ifelse(feature %in% names(pathway_name_map),
         pathway_name_map[feature],
         gsub("\\.", " ", feature))
}
```

```{r}
## Results summary

cat("=== SA ~ METABOLITES (Multivariate Linear Regression) ===\n")
cat("Total metabolites tested:          ", nrow(results_metabolites), "\n")
cat("Significant at p < 0.05:           ", sum(results_metabolites$p_value < 0.05, na.rm = TRUE), "\n")
cat("Surviving FDR (padj < 0.05):       ", sum(results_metabolites$p_adjusted < 0.05, na.rm = TRUE), "\n")
cat("High-confidence (regression & Spearman p < 0.05):", nrow(high_conf_met), "\n\n")
 
cat("=== SA ~ PATHWAYS (Multivariate Linear Regression) ===\n")
cat("Total pathways tested:             ", nrow(results_pathways), "\n")
cat("Significant at p < 0.05:           ", sum(results_pathways$p_value < 0.05, na.rm = TRUE), "\n")
cat("Surviving FDR (padj < 0.05):       ", sum(results_pathways$p_adjusted < 0.05, na.rm = TRUE), "\n")
cat("High-confidence (regression & Spearman p < 0.05):", nrow(high_conf_path), "\n\n")
 
cat("=== PATHWAY ~ METABOLITE (Pairwise Regression) ===\n")
cat("Total pairs tested:                ", nrow(results_path_met), "\n")
cat("Significant at p < 0.05:           ", sum(results_path_met$p_value < 0.05, na.rm = TRUE), "\n")
cat("Surviving FDR (padj < 0.05):       ", sum(results_path_met$p_adjusted < 0.05, na.rm = TRUE), "\n")
cat("High-confidence (FDR & Spearman p < 0.05):", nrow(high_conf_path_met), "\n")
 
## Save result tables

write.csv(results_metabolites, paste0(OUT_DIR, "SA_Metabolite_Regressions_Full.csv"),          row.names = FALSE)
write.csv(results_pathways,    paste0(OUT_DIR, "SA_Pathway_Regressions_Full.csv"),             row.names = FALSE)
write.csv(results_path_met,    paste0(OUT_DIR, "Pathway_Metabolite_Regressions_Full.csv"),     row.names = FALSE)
 
write.csv(results_metabolites[results_metabolites$p_value < 0.05, ], paste0(OUT_DIR, "SA_Metabolite_Hits_p05.csv"), row.names = FALSE)
write.csv(results_pathways[results_pathways$p_value < 0.05, ],       paste0(OUT_DIR, "SA_Pathway_Hits_p05.csv"),    row.names = FALSE)
write.csv(results_path_met[results_path_met$p_value < 0.05, ],       paste0(OUT_DIR, "Pathway_Metabolite_Hits_p05.csv"), row.names = FALSE)
 
write.csv(high_conf_met,      paste0(OUT_DIR, "High_Confidence_Metabolites_SA.csv"),           row.names = FALSE)
write.csv(high_conf_path,     paste0(OUT_DIR, "High_Confidence_Pathways_SA.csv"),              row.names = FALSE)
write.csv(high_conf_path_met, paste0(OUT_DIR, "High_Confidence_Pathway_Metabolite_Pairs.csv"), row.names = FALSE)
 
```

### 5.4. Visualisations

```{r}
## Visualisations
# --- Boxplot: SA-High vs SA-Low ---
create_sa_boxplot <- function(df, y_var, title_label) {
  plot_data <- df[!is.na(df$SA_status), ]
  ggplot(plot_data, aes(x = SA_status, y = .data[[y_var]], fill = SA_status)) +
    geom_boxplot(outlier.shape = NA, alpha = 0.7) +
    geom_jitter(width = 0.2, size = 2, alpha = 0.6) +
    scale_fill_manual(values = c("Low" = "#56B4E9", "High" = "#D55E00")) +
    labs(title = title_label, subtitle = "SA High vs SA Low",
         x = "S. aureus Status", y = "Relative Abundance") +
    theme_minimal() +
    theme(legend.position = "none",
          plot.title    = element_text(face = "bold", size = 20),
          plot.subtitle = element_text(size = 16),
          axis.text     = element_text(size = 16),
          axis.title    = element_text(size = 16, face = "bold"))
}
 
# --- Prepare plotting dataframes ---
meta_cols <- master_table[, c("NMR_sample_id", "SA_status", "lesional", "pH_mean")]
 
nmr_df <- as.data.frame(nmr_matched, check.names = FALSE)
nmr_df$NMR_sample_id <- rownames(nmr_df)
plot_master <- merge(nmr_df, meta_cols, by = "NMR_sample_id")
 
path_df <- as.data.frame(pathway_matched, check.names = FALSE)
path_df$NMR_sample_id <- rownames(path_df)

plot_pathways <- merge(path_df, meta_cols, by = "NMR_sample_id")
 
# --- Individual boxplots: significant metabolites (p < 0.05) ---
metabolite_hits <- results_metabolites[results_metabolites$p_value < 0.05, ]
for (met in metabolite_hits$feature) {
  if (met %in% colnames(plot_master)) {
    p <- create_sa_boxplot(plot_master, met, paste("Metabolite:", met))
    ggsave(paste0(OUT_DIR, "Metabolite_", met, ".png"), p, width = 6, height = 5, dpi = 300)
  }
}
 
# --- Individual boxplots: significant pathways (p < 0.05), readable names ---
pathway_hits <- results_pathways[results_pathways$p_value < 0.05 & !is.na(results_pathways$p_value), ]
pathway_hits$stars         <- sapply(pathway_hits$p_value, get_stars)
pathway_hits$feature_clean <- clean_pathway_name(pathway_hits$feature)
pathway_hits$labeled_feature <- paste0(pathway_hits$feature_clean, " ", pathway_hits$stars)
 
for (i in seq_len(nrow(pathway_hits))) {
  path <- pathway_hits$feature[i]
  if (path %in% colnames(plot_pathways)) {
    clean_name <- gsub("[^[:alnum:]]", "_", pathway_hits$feature_clean[i])
    p <- create_sa_boxplot(plot_pathways, path, paste("Pathway:", pathway_hits$feature_clean[i]))
    ggsave(paste0(OUT_DIR, "Pathway_", clean_name, ".png"), p, width = 8, height = 6, dpi = 300)
  }
}
 
# --- Forest plot: all significant pathways ---
path_significance_plot <- ggplot(pathway_hits, aes(x = Estimate, y = reorder(labeled_feature, Estimate))) +
  geom_vline(xintercept = 0, linetype = "dashed", color = "gray50") +
  geom_errorbarh(aes(xmin = Estimate - Std_Error, xmax = Estimate + Std_Error),
                 height = 0.3, linewidth = 1, color = "#009E73") +
  geom_point(size = 8, color = "#009E73") +
  geom_text(aes(x = Estimate + Std_Error, label = stars),
            hjust = -0.3, vjust = 0.4, size = 18, color = "black", fontface = "bold") +
  scale_y_discrete(labels = function(x) str_wrap(x, width = 35)) +
  labs(title    = "Microbial Pathway Shifts: High vs Low SA",
       subtitle = "(* p < 0.05, ** p < 0.01, *** p < 0.001)",
       x = "Regression Estimate (Abundance)", y = NULL) +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(plot.title    = element_text(face = "bold", size = 28),
        plot.subtitle = element_text(size = 22, color = "black"),
        axis.text.y   = element_text(size = 22, face = "bold", lineheight = 0.8),
        axis.text.x   = element_text(size = 22),
        axis.title.x  = element_text(size = 22, face = "bold"),
        panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),
        plot.margin   = margin(t = 10, r = 60, b = 10, l = 80))
 
ggsave(paste0(OUT_DIR, "Pathways_Significance.png"), path_significance_plot,
       width = 15, height = 0.5 * nrow(pathway_hits) + 3, dpi = 300)
 
# --- Forest plots: top 5 metabolites and top 5 pathways by p-value ---
top_met_df <- head(metabolite_hits[order(metabolite_hits$p_value), ], 5)
top_met_df$stars <- sapply(top_met_df$p_value, get_stars)
top_met_df$labeled_feature <- paste0(top_met_df$feature, " ", top_met_df$stars)
 
top_path_df <- head(pathway_hits[order(pathway_hits$p_value), ], 5)
top_path_df$stars <- sapply(top_path_df$p_value, get_stars)
top_path_df$feature_clean <- clean_pathway_name(top_path_df$feature)
top_path_df$labeled_feature <- paste0(top_path_df$feature_clean, " ", top_path_df$stars)
 
meta_top <- ggplot(top_met_df, aes(x = Estimate, y = reorder(labeled_feature, Estimate))) +
  geom_vline(xintercept = 0, linetype = "dashed", color = "black") +
  geom_errorbarh(aes(xmin = Estimate - Std_Error, xmax = Estimate + Std_Error),
                 height = 0.3, linewidth = 1, color = "#D55E00") +
  geom_point(size = 6, color = "#D55E00") +
  geom_text(aes(label = stars), hjust = -0.5, vjust = -0.5, size = 8, color = "black") +
  labs(title    = "Metabolic Shifts: High vs Low SA",
       subtitle = "(* p < 0.05, ** p < 0.01, *** p < 0.001)",
       x = "Regression Estimate (NMR Intensity)", y = NULL) +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(axis.text.y   = element_text(size = 22, face = "bold"),
        axis.text.x   = element_text(size = 22),
        axis.title.x  = element_text(size = 25, face = "bold"),
        plot.title    = element_text(face = "bold", size = 25),
        plot.subtitle = element_text(size = 22))
 
ggsave(paste0(OUT_DIR, "Metabolites_Significance_forest.png"), meta_top, width = 10, height = 6, dpi = 300)
 
path_top5_plot <- ggplot(top_path_df, aes(x = Estimate, y = reorder(labeled_feature, Estimate))) +
  geom_vline(xintercept = 0, linetype = "dashed", color = "gray50") +
  geom_errorbarh(aes(xmin = Estimate - Std_Error, xmax = Estimate + Std_Error),
                 height = 0.3, linewidth = 1, color = "#009E73") +
  geom_point(size = 6, color = "#009E73") +
  geom_text(aes(x = Estimate + Std_Error, label = stars),
            hjust = -0.3, vjust = 0.4, size = 8, color = "black", fontface = "bold") +
  scale_y_discrete(labels = function(x) str_wrap(x, width = 35)) +
  labs(title    = "Microbial Pathway Shifts: High vs Low SA (Top 5)",
       subtitle = "(* p < 0.05, ** p < 0.01, *** p < 0.001)",
       x = "Regression Estimate (Abundance)", y = NULL) +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(plot.title    = element_text(face = "bold", size = 22),
        plot.subtitle = element_text(size = 16, color = "gray30"),
        axis.text.y   = element_text(size = 16, face = "bold", lineheight = 0.8),
        axis.text.x   = element_text(size = 16),
        axis.title.x  = element_text(size = 18, face = "bold"),
        panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),
        plot.margin   = margin(t = 10, r = 50, b = 10, l = 80))
 
ggsave(paste0(OUT_DIR, "Pathways_Significance_forest.png"), path_top5_plot, width = 14, height = 7, dpi = 300)
 
# --- High-confidence bar plot: metabolites ---
metab_hits_plot <- ggplot(high_conf_met,
                           aes(x = Estimate, y = reorder(feature, Estimate),
                               fill = Estimate > 0)) +
  geom_bar(stat = "identity", alpha = 0.8, color = "black", linewidth = 0.2, width=0.5) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c("#D55E00", "#56B4E9"),
                    labels = c("Decreased with SA", "Increased with SA"),
                    name   = "Direction of Change") +
  labs(title    = "Metabolites Associated with S. aureus",
       subtitle = "High-Confidence Hits (Regression & Spearman p < 0.05)",
       x = "Regression Estimate (Effect Size)", y = NULL) +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(plot.title      = element_text(face = "bold", size = 28),
        plot.subtitle   = element_text(size = 20),
        axis.text.y     = element_text(size = 20, face = "bold"),
        axis.text.x     = element_text(size = 20),
        axis.title.x    = element_text(size = 22, face = "bold"),
        legend.position = "bottom",
        legend.title    = element_text(size = 22, face = "bold"),
        legend.text     = element_text(size = 20 ),
        legend.key.size = unit(1.2, "cm"),
        plot.margin     = margin(10, 10, 10, 20))
 
ggsave(paste0(OUT_DIR, "SA_Metabolite_Hits_Final.png"), metab_hits_plot, width = 10, height = 6, dpi = 300)
 
# --- High-confidence bar plot: pathways ---
high_conf_path_plot <- high_conf_path[!is.na(high_conf_path$feature), ]
high_conf_path_plot$feature_clean <- clean_pathway_name(high_conf_path_plot$feature)
 
path_hits_plot <- ggplot(high_conf_path_plot,
                          aes(x = Estimate, y = reorder(feature_clean, Estimate),
                              fill = Estimate > 0)) +
  geom_bar(stat = "identity", alpha = 0.8, color = "black", linewidth = 0.2) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c("#D55E00", "#56B4E9"),
                    labels = c("Decreased with SA", "Increased with SA"),
                    name   = "Direction of Change") +
  labs(title    = "Microbial Pathway Shifts Associated with S. aureus",
       subtitle = paste0("High-Confidence Pathways (Regression & Spearman p < 0.05) | n=",
                          nrow(high_conf_path_plot)),
       x = "Regression Estimate (Effect Size)", y = NULL) +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(plot.title      = element_text(face = "bold", size = 24),
        plot.subtitle   = element_text(size = 18, color = "gray30"),
        axis.text.y     = element_text(size = 18, face = "bold"),
        axis.text.x     = element_text(size = 16),
        axis.title.x    = element_text(size = 18, face = "bold"),
        legend.position = "bottom",
        legend.title    = element_text(size = 18, face = "bold"),
        legend.text     = element_text(size = 16),
        legend.key.size = unit(1.2, "cm"),
        panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),
        plot.margin     = margin(t = 10, r = 50, b = 10, l = 50))
 
ggsave(paste0(OUT_DIR, "SA_Pathway_Hits.png"), path_hits_plot,
       width = 16, height = 0.5 * nrow(high_conf_path_plot) + 4, dpi = 300)
 
# --- Pathway-Metabolite heatmap (top 20 pathways by FDR) ---
top_pathways_heatmap <- high_conf_path_met %>%
  group_by(pathway) %>%
  summarise(best_p = min(p_adjusted)) %>%
  arrange(best_p) %>%
  slice_head(n = 20) %>%
  pull(pathway)
 
heatmap_filtered <- high_conf_path_met[high_conf_path_met$pathway %in% top_pathways_heatmap, ]
heatmap_filtered$pathway_clean <- clean_pathway_name(heatmap_filtered$pathway)
 
max_abs <- max(abs(heatmap_filtered$Estimate), na.rm = TRUE)
 
library(ggplot2)
library(stringr) # Ensure this is loaded for str_wrap

heatmap_plot <- ggplot(heatmap_filtered, aes(x = metabolite, y = pathway_clean, fill = Estimate)) +
  geom_tile(color = "white", linewidth = 0.5) +
  
  # 1. Move color inside aes() and map it to Estimate
  geom_text(aes(label = stars, color = Estimate), size = 8, fontface = "bold", vjust = 0.7) +
  
  scale_y_discrete(labels = function(x) stringr::str_wrap(x, width = 35)) +
  
  scale_fill_gradient2(low = "#D55E00", mid = "white", high = "#56B4E9",
                       midpoint = 0, limits = c(-max_abs, max_abs),
                       name = "Regression\nEstimate") +
  
  # 2. Add a matching color scale for the text (Dark text on light mid, white text on dark ends)
  scale_color_gradient2(low = "white", mid = "gray20", high = "white",
                        midpoint = 0, limits = c(-max_abs, max_abs),
                        guide = "none") + # Hide this extra legend
  
  labs(title    = "Microbial Pathway-Metabolite Associations",
       subtitle = "Top 20 Pathways by FDR | * p<0.05  ** p<0.01  *** p<0.001",
       x = "Metabolite", y = "Pathway") +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(plot.title    = element_text(face = "bold", size = 29),
        plot.subtitle = element_text(size = 22, color = "gray30"),
        axis.text.x   = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1, size = 20, face = "bold"),
        axis.text.y   = element_text(size = 20, face = "bold", lineheight = 0.8), 
        axis.title.x  = element_text(size = 22, face = "bold"),
        axis.title.y  = element_text(size = 22, face = "bold"),
        legend.title  = element_text(size = 22, face = "bold"),
        legend.text   = element_text(size = 22),
        panel.grid    = element_blank(),
        plot.margin   = margin(t = 10, r = 30, b = 20, l = 10))
ggsave(paste0(OUT_DIR, "Pathway_Metabolite_Heatmap_Top20.png"), heatmap_plot, width = 16, height = 18, dpi = 300)
 
# --- Top 20 pathway-metabolite pairs bar plot ---
top20 <- head(high_conf_path_met[order(high_conf_path_met$p_adjusted), ], 20)
top20$pathway_readable <- clean_pathway_name(top20$pathway)
top20$pair_label <- paste0(
  str_trunc(top20$pathway_readable, 40),
  "  ->  ",
  top20$metabolite,
  "  ", top20$stars
)
 
pair_plot <- ggplot(top20, aes(x = Estimate, y = reorder(pair_label, Estimate),
                                fill = Estimate > 0)) +
  geom_vline(xintercept = 0, linetype = "dashed", color = "gray50") +
  geom_bar(stat = "identity", alpha = 0.8, color = "black", linewidth = 0.2) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c("#D55E00", "#56B4E9"),
                    labels = c("Decreased with Pathway Activity",
                               "Increased with Pathway Activity"),
                    name   = "Direction of Change") +
  labs(title    = "Top 20 Pathway-Metabolite Associations",
       subtitle = "High-Confidence Pairs (FDR & Spearman p < 0.05)",
       x = "Regression Estimate (Effect Size)", y = NULL) +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(plot.title      = element_text(face = "bold", size = 22),
        plot.subtitle   = element_text(size = 18, color = "gray30"),
        axis.text.y     = element_text(size = 14, face = "bold"),
        axis.text.x     = element_text(size = 14),
        axis.title.x    = element_text(size = 18, face = "bold"),
        legend.position = "bottom",
        legend.title    = element_text(size = 16, face = "bold"),
        legend.text     = element_text(size = 14),
        legend.key.size = unit(1.2, "cm"),
        plot.margin     = margin(t = 10, r = 30, b = 10, l = 10))
 
ggsave(paste0(OUT_DIR, "Pathway_Metabolite_Top20.png"), pair_plot, width = 18, height = 11, dpi = 300)
 
```

## Step 6: Mediation Analysis

So step 5 shows that certain metabolites/pathways are correlated with S. aureus level. This mediation analysis will see if those metabolites/pathways are actually used by SA to drive skin lesionality.

-   Independent variable = SA level

-   Mediator = metabolites/pathways

-   Outcome = lesional vs non lesional

Will run a loop for each metabolite and pathway to see if any are significant mediators. Using bootstrapped confidence intervals (n=5000 permutations), ending up with an estimate of the indirect effect to build the CI.

Scaled (Z-scores) the pathways to handle the tiny abundance numbers. Used logistic regression because lesional is binary.

```{r}
# Load the required library
#if(!require(mediation)) install.packages("mediation")
library(mediation)

# Fix the metadata in plotting objects
plot_master$pH_mean <- as.numeric(as.character(plot_master$pH_mean))
plot_pathways$pH_mean <- as.numeric(as.character(plot_pathways$pH_mean))

# --- 1. Define the Master Mediation Function ---
# This handles both Metabolites (Standard) and Pathways (Scaled)
# 1. Create a list to store results
mediation_summary_list <- list()

# 2. Get the list of significant metabolites (from your earlier results)
# You can use metabolite_hits$feature or high_conf_met$feature
target_metabolites <- metabolite_hits$feature 

print(paste("Starting mediation for", length(target_metabolites), "metabolites..."))

for(met in target_metabolites) {
  tryCatch({
    # Run the stable mediation function we defined earlier
    med_out <- run_sa_mediation_stable(met, plot_master, is_pathway = FALSE)
    
    # Extract the key numbers
    sum_med <- summary(med_out)
    
    mediation_summary_list[[met]] <- data.frame(
      Metabolite = met,
      ACME_Estimate = med_out$d0,
      ACME_P_Value = med_out$d0.p,
      ADE_Estimate = med_out$z0,
      ADE_P_Value = med_out$z0.p,
      Prop_Mediated = med_out$n0,
      Total_Effect = med_out$tau.coef
    )
    
    print(paste("Completed:", met))
    
  }, error = function(e) {
    message(paste("Could not run mediation for", met, ":", e$message))
  })
}

# 3. Combine into one table
mediation_results_table <- do.call(rbind, mediation_summary_list)

# 4. View and Sort by P-value
mediation_results_table <- mediation_results_table[order(mediation_results_table$ACME_P_Value), ]
print(mediation_results_table)

# 5. Save the table
write.csv(mediation_results_table, "~/Desktop/Research_Project/Scripts/Integration/Mediation/Mediation_Results_Metabolites.csv", row.names = FALSE)

# 1. Create a list to store results
mediation_pathway_list <- list()

# 2. Get the top 5 pathways (already sorted by p-value from your earlier code)
target_pathways <- head(top_pathways$feature, 5)

print(paste("Starting mediation for", length(target_pathways), "pathways..."))

for(path in target_pathways) {
  tryCatch({
    # Run the stable mediation function with is_pathway = TRUE
    med_out <- run_sa_mediation_stable(path, plot_pathways, is_pathway = TRUE)
    
    # Extract the key statistics
    mediation_pathway_list[[path]] <- data.frame(
      Pathway = path,
      ACME_Estimate = med_out$d0,
      ACME_P_Value = med_out$d0.p,
      ADE_Estimate = med_out$z0,
      ADE_P_Value = med_out$z0.p,
      Prop_Mediated = med_out$n0,
      Total_Effect = med_out$tau.coef,
      Samples_Used = med_out$nobs
    )
    
    print(paste("Completed:", path))
    
  }, error = function(e) {
    message(paste("Could not run mediation for", path, ":", e$message))
  })
}

# 3. Combine into one table
mediation_pathway_results <- do.call(rbind, mediation_pathway_list)

# 4. Sort by Mediation P-value (ACME)
mediation_pathway_results <- mediation_pathway_results[order(mediation_pathway_results$ACME_P_Value), ]

# 5. Print to console to inspect
print(mediation_pathway_results)

# 6. Save as CSV to your Project Folder
write.csv(mediation_pathway_results, 
          "~/Desktop/Research_Project/Scripts/Integration/Mediation/Mediation_Results_Pathways.csv", 
          row.names = FALSE)
```

# Tier 2: scRNA-seq Mechanistic Subset

## Load Single Cell RNA-seq Data

Loading the merged Seurat object

```{r}
# 1. Define the path
file_path <- "~/Desktop/iawards/single cell/abu/abu onedrive contents/Bioinformatics/scRNAseq/r_adata_microbiome.rds"

# 2. Load the data
# Since the file is a Gzipped Workspace (RDX3 header), we use load() + gzfile()
if (file.exists(file_path)) {
  load(gzfile(file_path))
  message("Success: Object 'adata' loaded into environment.")
} else {
  stop("File not found. Please check the OneDrive sync status.")
}

# 3. Verify and Inspect
# Check dimensions and layers
print(adata)

# Check the first few features to see if they are Genes or Taxa
head(rownames(adata))
```

## QC

Excluded cells with less than 200 genes and \>5% mitochondrial gene expression

```{r}
# 1. Join all layers into one single matrix (Seurat v5 requirement)
all_counts <- JoinLayers(adata[["RNA"]])

# 2. Re-create the Seurat Object to reset and align
adata_new <- CreateSeuratObject(counts = all_counts, project = "Epidermis_QC")

# 3. FORCE the naming alignment (Since we verified the order matches)
# This fixes the "No cells found" error by making matrix names match metadata row names
colnames(adata_new) <- rownames(adata@meta.data)

# 4. Attach your original metadata (les, s_aureus, clusters, sample, etc.)
adata_new@meta.data <- adata@meta.data

# 5. Calculate Mitochondrial percentage (Now it will save correctly in metadata)
adata_new[["percent.mt"]] <- PercentageFeatureSet(adata_new, pattern = "^MT-")

# 6. Apply your Quality Control (QC) filtering
# Keep cells with > 200 genes AND < 5% mitochondrial DNA
adata_qc <- subset(adata_new, subset = nFeature_RNA > 200 & percent.mt < 5)

# 7. FINAL VERIFICATION (Check these outputs in your console)
cat("\n--- QC SUMMARY ---\n")
print(paste("Total cells after QC:", ncol(adata_qc)))

cat("\n--- LESIONAL VS NON-LESIONAL SPLIT ---\n")
print(table(adata_qc$les))

cat("\n--- CELL COUNTS PER CLUSTER ---\n")
print(table(adata_qc$clusters))

cat("\n--- SAMPLE CONTRIBUTION BY STATUS ---\n")
print(table(adata_qc$sample, adata_qc$les))


```

### DESeq2

```{r}

#------ Pseudobulk-------
# This collapses the cells so we can compare the 6 samples (A17, A18, etc.)
pseudo_res <- AggregateExpression(adata_qc, 
                                  group.by = c("clusters", "sample", "les"), 
                                  assays = "RNA", 
                                  return.seurat = FALSE)

# This creates the 'mat' object your loop is looking for
mat <- pseudo_res$RNA
# -----------------------------------
# -- some samples are from the same patients, so account for this ---
library(DESeq2)
library(tidyverse)

# 1. Get the list of all clusters
all_clusters <- unique(adata_qc$clusters)


# 2. Start the Loop
for (cluster in all_clusters) {
  
  message("Processing cluster: ", cluster)
  
  # Identify columns for this specific cluster
  # We use the matrix 'mat' we created from AggregateExpression
  cluster_cols <- grep(paste0("^", cluster, "_"), colnames(mat), value = TRUE)
  
  # Skip if the cluster doesn't have enough samples (needs at least 3)
  if (length(cluster_cols) < 3) {
    message("Skipping ", cluster, ": Not enough samples.")
    next
  }
  
  # Subset matrix and create metadata for THIS cluster
  cluster_mat <- mat[, cluster_cols]
  
  # inside your loop...

  # 1. Simplify the metadata (Keep all samples, just drop the 'Subject' grouping)
  cluster_meta <- data.frame(ColName = cluster_cols) %>%
    separate(ColName, into = c("Cluster", "Sample", "Status"), sep = "_", extra = "merge") %>%
    mutate(Status = gsub("-", "", Status)) # Change "non-les" to "nonles"
  
  rownames(cluster_meta) <- cluster_meta$ColName
  cluster_meta$Status <- factor(cluster_meta$Status, levels = c("nonles", "les"))

  # 2. Use the simplified design
  dds <- DESeqDataSetFromMatrix(countData = cluster_mat,
                                colData = cluster_meta,
                                design = ~ Status)
  
  # 3. Run and save
  dds <- DESeq(dds, quiet = TRUE)
  res <- results(dds, contrast = c("Status", "les", "nonles"))
  
  # Save to CSV
  clean_name <- gsub(" ", "_", cluster)
  write.csv(as.data.frame(res), paste0("~/Desktop/Research_Project/Scripts/DEGs/DEG_Results_", clean_name, ".csv"))
}


```

### Visualisations

```{r}
master_table <- master_table %>%
  mutate(SA_status = case_when(
    sa_abundance > 35 ~ "High",
    sa_abundance < 10 ~ "Low",
    TRUE ~ NA_character_  # Samples in the 10-35% range become NA
  ))
load("~/Desktop/iawards/metadata/seq_metadata_filt.RData")

target_ids <- c("A17", "A18", "A21", "A22", "A37", "A38")

bridge_clean <- as.data.frame(seq_metadata_filt) %>%
  mutate(scRNA_ID = regmatches(as.character(seq_id), regexpr("A[0-9]+", as.character(seq_id)))) %>%
  filter(scRNA_ID %in% target_ids) %>%
  dplyr::select(scRNA_ID, eg_id) %>%
  distinct()


#------- VOLCANO PLOTS----------------
library(ggplot2)
library(EnhancedVolcano) 
#BiocManager::install('EnhancedVolcano')

# 1. Define the folder where your CSVs are and where you want to save plots
input_dir <- "~/Desktop/Research_Project/Scripts/DEGs/"
output_dir <- "~/Desktop/Research_Project/Scripts/DEGs/Volcano_Plots/"

# Create the output folder if it doesn't exist
if (!dir.exists(output_dir)) dir.create(output_dir)

# 2. Get a list of all your DEG result files
deg_files <- list.files(input_dir, pattern = "DEG_Results_.*\\.csv", full.names = TRUE)

cb_palette <- c('grey30', 'lightblue', 'royalblue','#e41a1c' )

# 3. Start the Loop
for (file in deg_files) {
  
  # Extract the cluster name from the filename for the title
  cluster_name <- gsub(".*DEG_Results_|.csv", "", basename(file))
  message("Plotting: ", cluster_name)
  
  # Read the data
  res_df <- read.csv(file, row.names = 1)
  
  # Skip if the file is empty or has too few rows to plot
  if (nrow(res_df) < 10) next
  
  # 4. Create the Volcano Plot
  # We use 'p' to store it so we can save it specifically
  p <- EnhancedVolcano(res_df,
                       lab = rownames(res_df),
                       x = 'log2FoldChange',
                       y = 'padj',
                       title = paste("Lesional vs Non-Lesional:", cluster_name),
                       subtitle = "Differential Expression Analysis",
                       xlim=c(-5,5),
                       ylim=c(0,5),
                       pCutoff = 0.05,
                       FCcutoff = 1.0,
                       col = cb_palette,
                       pointSize = 2.0,
                       labSize = 4.0,
                       legendLabels=c('Not sig.','Log2 FC','p-value', 'p-value & Log2 FC'),
                       legendPosition = 'right')
  
  # 5. Save the plot as a high-res PNG
  file_name <- paste0(output_dir, "Volcano_", cluster_name, ".png")
  
  ggsave(filename = file_name, 
         plot = p, 
         width = 10, 
         height = 8, 
         units = "in", 
         dpi = 300)
}

# -------- DOT PLOTS-----------
# 1. Normalize the data (LogNormalization is standard)
# This creates the 'data' layer that DotPlot is looking for
adata_qc <- NormalizeData(adata_qc, normalization.method = "LogNormalize", scale.factor = 10000)

gene_sets <- list(
  "Antimicrobial Genes" = c("SLPI", "S100A8", "S100A9", "DEFB4A", "CAMP"),
  "Barrier_Function Genes" = c("FLG", "LOR", "KRT1", "KRT10", "LCE3E"),
  "Inflammation Genes" = c("CCL19", "CCL4", "IL1B", "CXCL8", "TNF"),
  "Antigen_Presentation Genes" = c("CD1A", "CD1C", "HLA-DRA", "CD74")
)

# 1. Combine all your genes of interest into one small list
genes_to_plot <- unique(unlist(gene_sets))

# 2. Scale ONLY those genes
# This takes seconds and won't crash your RAM
adata_qc <- ScaleData(adata_qc, features = genes_to_plot)

# 3. Now run your DotPlot loop!
# (The loop code remains exactly the same as before)

# 1. Define Gene Sets relevant to S. aureus and Atopic Dermatitis


# 2. Create an output folder
plot_dir <- "~/Desktop/Research_Project/Scripts/DEGs/DotPlots/"
if (!dir.exists(plot_dir)) dir.create(plot_dir)

# 1. Create a combined identity 
# This creates groups like "OB_les", "OB_non-les", etc.
adata_qc$cluster_condition <- paste0(adata_qc$clusters, "_", adata_qc$les)

for (set_name in names(gene_sets)) {
```
