```{r}
  valid_genes <- intersect(gene_sets[[set_name]], rownames(adata_qc))
  if(length(valid_genes) == 0) next
  
  # 1. Start the plot and chain the layers correctly
  p <- DotPlot(adata_qc, 
               features = valid_genes, 
               group.by = "cluster_condition", 
               cols = c("blue", "red"), 
               scale = TRUE, 
               col.min = -1.5, 
               col.max = 1.5) + 
    RotatedAxis() +
    theme_bw() + 
    theme(
      # Bumping up the Gene Names (X-axis) - Bold and Large
      axis.text.x = element_text(size = 12, face = "bold", vjust = 1, hjust = 1), 
      
      # Bumping up the Cluster Names (Y-axis) - Bold and Large
      axis.text.y = element_text(size = 11, face = "bold"),
      
      # Making the Legend text larger
      legend.text = element_text(size = 10),
      legend.title = element_text(size = 12, face = "bold"),
      
      # Centering and enlarging the Title
      plot.title = element_text(size = 16, face = "bold", hjust = 0.5),
      
      # Keeping it clean
      panel.grid.major = element_line(color = "grey95"),
      panel.grid.minor = element_blank()
    ) +
    # This ensures the legend and titles are clear
    labs(title = paste("Expression Pattern:", set_name),
         color = "Relative Exp.",
         size = "% Expressed",
         x = "Marker Genes",
         y = "Cell Type & Condition")

  # 2. Save with a slightly wider aspect ratio to accommodate the larger font
  ggsave(filename = paste0(plot_dir, "Final_HD_DotPlot_", set_name, ".png"),
         plot = p, width = 12, height = 10, dpi = 300)
  
  message("Successfully saved: ", set_name)
}







# ----- Gene Correlation with SA-------------

library(reshape2)
library(dplyr)
library(ggplot2)

# 1. Setup Directories
main_dir <- "~/Desktop/Research_Project/Scripts/DEGs/Correlation_Results/"
if (!dir.exists(main_dir)) dir.create(main_dir, recursive = TRUE)

# 2. Define the exact clusters of interest
power_clusters <- c("IFE C", "OB", "KER1", "IFE B", "T CELL")

# 3. Create the Mapping Data Frame from master_table
# We still use relationship = "many-to-many" because one eg_id (Patient) 
# has multiple microbiome samples in master_table, but we've restricted 
# the 'y' side to only your 6 target single-cell samples.
s_aureus_map <- master_table %>%
  mutate(eg_id = as.character(eg_id)) %>%
  left_join(bridge_clean, by = "eg_id", relationship = "many-to-many") %>%
  dplyr::select(sample = scRNA_ID, 
         s_aureus = sa_abundance, 
         SA_status = SA_status) %>%
  filter(!is.na(sample)) %>% 
  distinct()

# 4. Main Correlation Loop
for (cluster in power_clusters) {
  
  message("--- Processing Cluster: ", cluster, " ---")
  
  clean_cluster <- gsub(" ", "_", cluster)
  cluster_dir <- paste0(main_dir, clean_cluster, "/")
  if (!dir.exists(cluster_dir)) dir.create(cluster_dir)
  
  # Load DEG results
  file_path <- paste0("~/Desktop/Research_Project/Scripts/DEGs/DEG_Results_", clean_cluster, ".csv")
  if (!file.exists(file_path)) {
    message("File not found: ", file_path)
    next
  }
  
  res_df <- read.csv(file_path, row.names = 1)
  top_5_genes <- head(rownames(res_df[order(res_df$padj), ]), 5)
  
  # Find columns in pseudobulk matrix 'mat'
  cluster_cols <- grep(paste0("^", cluster, "_"), colnames(mat), value = TRUE)
  
  if (length(cluster_cols) == 0) {
    message("No columns found in 'mat' for: ", cluster)
    next
  }

  for (gene in top_5_genes) {
    if (!gene %in% rownames(mat)) next
    
    sample_expr <- mat[gene, cluster_cols]
    
    # Skip genes with no variation (prevents math errors)
    if (sum(sample_expr) == 0 || sd(sample_expr) == 0) next

    # Build plotting data
    plot_data <- data.frame(
      Expression = as.numeric(sample_expr),
      Full_Name = names(sample_expr)
    )
    
    # Extract ID and map metadata
    plot_data$Sample_ID <- regmatches(plot_data$Full_Name, regexpr("A[0-9]+", plot_data$Full_Name))
    plot_data$S_aureus  <- s_aureus_map$s_aureus[match(plot_data$Sample_ID, s_aureus_map$sample)]
    plot_data$SA_Group  <- s_aureus_map$SA_status[match(plot_data$Sample_ID, s_aureus_map$sample)]
    plot_data$Status    <- ifelse(grepl("non-les", plot_data$Full_Name), "Non-Lesional", "Lesional")
    
    # CRITICAL: Skip if mapping failed to prevent "non-finite" viewport error
    if (all(is.na(plot_data$S_aureus))) {
      message("Skipping ", gene, ": No matching S. aureus data for these IDs.")
      next
    }

    r_val <- round(cor(plot_data$S_aureus, plot_data$Expression, method="spearman", use="complete.obs"), 2)
    
    # Create Plot - Color-coded by your strict High/Low Status
    p <- ggplot(plot_data, aes(x = S_aureus, y = Expression)) +
      geom_smooth(method = "lm", color = "black", se = FALSE, linetype = "dashed", alpha = 0.5) +
      geom_point(aes(color = SA_Group, shape = Status), size = 6) +
      annotate("label", x = -Inf, y = Inf, label = paste("Spearman r =", r_val), 
               hjust = -0.1, vjust = 1.5, size = 5, fontface = "bold", fill = "white") +
      scale_color_manual(values = c("High" = "#D55E00", "Low" = "#56B4E9"), na.value = "grey70") +
      theme_bw() +
      theme(panel.grid = element_blank()) +
      labs(title = paste(cluster, "| Gene:", gene),
           subtitle = "S. Aureus Thresholds: High >35%, Low <10%",
           x = "S. aureus Abundance (%)", 
           y = "Log-Normalised Expression",
           color = "SA Status")

    ggsave(filename = paste0(cluster_dir, "Correlation_", gene, ".png"), 
           plot = p, width = 7, height = 5, dpi = 300)
  }
}









# 1. Create a clean metadata dataframe to add
# This links the 'orig.ident' in Seurat to the 'SA_status' in your map
metadata_to_add <- s_aureus_map %>%
  dplyr::select(sample, SA_status) %>%
  distinct()

# 2. Match the rows correctly to the Seurat object
# We pull the 'orig.ident' from the Seurat metadata and match it to our map
new_metadata_column <- metadata_to_add$SA_status[match(adata_qc$orig.ident, metadata_to_add$sample)]

# 3. Add it into the Seurat object
adata_qc$SA_status <- new_metadata_column

# 4. Verification: You should see 'High', 'Low', and 'NA' here
table(adata_qc$SA_status, useNA = "always")


# 1. Define your top correlated genes
correlation_stars <- c("LINC01128", "AL627309.5", "AL627309.1", "SLPI")

# 2. Filter your Seurat object to only include High/Low samples
# This ensures the Dot Plot matches the 'extremes' logic of your correlation plots
adata_subset <- subset(adata_qc, subset = SA_status %in% c("High", "Low"))

# 1. Ensure the labels are descriptive
# This combines the Cluster and the SA status for the X-axis
adata_subset$plot_group <- paste0(adata_subset$clusters, " (", adata_subset$SA_status, ")")

# 2. Generate the plot
p <- DotPlot(adata_subset, 
             features = correlation_stars, 
             group.by = "plot_group", 
             cols = c("lightgrey", "#D55E00")) + # Using a gradient toward your High-SA Orange
  coord_flip() +
  theme_bw() +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 90, vjust = 0.5, hjust = 1, size = 10)) +
  labs(title = "S. aureus Dose-Response & Lesionality",
       subtitle = "Comparing High (>35%) vs Low (<10%) SA across all clusters",
       x = "Candidate Genes",
       y = "Cluster & SA Status",
       color = "Expression Intensity")

# 3. Add a manual legend for the Status Colors (High/Low)
# Since Seurat can be tricky with legends, we'll annotate the plot directly
print(p)


# 1. Force a clean 'status' column
# We check for common ways "Lesional" is stored (1, "1", or "Lesional")
adata_subset$detailed_status <- "Other" # Default

adata_subset$detailed_status[adata_subset$les %in% c(1, "1", "Lesional")] <- 
  paste0("Lesional | ", adata_subset$SA_status[adata_subset$les %in% c(1, "1", "Lesional")])

adata_subset$detailed_status[adata_subset$les %in% c(0, "0", "Non-Lesional")] <- 
  paste0("Non-Lesional | ", adata_subset$SA_status[adata_subset$les %in% c(0, "0", "Non-Lesional")])

# 2. Check the table - YOU SHOULD SEE 4 GROUPS HERE
print(table(adata_subset$detailed_status))

# 3. Define the full color palette
full_colors <- c(
  "Non-Lesional | Low"  = "#56B4E9", # Light Blue
  "Non-Lesional | High" = "#0072B2", # Dark Blue
  "Lesional | Low"      = "#009E73", # Green
  "Lesional | High"     = "#D55E00"  # Orange
)

# 4. Re-plot
VlnPlot(adata_subset, 
        features = c("SLPI", "LINC01128"), 
        group.by = "clusters", 
        split.by = "detailed_status", 
        pt.size = 0,
        cols = full_colors) + 
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1)) +
  labs(title = "Clinical State vs. Bacterial Load",
       fill = "State & SA Load")



# 1. Map based on the exact strings found: "les" and "non_les"
adata_subset$detailed_status <- "Unknown" 

# Map Lesional cells
is_lesional <- adata_subset$les == "les"
adata_subset$detailed_status[is_lesional] <- paste0("Lesional | ", adata_subset$SA_status[is_lesional])

# Map Non-Lesional cells
is_non_lesional <- adata_subset$les == "non_les"
adata_subset$detailed_status[is_non_lesional] <- paste0("Non-Lesional | ", adata_subset$SA_status[is_non_lesional])

# 2. Check the table - You should now see the 4 distinct groups!
print(table(adata_subset$detailed_status))

# 3. Final Plotting
full_colors <- c(
  "Non-Lesional | Low"  = "#56B4E9", # Light Blue
  "Non-Lesional | High" = "#0072B2", # Dark Blue
  "Lesional | Low"      = "#009E73", # Green
  "Lesional | High"     = "#D55E00"  # Orange
)

VlnPlot(adata_subset, 
        features = c("SLPI", "LINC01128"), 
        group.by = "clusters", 
        split.by = "detailed_status", 
        pt.size = 0,
        cols = full_colors) + 
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1))



library(dplyr)
library(ggplot2)

# 1. Prepare the data: Average expression per cluster per status
plot_data <- FetchData(adata_subset, vars = c("SLPI", "LINC01128", "clusters", "detailed_status")) %>%
  group_by(clusters, detailed_status) %>%
  summarise(
    mean_SLPI = mean(SLPI),
    mean_LINC = mean(LINC01128),
    .groups = 'drop'
  )

pdf("~/Desktop/Research_Project/Scripts/DEGs/Correlation_Results/Master/SLPI.pdf", width = 10, height = 7)

# 2. Create the Bar Chart for SLPI (The Defense)
ggplot(plot_data, aes(x = clusters, y = mean_SLPI, fill = detailed_status)) +
  geom_bar(stat = "identity", position = "dodge") +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c(
    "Non-Lesional | Low"  = "#56B4E9", 
    "Non-Lesional | High" = "#0072B2", 
    "Lesional | Low"      = "#009E73", 
    "Lesional | High"     = "#D55E00"
  )) +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1)) +
  labs(title = "Average SLPI Expression",
       y = "Mean Log-Normalised Expression",
       fill = "State & SA Load")

dev.off()

pdf("~/Desktop/Research_Project/Scripts/DEGs/Correlation_Results/Master/LINC.pdf", width = 10, height = 7)
# Create the Bar Chart for LINC
ggplot(plot_data, aes(x = clusters, y = mean_LINC, fill = detailed_status)) +
  geom_bar(stat = "identity", position = "dodge") +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c(
    "Non-Lesional | Low"  = "#56B4E9", 
    "Non-Lesional | High" = "#0072B2", 
    "Lesional | Low"      = "#009E73", 
    "Lesional | High"     = "#D55E00"
  )) +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1)) +
  labs(title = "Average LINC01128 Expression",
       y = "Mean Log-Normalised Expression",
       fill = "State & SA Load")
dev.off()




# First check what layers exist in your object
print(adata_qc[["RNA"]])

# Join layers first (Seurat v5 requirement)
adata_qc[["RNA"]] <- JoinLayers(adata_qc[["RNA"]])

# Now try the export again
mean_expr_per_celltype <- AverageExpression(
  adata_qc, 
  group.by = "clusters",
  assays = "RNA",
  slot = "data"
)$RNA

cat("Shape:", nrow(mean_expr_per_celltype), "genes x", 
    ncol(mean_expr_per_celltype), "cell types\n")
cat("Cell types:", colnames(mean_expr_per_celltype), "\n")

write.csv(mean_expr_per_celltype,
          "~/Desktop/Research_Project/Scripts/Integration/mean_expr_per_celltype.csv",
          row.names = TRUE)
# Mean expression per cell type split by SA status
# First add SA_status to the seurat object via the bridge
adata_qc$SA_status <- s_aureus_map$SA_status[
  match(adata_qc$orig.ident, s_aureus_map$sample)]

# Check the mapping worked
print(table(adata_qc$SA_status, useNA = "always"))

# Mean expression per cell type per SA group
mean_expr_high_sa <- AverageExpression(
  subset(adata_qc, subset = SA_status == "High"),
  group.by = "clusters",
  assays = "RNA",
  slot = "data"
)$RNA

mean_expr_low_sa <- AverageExpression(
  subset(adata_qc, subset = SA_status == "Low"),
  group.by = "clusters",
  assays = "RNA",
  slot = "data"
)$RNA

write.csv(mean_expr_high_sa,
          "~/Desktop/Research_Project/Scripts/Integration/mean_expr_high_sa.csv",
          row.names = TRUE)

write.csv(mean_expr_low_sa,
          "~/Desktop/Research_Project/Scripts/Integration/mean_expr_low_sa.csv",
          row.names = TRUE)

cat("High SA expression matrix:", nrow(mean_expr_high_sa), 
    "genes x", ncol(mean_expr_high_sa), "cell types\n")
cat("Low SA expression matrix:", nrow(mean_expr_low_sa), 
    "genes x", ncol(mean_expr_low_sa), "cell types\n")
```

```{r}
expr_matrix <- GetAssayData(adata_qc, layer = "data")

ct_col <- "clusters"  # corrected
sa_col <- "SA_status" # already correct

cat("Cell types:", unique(adata_qc@meta.data[[ct_col]]), "\n")
cat("SA_status:", table(adata_qc@meta.data[[sa_col]], useNA = "ifany"), "\n\n")

cell_types <- adata_qc@meta.data[[ct_col]]
sa_status  <- adata_qc@meta.data[[sa_col]]

# --- All samples ---
mean_expr_per_celltype <- do.call(cbind, lapply(unique(cell_types), function(ct) {
  cells <- colnames(adata_qc)[cell_types == ct]
  rowMeans(expr_matrix[, cells, drop = FALSE])
}))
colnames(mean_expr_per_celltype) <- unique(cell_types)
cat("All samples range:", round(min(mean_expr_per_celltype), 3),
    "to", round(max(mean_expr_per_celltype), 3), "\n")

# --- High SA ---
high_idx <- !is.na(sa_status) & sa_status == "High"
cat("High SA cells:", sum(high_idx), "\n")
high_types <- cell_types[high_idx]
mean_expr_high_sa <- do.call(cbind, lapply(unique(high_types), function(ct) {
  cells <- colnames(adata_qc)[high_idx & cell_types == ct]
  rowMeans(expr_matrix[, cells, drop = FALSE])
}))
colnames(mean_expr_high_sa) <- unique(high_types)
cat("High SA range:", round(min(mean_expr_high_sa), 3),
    "to", round(max(mean_expr_high_sa), 3), "\n")

# --- Low SA ---
low_idx <- !is.na(sa_status) & sa_status == "Low"
cat("Low SA cells:", sum(low_idx), "\n")
low_types <- cell_types[low_idx]
mean_expr_low_sa <- do.call(cbind, lapply(unique(low_types), function(ct) {
  cells <- colnames(adata_qc)[low_idx & cell_types == ct]
  rowMeans(expr_matrix[, cells, drop = FALSE])
}))
colnames(mean_expr_low_sa) <- unique(low_types)
cat("Low SA range:", round(min(mean_expr_low_sa), 3),
    "to", round(max(mean_expr_low_sa), 3), "\n\n")

# --- Save ---
out_dir <- "~/Desktop/Research_Project/Scripts/Integration/GEM/pyglpk/Data"

write.csv(mean_expr_per_celltype, file.path(out_dir, "mean_expr_per_celltype.csv"))
write.csv(mean_expr_high_sa,      file.path(out_dir, "mean_expr_high_sa.csv"))
write.csv(mean_expr_low_sa,       file.path(out_dir, "mean_expr_low_sa.csv"))

cat("Saved. File sizes:\n")
for (f in c("mean_expr_per_celltype.csv", "mean_expr_high_sa.csv", "mean_expr_low_sa.csv")) {
  fp <- file.path(out_dir, f)
  cat(sprintf("  %s  %.1f MB\n", f, file.size(fp) / 1e6))
}
```

### Hypergeometric Tests

Validating my findings using published dataset (Patrick et. al. (2023)) to see if the link between SA abundance and certain genes is significant, this helps account for the low n.

|  |  |  |
|------------------------|------------------------|------------------------|
| **Object Name** | **What it is** | **Use it for...** |
| **`mat`** | Pseudobulk count matrix | Calculations and Loops. |
| **`s_aureus_map`** | SA Abundance % per Patient | Mapping bacteria to expression. |
| **`plot_data`** | A temporary dataframe | Creating the bar/violin plots. |
| **`sa_correlations_master`** | The new object | **Hypergeometric Tests.** |

```{r}
# ============================================================
# PART 1: PREPARE REFERENCE DATA & MAPPING
# ============================================================
library(org.Hs.eg.db)
library(dplyr)
library(tibble)

# 1. Load your AD reference data (skipping the 2 header rows)
# Adjust the filename as needed
ref_genes <- read_excel("~/Desktop/Research_Project/Scripts/DEGs/Hypergeometric_Tests/AD_Reference_DEGs.xlsx", skip = 2) 

# 2. Map Ensembl IDs to Symbols locally (Faster than biomaRt)
sa_ensembl_ids <- c("ENSG00000229807", "ENSG00000230002", "ENSG00000237955")

bm_map <- AnnotationDbi::select(
  org.Hs.eg.db,
  keys    = sa_ensembl_ids,
  columns = c("ENSEMBL", "SYMBOL", "GENETYPE"),
  keytype = "ENSEMBL"
)

# Rename columns to standard format
colnames(bm_map) <- c("ensembl_gene_id", "external_gene_name", "gene_biotype")

# ============================================================
# PART 2: THE HYPERGEOMETRIC TEST (UNIVERSE CORRECTION)
# ============================================================

# 1. Define the Shared Universe
# CRITICAL: We only test genes that were measured in BOTH datasets
all_your_genes <- rownames(mat)
all_ref_genes  <- ref_genes$Gene
shared_universe <- intersect(all_your_genes, all_ref_genes)
N_universe      <- length(shared_universe)

cat("Universe size (shared genes):", N_universe, "\n")

# 2. Identify your Top Hits (Filtered by Shared Universe)
my_top_pos_symbols <- sa_correlations_master %>% 
  arrange(desc(Spearman_r)) %>% 
  head(500) %>% 
  pull(Gene) %>%
  intersect(shared_universe)

my_top_neg_symbols <- sa_correlations_master %>% 
  arrange(Spearman_r) %>% 
  head(500) %>% 
  pull(Gene) %>%
  intersect(shared_universe)

# 3. Identify Reference AD Hits (Filtered by Shared Universe)
ref_up_symbols <- ref_genes %>% 
  filter(adj.P.Val < 0.05 & logFC > 0) %>% 
  pull(Gene) %>%
  intersect(shared_universe)

ref_down_symbols <- ref_genes %>% 
  filter(adj.P.Val < 0.05 & logFC < 0) %>% 
  pull(Gene) %>%
  intersect(shared_universe)

# 4. Calculate Overlaps
overlap_pos <- length(intersect(my_top_pos_symbols, ref_up_symbols))
overlap_neg <- length(intersect(my_top_neg_symbols, ref_down_symbols))

# 5. Run the Hypergeometric Tests (phyper)
p_val_pos <- phyper(
  overlap_pos - 1, 
  length(ref_up_symbols), 
  N_universe - length(ref_up_symbols), 
  length(my_top_pos_symbols), 
  lower.tail = FALSE
)

p_val_neg <- phyper(
  overlap_neg - 1, 
  length(ref_down_symbols), 
  N_universe - length(ref_down_symbols), 
  length(my_top_neg_symbols), 
  lower.tail = FALSE
)

# ============================================================
# PART 3: TARGETED LINC/XIST SEARCH
# ============================================================

# Search for the hit across its many aliases (Atopic Dermatitis context)
target_aliases <- c("LINC01128", "XIST", "ENSG00000229807", "LOC100294145", "AL627309")

search_results <- ref_genes %>% 
  filter(Gene %in% target_aliases | grepl("AL627309", Gene, ignore.case = TRUE))

# ============================================================
# PART 4: PRINT RESULTS
# ============================================================
cat("\n=== ATOPIC DERMATITIS VALIDATION RESULTS ===")
cat("\nUniverse (Shared Genes):", N_universe)

cat("\n\n--- POSITIVE CORRELATION (Inflammatory Signal) ---")
cat("\nOverlap with Reference Up-regulated:", overlap_pos)
cat("\nP-value:", p_val_pos)

cat("\n\n--- NEGATIVE CORRELATION (Barrier Collapse) ---")
cat("\nOverlap with Reference Down-regulated:", overlap_neg)
cat("\nP-value:", p_val_neg)

cat("\n\n--- TARGETED SEARCH FOR LINC01128/XIST ---")
if(nrow(search_results) > 0) {
  print(search_results)
} else {
  cat("\nNo specific matches found for the LINC01128/XIST aliases in the reference list.")
}
```

Make two groups, the whole cleaned single cell dataset (20,877 cells) and the epidermis-only subset (8,193 cells, only IFE and Kertinocytes). This is what Helen did in previous analysis. The full set will be used to study SA interactions with T-cells and the overall skin microenvironment and the subset will be used to study Filaggrin (FLG) expression, differentation defects and how the skin structure breaks down during an eczema flare.

```{r}
# 1. Define the Epidermal barrier clusters
# (Excluding T-cells, B-cells, Melanocytes, Fibroblasts, and Follicles)
epidermis_barrier <- c("IFE B", "IFE SB", "KER1", "KER2")

# 2. Subset the data
adata_barrier <- subset(adata_qc, subset = clusters %in% epidermis_barrier)

# 3. Check the split (Target: ~2,940 lesional / ~5,253 non-lesional)
table(adata_barrier$les)
```

## Step 8: Cell Type Annotation

SingleR annotation of the scRNA-seq data to confirm which cell types are present and check cell number per sample per cell type to see which are sufficiently abundant for downstream analysis.

```{r}
Clusters <- read.csv("~/Desktop/iawards/single cell/abu/abu onedrive contents/Bioinformatics/scRNAseq/r_clusters.csv")

grep("res", colnames(Clusters@meta.data), value = TRUE)
head(Clusters)
```

## Step 9: DEG for Lesional vs Non-Lesional

```{r}
library(magrittr)

# Ensure 'les' and 'sample' are factors
Clusters$les <- factor(Clusters$les, levels = c("non-les", "les"))
Clusters$sample <- as.factor(Clusters$sample)

# Double check cluster names
print(unique(Clusters$clusters))

#------Pseudobulk to find differentially expressed genes------
install.packages("DESeq2")
library(DESeq2)
library(tidyverse)

# 1. Aggregate: creates columns like "KER1_A17" and "KER1_A18"
# must include 'les' in the grouping so we don't merge Les and Non-Les
pseudo_counts <- AggregateExpression(Clusters, 
                                     group.by = c("clusters", "sample", "les"), 
                                     assays = "RNA", 
                                     return.seurat = FALSE)

mat <- pseudo_counts$RNA

# 2. Extract a specific cluster (e.g., IFE SB)
# ---------------------------------------------------------
target_cluster <- "IFE SB"
# Logic: Find columns starting with the cluster name
cluster_cols <- grep(paste0("^", target_cluster, "_"), colnames(mat), value = TRUE)
cluster_mat <- mat[, cluster_cols]

# 3. Create the Paired Metadata Frame
# ---------------------------------------------------------
# We need to split the column names (e.g., "IFE SB_A17_les") into parts
meta_df <- data.frame(ColName = colnames(cluster_mat)) %>%
  separate(ColName, into = c("Cluster", "Patient", "Status"), sep = "_")

# Ensure Status is a factor with 'non-les' as the reference
meta_df$Status <- factor(meta_df$Status, levels = c("non-les", "les"))
meta_df$Patient <- as.factor(meta_df$Patient)
rownames(meta_df) <- colnames(cluster_mat)

# 4. Run the Paired DESeq2
# ---------------------------------------------------------
dds <- DESeqDataSetFromMatrix(countData = cluster_mat,
                              colData = meta_df,
                              design = ~ Patient + Status)

# Standard DESeq2 Pipeline
dds <- DESeq(dds)
res <- results(dds, contrast = c("Status", "les", "non-les"))

# Apply 'LFC Shrinkage' - highly recommended for small n to 
# reduce noise in genes with low counts
res_shrunk <- lfcShrink(dds, coef = "Status_les_vs_non.les", type = "apeglm")

# Filter for the "Success" list
sig_genes <- as.data.frame(res_shrunk) %>%
  filter(padj < 0.05 & abs(log2FoldChange) > 0.5) %>%
  arrange(padj)

print(head(sig_genes))
```

### 9.1. DEGs for SA high/low

```{r}
## Step 9b: DEG for SA High vs SA Low

# ============================================================
# PART 1: ADD SA_STATUS TO THE CLUSTERS SEURAT OBJECT
# ============================================================

# 1. Build the bridge: scRNA sample ID -> SA_status
#    (re-uses bridge_clean and master_table already in environment)
# Add SA_status to the actual Seurat object (adata_qc), not the metadata CSV
sa_status_map <- master_table %>%
  mutate(eg_id = as.character(eg_id)) %>%
  left_join(bridge_clean, by = "eg_id", relationship = "many-to-many") %>%
  dplyr::select(sample = scRNA_ID, SA_status) %>%
  filter(!is.na(sample)) %>%
  distinct()

# Map SA_status onto adata_qc using orig.ident (same as you did at line ~3569)
adata_qc$SA_status <- sa_status_map$SA_status[match(adata_qc$orig.ident, sa_status_map$sample)]

# Verify
table(adata_qc$SA_status, useNA = "always")

# Subset the SEURAT OBJECT to High/Low only
adata_sa <- subset(adata_qc, subset = SA_status %in% c("High", "Low"))
adata_sa$SA_status <- factor(adata_sa$SA_status, levels = c("Low", "High"))

# Now AggregateExpression on the Seurat object
pseudo_counts_sa <- AggregateExpression(adata_sa,
                                        group.by = c("clusters", "orig.ident", "SA_status"),
                                        assays = "RNA",
                                        return.seurat = FALSE)

mat_sa <- pseudo_counts_sa$RNA
# ============================================================
# PART 3: DESeq2 LOOP - ONE MODEL PER CLUSTER
# ============================================================

all_clusters_sa <- unique(Clusters_sa$clusters)

output_dir_sa <- "~/Desktop/Research_Project/Scripts/DEGs/SA_HighvsLow/"
if (!dir.exists(output_dir_sa)) dir.create(output_dir_sa, recursive = TRUE)

for (cluster in all_clusters_sa) {
  
  message("Processing cluster: ", cluster)
  
  cluster_cols <- grep(paste0("^", cluster, "_"), colnames(mat_sa), value = TRUE)
  
  if (length(cluster_cols) < 3) {
    message("Skipping ", cluster, ": Not enough samples.")
    next
  }
  
  cluster_mat <- mat_sa[, cluster_cols]
  
  cluster_meta <- data.frame(ColName = cluster_cols) %>%
    separate(ColName,
             into = c("Cluster", "Patient", "SA_group"),
             sep  = "_",
             extra = "merge") %>%
    mutate(SA_group = factor(SA_group, levels = c("Low", "High")),
           Patient  = as.factor(Patient))
  
  rownames(cluster_meta) <- cluster_cols
  
  if (length(unique(cluster_meta$SA_group)) < 2) {
    message("Skipping ", cluster, ": Only one SA group present.")
    next
  }
  
  # Check if any patient appears in BOTH High and Low (required for paired design)
  paired_patients <- cluster_meta %>%
    group_by(Patient) %>%
    summarise(n_groups = n_distinct(SA_group)) %>%
    filter(n_groups > 1)
  
  design_formula <- if (nrow(paired_patients) >= 2) ~ Patient + SA_group else ~ SA_group
  message("Using design: ", deparse(design_formula))
  
  dds <- DESeqDataSetFromMatrix(countData = cluster_mat,
                                colData   = cluster_meta,
                                design    = design_formula)
  
  dds <- DESeq(dds, quiet = TRUE)
  res <- results(dds, contrast = c("SA_group", "High", "Low"))
  
  clean_name <- gsub(" ", "_", cluster)
  write.csv(as.data.frame(res),
            paste0(output_dir_sa, "DEG_SA_Results_", clean_name, ".csv"))
  
  message("Saved: ", clean_name)
}
# ============================================================
# PART 4: VOLCANO PLOT LOOP - SA HIGH vs LOW
# ============================================================
library(ggplot2)
library(ggrepel)

volcano_dir_sa <- "~/Desktop/Research_Project/Scripts/DEGs/SA_HighvsLow/Volcano_Plots/"
if (!dir.exists(volcano_dir_sa)) dir.create(volcano_dir_sa, recursive = TRUE)

deg_files_sa <- list.files(output_dir_sa,
                            pattern = "DEG_SA_Results_.*\\.csv",
                            full.names = TRUE)

for (file in deg_files_sa) {
  
  cluster_name <- gsub(".*DEG_SA_Results_|\\.csv", "", basename(file))
  message("Plotting: ", cluster_name)
  
  res_df <- read.csv(file, row.names = 1) %>%
    filter(!is.na(padj), !is.na(log2FoldChange)) %>%
    mutate(
      gene = rownames(.),
      sig  = case_when(
        padj < 0.05 & log2FoldChange >  1 ~ "Upregulated in SA High",
        padj < 0.05 & log2FoldChange < -1 ~ "Upregulated in SA Low",
        TRUE                               ~ "Not significant"
      ),
      label = ifelse(padj < 0.05 & abs(log2FoldChange) > 1, gene, NA)
    )
  
  if (nrow(res_df) < 10) next
  
  p <- ggplot(res_df, aes(x = log2FoldChange, y = -log10(padj))) +
    geom_point(aes(colour = sig), size = 1.5, alpha = 0.7) +
    geom_text_repel(
      aes(label = label),
      size          = 5,
      max.overlaps  = Inf,       # never drop labels
      box.padding   = 0.5,       # space around each label box
      point.padding = 0.3,       # space between label and point
      segment.size  = 0.3,       # thickness of leader lines
      segment.alpha = 0.5,
      min.segment.length = 0.2,  # draw line even for close labels
      force         = 2,         # repulsion strength
      seed          = 42         # reproducible layout
    ) +
    geom_vline(xintercept = c(-1, 1), linetype = "dashed", colour = "grey40") +
    geom_hline(yintercept = -log10(0.05), linetype = "dashed", colour = "grey40") +
    scale_colour_manual(values = c(
      "Upregulated in SA High" = "#e41a1c",
      "Upregulated in SA Low"  = "royalblue",
      "Not significant"    = "grey60"
    )) +
    scale_x_continuous(limits = c(-5, 5)) +
    scale_y_continuous(limits = c(0, 5)) +
    labs(
      title    = paste("SA High vs SA Low:", gsub("_", " ", cluster_name)),
      subtitle = "Differential Expression Analysis (High >35 vs Low <10)",
      x        = "log2 Fold Change",
      y        = "-log10(adjusted p-value)",
      colour   = NULL
    ) +
    theme_bw() +
    theme(
      panel.grid       = element_blank(),
      legend.position  = "top",
      plot.title       = element_text(size = 18, face = "bold"),
      plot.subtitle    = element_text(size = 14),
      axis.title       = element_text(size = 16),
      axis.text        = element_text(size = 14),
      legend.text      = element_text(size = 14)
    )
  
  ggsave(filename = paste0(volcano_dir_sa, "Volcano_SA_", cluster_name, ".png"),
         plot   = p,
         width  = 10,
         height = 8,
         dpi    = 300)
}
```

```{r}
# -------- DOT PLOTS - SA HIGH vs LOW (Data-Driven DEGs) -----------

# Normalise and scale if not already done
adata_qc <- NormalizeData(adata_qc, normalization.method = "LogNormalize", scale.factor = 10000)

# Subset to High/Low only
adata_sa_dot <- subset(adata_qc, subset = SA_status %in% c("High", "Low"))
adata_sa_dot$cluster_SA <- paste0(adata_sa_dot$clusters, "_", adata_sa_dot$SA_status)

plot_dir_sa <- "~/Desktop/Research_Project/Scripts/DEGs/SA_HighvsLow/DotPlots/"
if (!dir.exists(plot_dir_sa)) dir.create(plot_dir_sa, recursive = TRUE)

# Loop over each cluster's DEG results file
deg_files_sa <- list.files(output_dir_sa,
                            pattern = "DEG_SA_Results_.*\\.csv",
                            full.names = TRUE)
# Collect ALL significant DEGs across all clusters first
all_sig_genes <- c()

for (file in deg_files_sa) {
  res_df <- read.csv(file, row.names = 1) %>%
    filter(!is.na(padj), !is.na(log2FoldChange),
           padj < 0.05, abs(log2FoldChange) > 1)
  all_sig_genes <- c(all_sig_genes, rownames(res_df))
}

# Deduplicate and keep only genes in the Seurat object
all_sig_genes <- intersect(unique(all_sig_genes), rownames(adata_sa_dot))

# Scale ALL of them once, before the loop
adata_sa_dot <- ScaleData(adata_sa_dot, features = all_sig_genes)

# Now run the dotplot loop WITHOUT ScaleData inside it
for (file in deg_files_sa) {
  
  cluster_name <- gsub(".*DEG_SA_Results_|\\.csv", "", basename(file))
  message("Plotting dotplot for: ", cluster_name)
  
  res_df <- read.csv(file, row.names = 1) %>%
    filter(!is.na(padj), !is.na(log2FoldChange),
           padj < 0.05, abs(log2FoldChange) > 1) %>%
    arrange(padj)
  
  if (nrow(res_df) == 0) {
    message("Skipping ", cluster_name, ": No significant DEGs.")
    next
  }
  
  sig_genes  <- head(rownames(res_df), 20)
  valid_genes <- intersect(sig_genes, rownames(adata_sa_dot))
  
  if (length(valid_genes) == 0) {
    message("Skipping ", cluster_name, ": No DEGs found in Seurat object.")
    next
  }
  
  p <- DotPlot(adata_sa_dot,
               features = valid_genes,
               group.by = "cluster_SA",
               cols     = c("blue", "red"),
               scale    = TRUE,
               col.min  = -1.5,
               col.max  =  1.5) +
    RotatedAxis() +
    theme_bw() +
    theme(
      axis.text.x      = element_text(size = 16, face = "bold", vjust = 1, hjust = 1),
      axis.text.y      = element_text(size = 16, face = "bold"),
      axis.title.x     = element_text(size = 18, face = "bold"),
      axis.title.y     = element_text(size = 18, face = "bold"),
      legend.text      = element_text(size = 14),
      legend.title     = element_text(size = 16, face = "bold"),
      plot.title       = element_text(size = 20, face = "bold", hjust = 0.5),
      plot.subtitle    = element_text(size = 16, hjust = 0.5),
      panel.grid.major = element_line(color = "grey95"),
      panel.grid.minor = element_blank()
    ) +
    labs(title    = paste("Significant DEGs:", gsub("_", " ", cluster_name)),
         subtitle = paste0("S. aureus High (>35) vs Low (<10) | n=", length(valid_genes), " genes"),
         colour   = "Relative Exp.",
         size     = "% Expressed",
         x        = "Significant DEGs",
         y        = "Cell Type & SA Status")
  
  ggsave(filename = paste0(plot_dir_sa, "DotPlot_SA_DEGs_", cluster_name, ".png"),
         plot = p, width = 14, height = 11, dpi = 300)
  
  message("Saved dotplot for: ", cluster_name)
}
```

### 9.2. GSEA

```{r}
library(fgsea)
library(msigdbr)

gsea_base_dir_sa <- "~/Desktop/Research_Project/Scripts/DEGs/GSEA_Results/SA_HighvsLow/"
dir.create(gsea_base_dir_sa, showWarnings = FALSE, recursive = TRUE)

h_df <- msigdbr(species = "Homo sapiens", collection = "H")
pathways_h <- split(x = h_df$gene_symbol, f = h_df$gs_name)

deg_files_sa <- list.files(output_dir_sa,
                            pattern = "DEG_SA_Results_.*\\.csv",
                            full.names = TRUE)

for (file in deg_files_sa) {
  
  cluster_name <- gsub(".*DEG_SA_Results_|\\.csv", "", basename(file))
  message("--- Running GSEA for: ", cluster_name, " ---")
  
  # Each cluster gets its own subfolder
  cluster_dir <- paste0(gsea_base_dir_sa, cluster_name, "/")
  dir.create(cluster_dir, showWarnings = FALSE)
  
  res_df <- read.csv(file, row.names = 1) %>%
    filter(!is.na(log2FoldChange), !is.na(padj))
  
  if (nrow(res_df) < 15) {
    message("Skipping ", cluster_name, ": Not enough genes.")
    next
  }
  
  gene_ranks <- res_df$log2FoldChange
  names(gene_ranks) <- rownames(res_df)
  set.seed(123)
  gene_ranks <- gene_ranks + rnorm(length(gene_ranks), mean = 0, sd = 1e-10)
  gene_ranks <- sort(gene_ranks, decreasing = TRUE)
  
  fgsea_res <- fgsea(pathways = pathways_h,
                     stats    = gene_ranks,
                     minSize  = 15,
                     maxSize  = 500)
  
  sig_pathways <- fgsea_res[fgsea_res$padj < 0.05, ]
  sig_pathways <- sig_pathways[order(sig_pathways$NES, decreasing = TRUE), ]
  
  if (nrow(sig_pathways) == 0) {
    message("No significant pathways for ", cluster_name)
    next
  }
  
  # --- Save CSV ---
  sig_pathways_save <- sig_pathways
  sig_pathways_save$leadingEdge <- sapply(sig_pathways_save$leadingEdge,
                                           function(x) paste(x, collapse = ", "))
  write.csv(as.data.frame(sig_pathways_save),
            paste0(cluster_dir, "GSEA_SA_Significant_Pathways_", cluster_name, ".csv"),
            row.names = FALSE)
  
  # --- Top pathway enriched in SA High ---
  top_high <- sig_pathways[sig_pathways$NES > 0, ]
  if (nrow(top_high) > 0) {
    top_high <- top_high[order(top_high$NES, decreasing = TRUE), ]
    p_high <- plotEnrichment(pathways_h[[top_high$pathway[1]]], gene_ranks) +
      labs(title    = paste0(gsub("_", " ", cluster_name), " | ", top_high$pathway[1]),
           subtitle = paste0("NES = ", round(top_high$NES[1], 2),
                             ", padj = ", round(top_high$padj[1], 3),
                             " | Enriched in SA High")) +
      theme_bw() +
      theme(plot.title    = element_text(size = 14, face = "bold"),
            plot.subtitle = element_text(size = 11),
            axis.title    = element_text(size = 12),
            axis.text     = element_text(size = 11))
    ggsave(paste0(cluster_dir, "Top_Pathway_SAHigh.pdf"), p_high, width = 8, height = 6)
  }
  
  # --- Top pathway enriched in SA Low ---
  top_low <- sig_pathways[sig_pathways$NES < 0, ]
  if (nrow(top_low) > 0) {
    top_low <- top_low[order(top_low$NES, decreasing = FALSE), ]
    p_low <- plotEnrichment(pathways_h[[top_low$pathway[1]]], gene_ranks) +
      labs(title    = paste0(gsub("_", " ", cluster_name), " | ", top_low$pathway[1]),
           subtitle = paste0("NES = ", round(top_low$NES[1], 2),
                             ", padj = ", round(top_low$padj[1], 3),
                             " | Enriched in SA Low")) +
      theme_bw() +
      theme(plot.title    = element_text(size = 14, face = "bold"),
            plot.subtitle = element_text(size = 11),
            axis.title    = element_text(size = 12),
            axis.text     = element_text(size = 11))
    ggsave(paste0(cluster_dir, "Top_Pathway_SALow.pdf"), p_low, width = 8, height = 6)
  }
  
  # --- Summary barplot of all significant pathways ---
  summary_df <- sig_pathways %>%
    mutate(
      direction     = ifelse(NES > 0, "Enriched in SA High", "Enriched in SA Low"),
      pathway_clean = gsub("HALLMARK_", "", pathway),
      pathway_clean = gsub("_", " ", pathway_clean)
    ) %>%
    arrange(NES)
  
  summary_df$pathway_clean <- factor(summary_df$pathway_clean,
                                      levels = summary_df$pathway_clean)
  
  p_summary <- ggplot(summary_df, aes(x = NES, y = pathway_clean, fill = direction)) +
    geom_bar(stat = "identity") +
    geom_vline(xintercept = 0, linetype = "dashed", colour = "black") +
    scale_fill_manual(values = c("Enriched in SA High" = "#e41a1c",
                                  "Enriched in SA Low"  = "royalblue")) +
    theme_bw() +
    theme(
      axis.text.y      = element_text(size = 12),
      axis.text.x      = element_text(size = 12),
      axis.title       = element_text(size = 14, face = "bold"),
      plot.title       = element_text(size = 16, face = "bold"),
      plot.subtitle    = element_text(size = 12),
      legend.text      = element_text(size = 12),
      legend.title     = element_blank(),
      panel.grid.minor = element_blank()
    ) +
    labs(title    = paste("Significant Hallmark Pathways:", gsub("_", " ", cluster_name)),
         subtitle = "SA High (>35) vs SA Low (<10) | padj < 0.05",
         x        = "Normalised Enrichment Score (NES)",
         y        = NULL)
  
  ggsave(filename = paste0(cluster_dir, "All_Pathways_Summary.pdf"),
         plot     = p_summary,
         width    = 10,
         height   = 0.4 * nrow(summary_df) + 3,
         dpi      = 300)
  
  message("Success: ", nrow(sig_pathways), " pathways saved for ", cluster_name)
}






col_meta <- data.frame(ColName = colnames(mat_sa)) %>%
  separate(ColName, 
           into = c("Cluster", "Patient", "SA_group"), 
           sep  = "_", 
           extra = "merge") %>%
  filter(SA_group %in% c("High", "Low"))

sample_counts <- col_meta %>%
  group_by(Cluster, SA_group) %>%
  summarise(n_samples = n(), .groups = "drop") %>%
  pivot_wider(names_from = SA_group, values_from = n_samples, values_fill = 0) %>%
  mutate(Total = High + Low)

kable(sample_counts, caption = "Pseudobulk samples per cluster by SA status")

write.csv(sample_counts,
          "~/Desktop/Research_Project/Scripts/DEGs/SA_HighvsLow/Pseudobulk_Sample_Counts.csv",
          row.names = FALSE)
```

## Step 10: LIANA+ Cell Communication

## Step 11: Genome-Scale Metabolic Model

```{r}
# Install if needed
# BiocManager::install("SeuratDisk")
library(SeuratDisk)

# Convert and save as h5ad
SaveH5Seurat(adata_qc, 
             filename = "~/Desktop/Research_Project/Scripts/Integration/adata_qc.h5Seurat",
             overwrite = TRUE)

Convert("~/Desktop/Research_Project/Scripts/Integration/adata_qc.h5Seurat", 
        dest = "h5ad",
        overwrite = TRUE)

# Verify what's in it before exporting
cat("Cell types present:\n")
print(table(adata_qc$clusters))

cat("\nSamples present:\n")
print(table(adata_qc$orig.ident))

cat("\nLesional breakdown:\n")
print(table(adata_qc$les))
```
